artist.profile_description: I am creating tunes with (mostly) biology themed lyrics, and some quirky songs of various topics. Feel free to comment.

songs.title: 300 Million to One (The Great Haploid Migration)
songs.tags: A fast-paced synth-pop or educational rap track at 120 BPM, anchored by pulsing synth bass, bright arpeggiators, and crisp electronic drums. Verses alternate percussive rap with punchy synth hooks, building into soaring choruses with layered harmonies and playful sample interludes.
songs.prompt:
(Intro: A steady, pulsing bassline representing a heartbeat. Synthesizer swells.)

(Verse 1: The Launch & The Gate) 
Three hundred million waiting at the start 
Ejected from the storage by a beating heart 
Wrapped in fibrinogen, a coagulated gel 
Protected from the acid of the vaginal cell. 
But wait twenty minutes, let the PSA activate 
Liquefy the clot, boys, we gotta navigate! 
Hit the cervix, looking for the estrogen sign 
Is the MUC-5-B aligned in a parallel line? 
If it’s Type G mucus, we’re stuck in the mesh 
But it’s Type E today—a liquid highway through the flesh! 
Swim through the lanes, leave the mutants behind 
We’re looking for a gamete of the female kind!

(Chorus) 
It’s a chemical handshake, a molecular dance 
Biology’s leaving nothing to chance. 
From the crypts to the tube, following the heat 
Till the haploid sets make the diploid complete. 
(Yeah, make it complete!)

(Verse 2: The Uterus & Capacitation) 
Now we’re in the uterus, floating in the dark 
But we’re chemically incompetent, missing the spark. 
We need capacitation or we’ll never get in 
Albumin comes down, strips the cholesterol from our skin! 
My membrane is fluid! 
The channels unlock! 
Calcium and Bicarbonate rush the cell block! 
Activate the sAC, pump the cAMP
Turn on Protein Kinase, give energy to me! 
My tail was steady, now it’s thrashing about 
Hyperactivation! 
I’m whipping it out!

(Bridge: The Navigation) (Tempo slows down, music becomes mysterious/spacious) 
We hit the Fallopian, the air is getting thin 
Thermotaxis guiding us—seek the heat on the skin. 
Wait... do you taste that? 
A gradient flow? 
Progesterone cloud from the Cumulus glow! 
Open the CatSper channels, let the calcium drive 
Steer the flagellum, keep the mission alive! 
There she is... 
The Oocyte. 
Massive and waiting. 
The end of the night.

(Verse 3: The Acrosome Reaction) 
Hit the Cumulus matrix, Hyaluronidase enzyme 
Digging through the cells, yeah we’re making good time. 
Touch the Zona Pellucida, that hard outer shell 
Bind to ZP3—now unleash the hell! 
Acrosome Reaction! 
Exocytosis bloom! 
Spilling out acrosin to clear up the room! 
Drill a hole in the glycoprotein wall 
Fuse with the membrane, standing tall!

(Outro) 
The membranes merge. 
The DNA flows. 
Cortical Reaction! 
The zinc spark glows! 
Hardens the shell so no others can enter 
Just me and the nucleus, dead in the center. 
23 meets 23. 
We are one. 
We are Zygote. 
The division’s begun.

(Fade out on the sound of rapid cell division)

songs.title: The Coffin Fly: The Navigator of the Grave
songs.tags: Somber acoustic guitar arpeggios set a dark folk atmosphere, with subtle fingerpicking and sparse, resonant percussion. Deep male vocals carry a melancholic, mysterious tone. Light strings and distant harmonium textures add depth, while dynamic swells intensify the haunting mood.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
In the shadows, small and gray
A humpbacked form, I scuttle away
Phoridae, my ancient line
Not one for flying, that's by design
I live my life in a jerky dance
Searching for a final chance
Not for me, but for my own
In the quiet earth, below the stone

[Chorus]
I'm the coffin fly, a navigator of the grave ⚰️
I find the peace that you all save
A chemical beacon, a whisper through the clay
I find the life you put away
Down in the dark, where secrets sleep
A promise that my young will keep

[Verse 2]
You think it's over, locked in deep
But sulfur secrets start to seep
Dimethyl trisulfide, a scent so clear
It tells me that a feast is near
I dig through feet of dirt and dread
Answering the call of the dead
In low-oxygen, I lay my brood
On their silent, waiting food

[Chorus]
'Cause I'm the coffin fly, a navigator of the grave ⚰️
I find the peace that you all save
A chemical beacon, a whisper through the clay
I find the life you put away
Down in the dark, where secrets sleep
A promise that my young will keep

[Bridge]
But don't you think this skill's for you?
My ghoulish talent isn't new
Before your graves, before your time
I've solved this subterranean crime
A badger's burrow, a fox's tomb
Dispelled the deep and ancient gloom
Your burials are just a stage
For a skill that's from a different age ⏳

[Chorus]
Yes, I'm the coffin fly, a navigator of the grave ⚰️
I find the peace that you all save
A chemical beacon, a whisper through the clay
I find the life you put away
Down in the dark, where secrets sleep
A promise that my young will keep

[Outro]
A silent witness, dark and deep...
The cycle turns... a secret I will keep...
(Acoustic guitar fades out)

songs.caption: This song is about the Coffin Fly (Conicera tibialis, a species of Phoridae). Unlike many surface flies, the female is uniquely adapted to navigate deep into the soil (over six feet) to find buried remains.This makes it a star in forensic entomology, as it is one of the few species that can detect the chemical signs of decomposition, such as dimethyl trisulfide, and colonize a corpse even after long-term burial. #CoffinFly #ConiceraTibialis #ForensicScience #Phoridae

songs.title: The Third Domain (Woese's Quest)
songs.tags: Anthemic 80s heartland rock pulse drives with energetic drums, crisp electric guitars, and a pulsing bass line. The verses build over warm grand piano and organ layers, exploding into choruses that feature a soaring saxophone melody. Earnest raspy male vocals cut through, backed by rich harmonies.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
(Driving drum beat and a memorable electric guitar riff kick in)
(Yeah!)

[Verse 1]
In a world of tiny life, a mystery profound
Microbes hidden from our sight, on that forgotten ground
For years we grouped them, black and white, in kingdoms known to all
But one man saw a different light, he heard a different call
Carl Woese, with a steady hand, in his Urbana lab
Vowed he would understand, the story they all had

[Chorus]
Oh, Woese, Carl Woese, with his ribosomal quest!
He found a hidden kingdom, puttin' old beliefs to the test!
No longer just the bacteria, a single, ancient line!
Archaea emerged, a revolutionary design!
The three domains of life, a tree so grand and wide!
Carl Woese, you opened up the whole world inside!

[Verse 2]
Through rRNA, he traced the code, a molecular clue
Unraveling the ancient road, for creatures old and new
He saw the difference, clear and stark, where the branches had diverged
A fundamental, fiery spark, where all of life emerged
These Archaea, living in extremes, in vents and salty lakes
They were shattering all the old dreams, for goodness gracious sakes!

[Chorus]
Oh, Woese, Carl Woese, with his ribosomal quest!
He found a hidden kingdom, puttin' old beliefs to the test!
No longer just the bacteria, a single, ancient line!
Archaea emerged, a revolutionary design!
The three domains of life, a tree so grand and wide!
Carl Woese, you opened up the whole world inside!

[Bridge]
He challenged dogma, stood his ground, with evidence so strong!
A deeper truth he finally found, where it was hiding all along!
The tree of life, forever changed, by what his data showed!
A whole new map he rearranged, on that genetic road!

[Guitar Solo]
(Energetic and melodic electric guitar solo, with the band driving hard behind it)

[Outro]
Yeah! The Third Domain!
(He changed the game!)
Archaea!
(We'll shout his name!)
Carl Woese!
(Guitar riff fades out with a final, crashing cymbal)

songs.caption: This song celebrates Carl Woese, the scientist who revolutionized the Tree of Life in the 1970s. By analyzing ribosomal RNA (rRNA), he proved that life consisted of three primary domains (not just two kingdoms): Bacteria, Eukarya, and the newly discovered Archaea. Archaea are unique microbes, often found in extreme environments, that represent an ancient, separate evolutionary line. #CarlWoese #Archaea #ThreeDomains #MolecularBiology #rRNA

songs.title: Sexton Beetle song
songs.tags: This dark folk ballad in gothic americana style unfolds with fingerpicked acoustic guitar and deep, resonant upright bass creating a steady, ominous pulse. Atmospheric layers from a haunting cello weave between verses. Baritone vocals deliver the story solemnly, with sparse hand percussion or foot-tapping subtly reinforcing the rhythm. Each section quietly builds with added texture—bowed cello harmonics, gentle vocal harmonies, perhaps faint distant reverb—to enhance the immersive, rhythmic darkness.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
(Acoustic guitar intro, slow and moody)

[Verse 1]
In the moss and in the loam, where the fallen chestnuts lie
There's a whisper on the breeze, a scent of when the living die
Not of sorrow, not of dread, but a promise in the air
For the beetle, black and orange, who will answer to the prayer.

[Verse 2]
There he finds a fallen shrew, still and silent on the ground
Soon a female finds him there, and the rivals gather 'round
After clash of shell and jaw, the worthy pair has won the prize
And they vow to work together underneath the watching skies.

[Chorus]
Oh, they call me Sexton Beetle, nature's undertaker
From the shadow of the reaper, I'm a loving cradle-maker
I will dig a silent tomb, where the cycle can restart
Turning death into a future with all my beating heart.

[Verse 3]
Side-by-side they dig and shove, 'til the corpse is tucked below
Strip the fur and roll the flesh, so their children safe can grow
Paint it with a secret spit, to preserve it from the rot
A perfect, lasting pantry, in their dark and hallowed spot.

[Verse 4]
And the mites who rode his back, a phoretic, loyal band
Now disembark with purpose, to give a helping hand
They will hunt the eggs of flies, destroy the maggot host
Guarding the beetle's treasure, the thing they value most.

[Chorus]
Oh, they call me Sexton Beetle, nature's undertaker
From the shadow of the reaper, I'm a loving cradle-maker
I will dig a silent tomb, where the cycle can restart
Turning death into a future with all my beating heart.

[Bridge]
When the larvae start to hatch, they beg with hungry cries
And their mother gives them food, regurgitated for their size
If the brood is far too large, she'll make a brutal, gentle choice
So the strong will grow up stronger, and have their own true voice.

[Guitar Solo]
(Solemn acoustic guitar solo with cello accompaniment)

[Outro Chorus]
Yes, they call me Sexton Beetle, nature's undertaker
From the shadow of the reaper, I'm a loving cradle-maker
Turning endings to beginnings, I have always played my part...
Turning death into a future, with all my beating heart.

[Outro]
(Guitar and cello fade out)

songs.caption: This song celebrates the Sexton Beetle (Nicrophorus), nature's undertaker. A male and female pair bury a small carcass, creating a nursery underground. They preserve the "meat" with a special secretion and the female feeds the hatched larvae directly. This is a rare example of intense biparental care among insects, ensuring their offspring thrive on the protected food resource. #SextonBeetle #Nicrophorus #ParentalCare #Decomposition

songs.title: I am the very model of a modern molecular biologist V1
songs.tags: A fast allegro tempo kicks off with a playful orchestra—bright strings, lively winds, and nimble percussion. Crisp staccato notes bounce beneath an agile male baritone, whose rapid, theatrical delivery dances atop the arrangement. Dynamic swells and comic flourishes keep the energy buoyant.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
I am the very model of a modern molecular biologist,
I've information genomic, proteomic, and metabolicalist;
I know the central dogma and the pathways kinase-activated,
From signal transduction cascades to proteins polyadenylated.
I'm very well acquainted, too, with matters of the CRISPR-Cas,
I design my guide RNAs to edit genes without a fuss;
For quantifying mRNA I run my qPCRs with glee,
And check the product size on gels with ethidium bromide, you see.

[Chorus]
I'm very good at Western Blots to find a protein's proper place,
And I can name the cell lines from a human to a mouse's race.
In short, in matters genomic, proteomic, and metabolicalist,
I am the very model of a modern molecular biologist.

[Verse 2]
I can recite the history of Watson, Crick, and other docs,
I troubleshoot my failed results and handle reagent shocks,
I spend my evenings writing grants with figures and appendices,
And argue with Reviewer #2 over what their weirdest addend is.
Then I can run a flow machine with twelve fluorescent antibodies,
And tell a plasmid from a phage or from a lentiviral body;
In conics I can floor peculiarities parabolous,
I analyze my sequencing data 'til the server crashes on us.

[Chorus]
I'm very good at Western Blots to find a protein's proper place,
And I can name the cell lines from a human to a mouse's race.
In short, in matters genomic, proteomic, and metabolicalist,
I am the very model of a modern molecular biologist.

[Bridge]
In fact, when I can visualize a kinase phosphorylation,
And what is meant by "SUMOylation" or "ubiquitination,"
When I know more of ecosystems than what's on my petri plate,
And can describe a food web or a creature's true life history trait;
When I have learned what progress has been made in organismal science,
And can identify a bird without my phone's specific guidance...

[Outro]
In short, when I've a smattering of knowledge in classical zoology,
You'll say a better scientist has never graced a department of Molecular Biology!

songs.caption: Inspired  by  "Modern Major-General," this track celebrates the exhaustive knowledge required in modern biology! It showcases expertise in the Central Dogma (DNA → RNA → Protein), techniques like CRISPR-Cas gene editing, qPCR for quantifying RNA, and Western Blots for protein analysis. From signal transduction cascades to battling Reviewer #2, this is a humorous tribute to the highly specialized—and yet surprisingly broad—field of Molecular Biology. #MolecularBiology #CRISPR #ScienceMusic #

songs.title: Evidence (Remix)
songs.tags: lyrical pop
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Fossil records in the stone
Telling stories of ages gone
Transitional forms laid to rest
Nature's evolutionary test
Tiktaalik and Archaeopteryx
Ancient puzzles science fixed

[Chorus]
It's all in the evidence, written in time
Millions of years, not seven days divine
DNA, fossils, and geology agree
Evolution shaped you and me

[Verse 2]
Vestigial parts with no design
Whale legs and blind eyes define
The recurrent laryngeal nerve
Takes the long path without reserve
Our DNA tells ancient tales
98 percent shared with chimpanzees

[Chorus]
It's all in the evidence, written in time
Millions of years, not seven days divine
DNA, fossils, and geology agree
Evolution shaped you and me

[Bridge]
Radiometric dating doesn't lie
Light from stars that's billions of years in the sky
Pangaea's drift across the deep
Continental plates that never sleep
Biogeography's distribution plans
Life adapting as best it can

[Verse 3]
In labs we watch it unfold
Bacteria evolve, as we're told
Antibiotic resistance grows
Natural selection as it goes
Ring species around mountains high
Evolution we can't deny

[Final Chorus]
It's all in the evidence, written in time
Billions of years, not seven days divine
Geology, biology, astronomy agree
Evolution shaped you and me
Evolution shaped reality

[Outro]
Follow where the evidence leads
Not ancient texts or supernatural creeds
Science adjusts as new facts appear
The natural world becomes more clear

songs.caption: This song champions the vast scientific evidence supporting evolution. It synthesizes findings from multiple disciplines, citing fossils (like Tiktaalik), vestigial structures (like whale leg bones), DNA comparisons (with chimpanzees), radiometric dating, and biogeography. It highlights natural selection in action, such as antibiotic resistance, affirming that evolution is a continuous process supported by chemistry and geology across billions of years. #Evolution #Science #EvidenceBased

songs.title: The Paradox of Porn
songs.tags:
Comedy, Quirky, Lighthearted,
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE INFO
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh,
Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh,

[Verse 1]
When it comes to tech, there's a funny scene,
A hidden force, often unseen,
From VHS to the internet craze,
It’s the world of porn, leading the ways.

[Chorus]
Porn and tech, an unlikely pair,
In every new gadget, it's always there,
Streaming or VR, it’s hard to ignore,
Porn's on the forefront, knocking at the door.

[Verse 2]
Remember the days of dial-up tones,
Slow connections, moans and groans,
Then came broadband, fast and clear,
Porn was right there, pioneers.

[Chorus]
Porn and tech, an unlikely pair,
In every new gadget, it's always there,
Streaming or VR, it’s hard to ignore,
Porn's on the forefront, knocking at the door.

[Bridge]
From Blu-ray discs to high-speed fiber,
Every tech advance, they’re the early subscriber,
In the virtual world, with goggles on,
Porn's pushing boundaries, leading us along.

[Verse 3]
Humanoid robots, walking and talking,
In the world of tech, they're the ones flocking,
Artificial charm, with a risqué twist,
Porn’s in the lead, they can’t resist.

[Chorus]
Porn and tech, an unlikely pair,
In every new gadget, it's always there,
Streaming or VR, it’s hard to ignore,
Porn's on the forefront, knocking at the door.

[Outro]
Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh,
Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh,
In the world of tech, here's the truth to see,
Porn's always ahead, in the industry.

songs.caption: This song points out a long-observed phenomenon: the adult entertainment industry often acts as a major early adopter and key driver of new consumer technology. From pushing the adoption of VHS over Betamax and demanding early high-speed broadband, to leading the charge into technologies like Virtual Reality (VR) and advanced streaming formats, this industry frequently validates and pushes the commercial limits of new tech. #PornAndTech #Innovation #EarlyAdopter #TechCommentary

songs.title: Hammerhead Ribozyme Rock
songs.tags: A hard rock anthem, tempo 125 BPM
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Fifty nucleotides long
Metal ions make me strong
Three helical stems I hold
Watch my catalysis unfold

[Chorus]
I'm the Hammerhead, watch me cleave
Breaking bonds is what I achieve
C-U-G-A-N-G-A at my core
That's what I'm fighting for!

[Verse 2]
Magnesium's my heavy metal friend
Helps me cut right at the end
Hydroxyl attack, precise and clean
Most ancient enzyme ever seen

[Bridge]
Phospho-di-ester bonds - BREAK!
Cyclic phosphates - MAKE!
Self-cleaving all night long
RNA world, where I belong

[Chorus]
I'm the Hammerhead, watch me cleave
Breaking bonds is what I achieve
C-U-G-A-N-G-A at my core
That's what I'm fighting for!

[Verse 3]
From viroids to satellites
Conserved through evolution's nights
Minimal structure, maximum effect
RNA catalyst, command respect

[Final Chorus]
I'm the Hammerhead, watch me cleave
Breaking bonds is what I achieve
C-U-G-A-N-G-A at my core
That's what I'm made for!

[Outro]
(Spoken with heavy guitar backing)
Clay-made, self-spliced, ribozyme might
Catalytic RNA tonight!

songs.caption: The Hammerhead Ribozyme: A tiny, self-cleaving RNA molecule only about 50 nucleotides long. It acts as an enzyme—a catalyst—by using metal ions (like Magnesium) to precisely break (cleave) its own phosphodiester bonds at the C-U-G-A-N-G-A core. A key relic of the ancient RNA world.

songs.title: If I was God ...(I'd redesign)
songs.tags: Progressive Rock
songs.prompt:
If I Was God
Verse 1:
Looking through my microscope today
At parasites that make their hosts decay
Wasps that lay their eggs in living prey
Nature's cruelty on display
Chorus:
If I was God, I'd redesign
These faulty paths of grand design
Fix the errors, ease the pain
Make these systems work again
Verse 2:
Why give whales vestigial legs to bear?
Or blind cave fish with eyes that cannot stare?
Recurrent laryngeal nerve's detour
A circuit that makes no sense for sure
(Chorus)
Bridge:
Guinea worms beneath the skin
Toxoplasma deep within
Inefficient DNA
Copying errors day by day
Verse 3:
Cancer cells that multiply with rage
Immune systems that turn with age
Birth canals too narrow for our heads
These designs fill me with dread
(Chorus)
Outro:
If I was God, I'd start anew
Design it better, clean and true
But I'm just here to observe and say
Evolution shaped us all this way
songs.full_lyrics:
If I Was God
Verse 1:
Looking through my microscope today
At parasites that make their hosts decay
Wasps that lay their eggs in living prey
Nature's cruelty on display
Chorus:
If I was God, I'd redesign
These faulty paths of grand design
Fix the errors, ease the pain
Make these systems work again
Verse 2:
Why give whales vestigial legs to bear?
Or blind cave fish with eyes that cannot stare?
Recurrent laryngeal nerve's detour
A circuit that makes no sense for sure
(Chorus)
Bridge:
Guinea worms beneath the skin
Toxoplasma deep within
Inefficient DNA
Copying errors day by day
Verse 3:
Cancer cells that multiply with rage
Immune systems that turn with age
Birth canals too narrow for our heads
These designs fill me with dread
(Chorus)
Outro:
If I was God, I'd start anew
Design it better, clean and true
But I'm just here to observe and say
Evolution shaped us all this way
songs.caption: This song critiques "intelligent design" by highlighting biological "flaws" as evidence of evolution. It cites examples like cruel parasitism (wasps, Guinea worms), vestigial structures (whale legs, blind cave fish eyes), inefficient anatomical paths (recurrent laryngeal nerve), and even cancer. The lyrics conclude these "errors" reflect a system shaped by natural selection and genetic mutation, not perfect creation. #Evolution #BadDesign #NaturalSelection #Biology

songs.title: First Replicator
songs.tags: Minimalist techno starting at 100 BPM, gradually layering complexity
songs.prompt:
R-N-A strand in the chemical night
Copy, paste, divide and fight
No cell wall, no membrane bound
Just information going round and round

Catalyze (catalyze)
Memorize (memorize)
Self-replicate
Mutations wait

I am the code that learned to grow
Template running fast and slow
Making copies, some survive
First algorithm coming alive

Ribozymes dancing in the clay
Each mistake showing the way
Natural selection takes its hold
As genetic stories start to unfold


Error, copy, change, repeat
Evolution's first heartbeat
Building blocks of what we'll be
Started with simplicity

A-U-G-C-A-U-G-C
Copy. Change. Survive.
Copy. Change. Alive.

Ribozymes dancing in the clay
Each mistake showing the way
Natural selection takes its hold
As genetic stories start to unfold

Error, copy, change, repeat
Evolution's first heartbeat
Building blocks of what we'll be
Started with simplicity

A-U-G-C-A-U-G-C
Copy. Change. Survive.
Copy. Change. Alive.

A-U-G-C-A-U-G-C
Copy. Change. Survive.
Copy. Change. Alive.

songs.caption: This song rocks out about the RNA World Hypothesis, a theory suggesting that early life used RNA for both storing genetic information and catalyzing chemical reactions (a function performed by ribozymes). This single-molecule system allowed for self-replication and rapid mutation/error, creating the conditions necessary for natural selection and evolution to begin before the invention of DNA or complex proteins. #RNAWorld #OriginOfLife #Ribozymes #Evolution

songs.title: Protein Synthesis Rap (Remastered)
songs.tags: Boom Bap Rap
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1] [Rap Verse]
Yo, it starts in the nucleus, DNA unwind,
Transcription begins, gotta read the line.
RNA polymerase, doin' its thing,
Makin' mRNA, let the message ring.

Nucleotides matchin', bases align,
Coppin' the code, in a perfect design.
From A to U and C to G,
mRNA's the key, now it's time to see.

[Chorus] [Catchy Hook]
Protein synthesis, it's the way to go,
From the DNA to the proteins that we know.
Ribosomes read it, amino acids grow,
In the cell's factory, watch it all flow.

[Verse 2] [Rap Verse]
mRNA leaves, through the nuclear gate,
Headin' to the cytoplasm, it won't wait.
Ribosomes bind, translation starts,
Reading every codon, playin' its part.

tRNA's bringin' amino acids in line,
Matchin' every codon, one at a time.
Peptide bonds form, chain's gettin' long,
Buildin' up the protein, makin' it strong.

[Chorus] [Catchy Hook]
Protein synthesis, it's the way to go,
From the DNA to the proteins that we know.
Ribosomes read it, amino acids grow,
In the cell's factory, watch it all flow.

[Bridge]
Elongation's rollin', chain keeps buildin',
Foldin' into shapes, structure fulfillin'.
Post-translational mods, give it some flair,
Now it's a protein, ready to repair.



[Outro] [Fade to End]
From genes to proteins, the cell's own hustle,
Protein synthesis, life's flexing muscle.

songs.title: Urea Blues
songs.tags: Jazz, blues, soul
songs.prompt:
Verse 1: 
In the liver’s midnight glow
Ammonia starts to flow
From protein’s ancient ties
A tale of nitrogen highs.

Chorus: 
Urea cycle, 
smooth as a jazz tune
In the body's quiet room.
Turning waste to a sweet melody, Setting our cells free.

Verse 2: 
Ornithine’s dance, 
citrulline’s chance, 
In a cycle’s timeless trance. 
With arginine’s grace, 
it finds its place, 
Detoxing with elegant pace.

Bridge: 
Oh, the enzymes know their cues, Arginase, how it soothes. 
From ammonia's restless wail, 
To urea’s seamless trail.

Chorus: 
Urea cycle, smooth as a jazz tune, 
In the body's quiet room. 
Turning waste to a sweet melody, Setting our cells free.

Verse 3: 
In the bloodstream's flow, it goes, 
To the kidneys, it bestows. 
With every beat, it’s a treat, 
Keeping our balance neat.

Chorus: 
Urea cycle, smooth as a jazz tune, 
In the body's quiet room. 
Turning waste to a sweet melody, Setting our cells free.

Outro: 
So here's to the cycle, cool and fine, 
A bluesy beat in the body's line. 
With every step, it’s clear to see, Biology’s harmony.

songs.title: Journey of Change
songs.tags: Progressive Rock
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
(Instrumental - sweeping guitar riffs, steady drumbeat)

[Verse 1]
In the cradle of the ancient seas,
Life began its endless quest,
From the depths to the canopies,
Nature's grand and ultimate test.

[Pre-Chorus]
Through the ages, time unfolds,
A story of strength and endless molds.

[Chorus]
Journey of change, we evolve and rise,
From the dawn of life to the endless skies,
Every step, a tale untold,
In the dance of time, we find our hold.

[Verse 2]
From the fish to the bird in flight,
Transformation in every sight,
Land to air, and sea to land,
Crafted by nature's gentle hand.

[Pre-Chorus]
From the simple to the complex,
A symphony of life's reflex.

[Chorus]
Journey of change, we evolve and rise,
From the dawn of life to the endless skies,
Every step, a tale untold,
In the dance of time, we find our hold.

[Bridge]
Echoes of the past, in our genes reside,
A legacy of life, in us they hide,
Forward we march, with knowledge keen,
In this grand tapestry, we are seen.

[Chorus]
Journey of change, we evolve and rise,
From the dawn of life to the endless skies,
Every step, a tale untold,
In the dance of time, we find our hold.

[Outro]
(Instrumental - crescendo, fading out)

songs.title: Spermatogenesis V2
songs.tags: female singer, piano, energetic, soul
songs.prompt:
[Piano intro]
[Verse 1]
In the seminiferous tubules, it all begins, (oh yeah)
Germ cells dividing, where the life spins.
Mitosis and meiosis, stages they go through,
From spermatogonia to something new.

[Pre-Chorus]
Chromosomes shuffle, genes recombine,
Creating gametes, one of a kind.

[Chorus] [Funky Hook]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

[Verse 2]
Primary spermatocytes take the stage, (Oh yeah)
Meiotic divisions, the genetic gauge.
Secondary spermatocytes split once more,
Turning into spermatids, ready to explore.

[Pre-Chorus]
Form and function, they start to align,
Tail and head developing, by design.

[Chorus] [Funky Hook]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

songs.title: Decomposition's Lament
songs.tags:
Ambient Rock, Progressive, EducatAtmospheric, Mixed Vocals, Clear Vocal, Hifi
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE INFO
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Ambient Rock, Progressive Rock]
[SOUNDS LIKE: Pink Floyd, Porcupine Tree, Anathema]
[STYLE: Atmospheric, Melodic, Experimental, Narrative]
[MOOD: Reflective, Contemplative, Mysterious, Somber]
[VOCALS: Mixed (Male and Female), Harmonious, Layered]
[ARRANGEMENT: Moderate tempo, Ethereal textures, Dynamic builds]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Electric guitar, Synthesizers, Bass, Drums, Piano, Ambient pads]
[TEMPO: Moderate, 90-110 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Rich soundscapes, Warm tones, Layered textures, High fidelity, Produced by Steven Wilson]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Chorus, Verse, Chorus, Bridge, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: Gentle and dynamic, Gradual builds and releases, Smooth transitions]
[EMOTIONS: Reflection, Curiosity, Mystery, Tranquility]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Short Instrumental Intro]

[Verse 1 - Male]
Silent beginning, cells break down within
Autolysis starts, where life once had been
Enzymes released, breaking bonds so deep
Molecules unravel in this final sleep

[Chorus - Female]
Rigor mortis, stiffness claims the form
Muscles freeze in death’s quiet storm
Algor mortis, body cools its heat
Nature's clock ticks, in its rhythmic beat

[Instrumental Interlude]

[Verse 2 - Female]
Decomposition takes its toll, bacteria thrive
Breaking down the body's soul, in a dance, alive
Odor’s veil, gases rise, a spectral display
Insects come, drawn to the decay

[Chorus - Male]
Rigor mortis, stiffness claims the form
Muscles freeze in death’s quiet storm
Algor mortis, body cools its heat

[Chorus] [Funky Hook]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.

Meiotic divisions, the genetic gauge.
Secondary spermatocytes split once more,
Turning into spermatids, ready to explore.

Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,

Creating life, in this funky slice.

Ready for action, to meet the ovum too.

[Chorus] [Big Finish]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

[Outro] [Fade to End]

The wonder of life, through spermatogenesis.

songs.title: Neuroscience of Love
songs.tags: A synth-pop and neo-soul fusion opens with lush analog synth pads and fluid electric piano, supported by punchy drum machines and syncopated bass. Verses groove with sparse guitar chops and airy vocal harmonies, while choruses bloom with layered synth leads and soulful brass stabs.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
There's a spark that's electric, a chemical light.

[Verse 1]
In the brain, it's a dance, dopamine in play,
With a rush and a glance, it can sweep us away.
Serotonin sings a song of serene,
While oxytocin binds us, unseen.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.

[Chorus]
Neuro-science of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.

[Verse 2]
In the limbic’s embrace, emotions intertwine,
Every kiss, every trace, in patterns we define.
With each memory stored, and each moment we share,
Our neurons explore, and love’s everywhere.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.



[Chorus]
Neuro-science of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.

[Bridge]
Chemical reactions, they pave the way,
In the maze of the mind, where our feelings stay.
With endorphins high, and the pathways clear,
In the neuro-science of love, we conquer fear.


[Outro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
Love's a journey of the brain, in the chemical light.

songs.caption: his song explores the neurochemical basis of love and attachment. It highlights key brain circuits and neurotransmitters responsible for human connection: Dopamine for pleasure and reward, Serotonin for mood and well-being, and Oxytocin (the "bonding hormone") for deep attachment. The track emphasizes that love is not just a feeling, but a result of synchronized synapses firing across the brain, especially in the limbic system (emotion center). #Neuroscience #Dopamine #Oxytocin #BrainChemistry

DNA

songs.caption: This song celebrates DNA as the fundamental genetic blueprint of life. It highlights how traits are passed from parent to child (inheritance) and how the accumulation of small changes, known as mutations, provides the raw material necessary for evolution. DNA's structure and code (the lineage flowed) hold the history and potential of all life, connecting us to our ancestors and defining our unique characteristics. #DNA #Genetics #Evolution #Inheritance

songs.title: The Dance of the Daughter Cells (Mitosis)
songs.tags: A lively bossa nova groove underpins the verses with crisp nylon guitar, syncopated percussion, and jazzy syncopation. The chorus introduces bright pop synths, pulsing electronic bass, and airy pads, while layered vocal harmonies contrast against the warm, organic rhythm section for a lush, infectious texture.
songs.prompt:
[Intro] 
[Synthesizer pulsing, steady beat build-up]
[Spoken word, deep voice] 
Focus. Division. Differentiation. Let's go.

[Verse 1] 
[Rhythmic, steady flow]
It starts in the quiet, the G1 phase 
Growing and metabolic pathways ablaze 
Then we hit the S-phase, synthesis starts 
Replicating the code, the most vital of parts 
Helicase unzips, polymerase binds 
Doubling the genome, no base left behind 
We check it in G2, fixing the flaws 
Before we submit to physical laws

[Pre-Chorus] [Building tension] 
Two sister cromatids held at the waist 
By a sentromere knot, not a moment to waste!

[Chorus]
 [Explosive, catchy, anthem style] 
Mitosis, mitosis, the cycle divides 
Where one becomes two, and the genome resides Identical copies, a perfect design 
Preserving the sequence, the unbroken line
 From one generation passed down to the next 
The biological code, the cellular text!

[Verse 2] [Fast flow, articulate] 
Now enter Prophase, the chromatin winds 
Condensing to chromosomes, the structure binds 
The nucleolus fades, it disappears 
The sentrosomes migrate, calming the fears 
They spin out the microtubules so wide 
While the nuclear envelope breaks down inside 
Kinetocores grab onto spindles so tight 
Prepare for the movement, prepare for the fight!

[Verse 3] [Driving beat continues] 
It’s Metaphase now, tension is high 
Chromosomes line up, eye to eye 
At the metaphase plate, the equator zone 
Waiting for signals from the unknown (Stop) Snap! 
[Resume Beat] 
The checkpoint is passed! 
Anaphase starts, moving so fast 
The cohesin breaks, the sisters split 
Pulled to the poles bit by bit!

[Verse 4] 
We reach Telophase, the journey is done 
Two nuclei form where there was just one 
The chromosomes relax, uncoil the thread 
The spindles dissolve, the tension is shed 
But we aren't quite finished, we need to divide 
Cytokinesis pinches the side 
A cleavage furrow of actin rings 
Two daughter cells, identical things!

[Bridge] [Tempo change, Half-time beat, Heavy Bass]
[Focus on diction] 
But wait! How does a skin cell know not to be a neuron? They share the same script, but the mask they put on 
Is crafted by epigenetic control 
Defining the function, defining the soul 
DNA methylation adds a tag to the base 
Silencing genes, shutting them out of the race!

[Guitar Solo] [Melodic and high energy]

[Bridge 2] [Staccato, punchy vocals]
Tightly packed heterochromatin sleeps 
While euchromatin opens and secrets it keeps 
Histone acetylation loosens the grip 
Letting transcription factors take a trip 
One becomes liver, one becomes bone 
Though the library's shared, the reading is their own!

[Chorus] [Maximum Energy] 
Mitosis, mitosis, the cycle divides 
Where one becomes two, and the gee-nome resides 
But Differentiation decides what we see 
Through the chemical markers of identity 
From stem cells to tissues, complex and grand 
Orchestrated strictly by the cellular hand!

[Outro] [Fading out with echoing vocals] 
Methyl on... (Acetyl off) 
Condense... (Divide) 
Express... The cycle continues. (The cycle continues)

songs.title: The Silent Thief's Embrace
songs.tags: At 85 BPM and in a minor key, this haunting indie folk song fuses cinematic orchestral layers—deep strings, sparse piano, and ambient drones. Dual male/female vocals intertwine in a tender duet, underscored by subtle acoustic guitar, distant percussion, and plaintive woodwinds.
songs.prompt:
[Introduction] [Acoustic guitar plucking, slow and ethereal] [Atmospheric wind sounds]

[Verse 1] 
In the quiet of the twilight, where the forest shadows creep 
A tiny winged traveler wakes while the village is asleep 
She seeks a drop of lifeblood, a silent, stinging thief 
But leaves behind a burden that will only bring us grief 
The promastigote is waiting, with a flagellum like a whip 
Sailing through the vessel in a microscopic ship 
It meets the macrophage, the guardian of the bone 
And finds a hollow sanctuary to call its very own

[Pre-Chorus] 
[Strings swell slowly] 
The whip is shed, the shape is round In the silence, no one hears a sound

[Chorus] [Full cinematic orchestration] 
Oh, Leishmania, the thief within the cell 
Transforming in the darkness where the hidden spirits dwell 
From the desert to the jungle, a story etched in scars 
A battle fought in silence underneath the lonely stars 
You steal the light of healing, you hide from every eye 
While the body asks the question: "Must a part of me now die?"

[Verse 2] 
[Music strips back to piano and cello] 
Inside the phagolysosome, the defense is left forlorn 
You thrive inside the acid, an amastigote is born 
You multiply with grace, until the cell is weary 
The microscope reveals a truth that's cold and dreary 
It bursts in silent sorrow, and a thousand more take flight 
To find another fortress in the middle of the night 
Is it Cutaneous, a mark upon the skin? 
Or Visceral, the Black Fever burning deep within?

[Bridge] [Building tension, rhythmic drumming]
The spleen begins to heavy, the liver starts to swell 
The marrow loses rhythm, like a cracked and hollow bell 
The sandfly takes her portion, and the cycle starts anew 
Taking back the parasites to pass them on to you 
Scientific, cold, and calculated—nature’s ancient design 
But for the one who suffers, it’s a long and broken line!

[Guitar Solo - Melancholic and Distorted]

[Chorus] [Maximum intensity] 
Oh, Leishmania, the thief within the cell 
Transforming in the darkness where the hidden spirits dwell 
From the desert to the jungle, a story etched in scars 
A battle fought in silence underneath the lonely stars!

[Outro] [Music fades to a single acoustic guitar] 
The Phlebotomus is flying... 
The Lutzomyia is near... 
Rest now, weary traveler... 
Let the fever... fade... [Fade to silence]

[End]
songs.full_lyrics:
[Introduction] [Acoustic guitar plucking, slow and ethereal] [Atmospheric wind sounds]

[Verse 1] 
In the quiet of the twilight, where the forest shadows creep 
A tiny winged traveler wakes while the village is asleep 
She seeks a drop of lifeblood, a silent, stinging thief 
But leaves behind a burden that will only bring us grief 
The promastigote is waiting, with a flagellum like a whip 
Sailing through the vessel in a microscopic ship 
It meets the macrophage, the guardian of the bone 
And finds a hollow sanctuary to call its very own

[Pre-Chorus] 
[Strings swell slowly] 
The whip is shed, the shape is round In the silence, no one hears a sound

[Chorus] [Full cinematic orchestration] 
Oh, Leishmania, the thief within the cell 
Transforming in the darkness where the hidden spirits dwell 
From the desert to the jungle, a story etched in scars 
A battle fought in silence underneath the lonely stars 
You steal the light of healing, you hide from every eye 
While the body asks the question: "Must a part of me now die?"

[Verse 2] 
[Music strips back to piano and cello] 
Inside the phagolysosome, the defense is left forlorn 
You thrive inside the acid, an amastigote is born 
You multiply with grace, until the cell is weary 
The microscope reveals a truth that's cold and dreary 
It bursts in silent sorrow, and a thousand more take flight 
To find another fortress in the middle of the night 
Is it Cutaneous, a mark upon the skin? 
Or Visceral, the Black Fever burning deep within?

[Bridge] [Building tension, rhythmic drumming]
The spleen begins to heavy, the liver starts to swell 
The marrow loses rhythm, like a cracked and hollow bell 
The sandfly takes her portion, and the cycle starts anew 
Taking back the parasites to pass them on to you 
Scientific, cold, and calculated—nature’s ancient design 
But for the one who suffers, it’s a long and broken line!

[Guitar Solo - Melancholic and Distorted]

[Chorus] [Maximum intensity] 
Oh, Leishmania, the thief within the cell 
Transforming in the darkness where the hidden spirits dwell 
From the desert to the jungle, a story etched in scars 
A battle fought in silence underneath the lonely stars!

[Outro] [Music fades to a single acoustic guitar] 
The Phlebotomus is flying... 
The Lutzomyia is near... 
Rest now, weary traveler... 
Let the fever... fade... [Fade to silence]

[End]

songs.title: The Endless (Sandman)

[Chorus]

Destiny's book, our fates in rune.

[Chorus]
Dream and Death, Desire's call,
Despair and Delirium, they bind us all,
Destiny's path, Destruction's fall,

songs.title: Dance of Desire
songs.tags: Folk, melodic, catchy, melodic interlude, female singer
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1] [Folk] [Melodic]
In the wild where flowers bloom,
A dance begins under the moon.
Feathers bright, colors bold,
Stories of attraction, centuries old.

[Chorus] [Folk] [Catchy Hook]
It’s the dance of desire, nature’s sweet call,
From the tiniest insect to creatures tall.
Selection's song, where beauty prevails,
In the dance of desire, love never fails.

[Verse 2] [Folk] [Melodic]
The peacock spreads his vibrant plume,
To win a mate in morning's gloom.
Stag with antlers, mighty and grand,
Battles for love across the land.

[Chorus] [Folk] [Catchy Hook]
It’s the dance of desire, nature’s sweet call,
From the tiniest insect to creatures tall.
Selection's song, where beauty prevails,
In the dance of desire, love never fails.

[Bridge] [Folk] [Melodic Interlude]
Songs of whales in the deep blue sea,
Birds in the sky with a melody sweet.

[Chorus] [Folk] [Catchy Hook]
It’s the dance of desire, nature’s sweet call,
From the tiniest insect to creatures tall.
Selection's song, where beauty prevails,
In the dance of desire, love never fails.

[Outro] [Folk] [Melodic]
From dawn till dusk, night and day,
The dance of desire is here to stay.

songs.title: The Ribosome Rag
songs.tags: A vaudeville-inspired track with a frantic tempo starts with ragtime piano, upright bass, and playful brass hits. Percussive snare marches drive the verses, while accordion and clarinet add lively color. Quick banjo strums accent breaks, and a zany xylophone solo energizes the bridge.
songs.prompt:
(Music: A frantic, up-tempo ragtime piano)

(Verse 1: The Code) 
 It starts in the vault, the big boss's design, The double-strand-helix, the spiraling line! But you can't take the book from the library shelf, No, you copy the page for your tiny-small self! So the Polymerase runs down the track, clickety-clack! It unzips the G's and the C's in the stack, It matches the A's and the T's (no, wait—U's!), A single-strand message, the cellular news!

(Chorus) 
Oh, the Ribososome Rag, it's a heck of a race! Gotta build a new part, gotta set the right pace! Read the tape, get the piece, make the chain, check the fold, It's the liveliest story that's ever been told! You might slip on a codon, your chain's in a knot, But you gotta keep building with all that you've got!

(Verse 2: The Factory) 
The messenger RNA slides in the groove, That big Ribosome is ready to make its first move! It grabs the new tape and it shouts, "What's the start?" It's an "A-U-G" signal to tear it apart! Well, to read it, at least, in its A-site and P-site, A three-letter codon in the cellular twilight! The T-R-N-A's shuttle the parts to the line, An amino acid matched to the triplet design!

(Chorus) 
Oh, the Ribosome Rag, it's a heck of a race! Gotta build a new part, gotta set the right pace! Read the tape, get the piece, make the chain, check the fold, It's the liveliest story that's ever been told! You might slip on a codon, your chain's in a knot, But you gotta keep building with all that you've got!

(Bridge: The Pressure Cooker!) 
A "G-G-C" brings in a glycine, that's swell! A "U-U-U" fetches a "phenyl..." (oh, well!) You gotta go fast, but you gotta be right, Or the protein you're building is in for a fright!

 But the copy was sloppy! A base was removed! Now the whole reading frame has been shifted and grooved! The Ribosome it reads "G-U-A-A-C-G..." but the words are all wrong, Now the batter is bitter, the chain is too long! It's a frameshift mutation, a cellular plight, This protein won't function! (Not quite!) (Whew!)

(Chorus) 
Oh, the Ribosome Rag, it's a heck of a race! Gotta build a new part, gotta set the right pace! Read the tape, get the piece, make the chain, check the fold, It's the liveliest story that's ever been told! You might slip on a codon, your chain's in a knot, But you gotta keep building with all that you've got!

(Outro: The Breakdown) 
Stop codon. Stop codon. (Faster) Release factor! Release factor! (Even faster) The new protein's done, see it wiggle and fold! But is it the protein the blueprint foretold? ...Was it? 
[One final, crashing piano chord!]

songs.title: Strange Love, in Secret Ways (Indie folk/Whimsical)
songs.tags: The track opens with fingerpicked acoustic guitar and playful percussion, layered with warm upright bass. Whimsical synth flourishes and glockenspiel add quirky pop touches. The chorus introduces handclaps and tight vocal harmonies. A brief bridge features toy piano before a lively final refrain.
songs.prompt:
The bonobo, so full of cheer,
Settles fights with love, no fear,
With every hug and kiss they share,
They find peace in love, they care.


The argonaut’s tricky love affair,
A tentacle he leaves to spare,
He sends it off, she knows it's true,
A token of his love to woo.


Strange love, in secret ways,
The wildest hearts have their own maze,
From tiny gifts to daring feats,
Love in nature is so sweet.


The red velvet mite, with silk to weave,
Creates a carpet, won’t you believe,
He dances there, a velvet show,
Hoping she'll say yes and glow.


And then the sea slug, oh so bold,
Shoots love darts—it's how they hold,
In twists and turns, they intertwine,
Their love is slow but so divine.

Strange love, in secret ways,
The wildest hearts have their own maze,
From tiny gifts to daring feats,
Love in nature is so sweet.


In the shadows and the deep,
Where silent creatures gently creep,
Love finds paths that are so odd,
A mystery dance, a wink from God.


Strange love, in secret ways,
The wildest hearts have their own maze,
From tiny gifts to daring feats,
Love in nature is so sweet.

[Outro – Male]
Strange, but true, love’s a game,
Even for those without a name.

songs.caption: This song celebrates the incredible diversity of animal mating rituals. It highlights the peaceful, conflict-avoiding sexual behavior of the bonobo; the external fertilization of the Argonaut octopus where the male detaches a sperm-carrying tentacle; the elaborate courtship display of the red velvet mite; and the unique "love dart" exchange of certain sea slugs and snails. Nature's way of finding a mate is truly strange and fascinating! #MatingRituals #AnimalBehavior

songs.title: Glycolysis
songs.tags:
Educational, Upbeat, Pop, Male Vocals
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE DETAILS
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Pop, Educational]
[STYLE: Upbeat, Catchy, Informative]
[MOOD: Fun, Energetic, Engaging]
[VOCALS: Male, Clear, Enthusiastic]
[ARRANGEMENT: Medium tempo, Bouncy rhythm, Catchy melodies]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Acoustic guitar, Piano, Drums, Bass, Synthesizers]
[TEMPO: Medium, 120-130 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Clean, Modern, Polished, Crisp vocals]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Bridge, Chorus, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: Energetic throughout, Catchy hooks, Clear transitions]
[EMOTIONS: Curiosity, Excitement, Interest, Engagement]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Intro]
[Upbeat instrumental with acoustic guitar and bouncy rhythm]

[Verse 1]
Let's talk about glycolysis,
A ten-step process, let's not miss,
Glucose breaks down, we must insist,
Energy's made, in this list.

[Pre-Chorus]
Two ATPs go in to start,
Glycolysis, it plays its part.

[Chorus]
Glycolysis, breaking glucose down,
In the cytoplasm, all around,
ATP and NADH,
Energy for every age.

[Verse 2]
Hexokinase steps in first,
Phosphorylates, quenches thirst,
Isomerase changes the shape,
Fructose-6-phosphate, it creates.

[Pre-Chorus]
Phosphofructokinase next in line,
Adding phosphates, it’s enzyme time.

[Chorus]
Glycolysis, breaking glucose down,
In the cytoplasm, all around,
ATP and NADH,
Energy for every age.

[Bridge]
Aldolase splits the sugar wide,
DHAP and G3P inside,
Triose phosphate isomerase,
Makes sure G3P stays in the race.

[Chorus]
Glycolysis, breaking glucose down,
In the cytoplasm, all around,
ATP and NADH,
Energy for every age.

[Verse 3]
G3P dehydrogenase,
Oxidation in a blaze,
1,3BPG is made,
Phosphoglycerate kinase parade.

[Pre-Chorus]
Phosphoglycerate mutase then,
2PG is formed again.

[Chorus]
Glycolysis, breaking glucose down,
In the cytoplasm, all around,
ATP and NADH,
Energy for every age.

[Outro]
[Upbeat instrumental with acoustic guitar, fading out]
Enolase and pyruvate kin-ase,
PEP to pyruvate, end of the race,
Four ATPs and NADH,
Glycolysis, our energy base.

songs.title: Vesicle Voyage
songs.tags: Bright analog synths, punchy electronic drums, and syncopated bass lines establish a playful 80s synth-pop groove. Percussive synth stabs accent the verses, while soaring synth leads and layered harmonies lift the chorus. Brief instrumental breaks feature catchy melodies for a joyful, engaging vibe.
songs.prompt:
[Verse] 
In the cell, a bustling city, life's demands are never pity 
Proteins made, a vital crew, with jobs to do, both old and new 
From DNA, an mRNA, translated on a ribosome, hooray! 
But where they go, that's the real quest, to pass the cell's ultimate test.

[Chorus] 
Oh, protein transport, a cellular dance, 
From the ribosome, they get their chance. 
To the cytosol, or to a membrane, 
Through pathways clear, again and again! 
With signals and pores, and chaperones too, 
The right destination, for me and for you!

[Verse] 
Some proteins stay, in the cytoplasm's flow, 
No special tag, nowhere else to go. 
Like enzymes busy, with tasks on their mind, 
Or structural scaffolding, of the cellular kind. 
But others need entry, to the nucleus grand, 
With NLS signals, held tight in their hand. 
Importin escorts them, through the NPC gate, 
A Ran-GTP push, sealing their fate!

[Chorus] 
Oh, protein transport, a cellular dance, 
From the ribosome, they get their chance. 
To the cytosol, or to a membrane, 
Through pathways clear, again and again! 
With signals and pores, and chaperones too, 
The right destination, for me and for you!

[Verse] 
Mitochondria call, with their own special plea, 
For power-producing proteins, for you and for me. 
Unfolded they journey, through TOM and then TIM, 
A membrane potential, making the trip quite prim. 
And peroxisomes waiting, for enzymes so keen, 
With PTS signals, a beautiful scene. 
Pex5 and Pex7, their cargo they haul, 
Into the matrix, standing up tall!

[Bridge] 
But some are too grand, for a post-translational trip, 
Co-translation's the answer, they make the right slip. 
An ER signal sequence, emerging so fast, 
SRP binds swiftly, translation held fast! 
To the ER membrane, the ribosome docks, 
Through Sec61's channel, defying all locks. 
Into the lumen they enter, with chaperones near, 
Folding and modifying, dispelling all fear.

[Chorus]
 Oh, protein transport, a cellular dance, 
From the ribosome, they get their chance. 
To the cytosol, or to a membrane, 
Through pathways clear, again and again! 
With signals and pores, and chaperones too, 
The right destination, for me and for you!

[Verse] 
From the ER to Golgi, in vesicles they glide, 
Through cis, medial, and trans, with modifications inside. 
At the Trans-Golgi Network, a sorting array, 
M6P for lysosomes, showing the way. 
Clathrin coats vesicles, to endosomes they fly, 
Releasing their cargo, beneath a low pH sky. 
For the membrane or secretion, a different design, 
Embedded or exiting, truly divine!

[Outro] So next time you're thinking, of what cells can do, 
Remember the proteins, and their journeys anew. 
From production to usage, a pathway so grand, 
The magic of transport, throughout the whole land! 
Yeah, protein transport, it never gives in! 
Let the cellular journey, forever begin!
songs.full_lyrics:
[Verse] 
In the cell, a bustling city, life's demands are never pity 
Proteins made, a vital crew, with jobs to do, both old and new 
From DNA, an mRNA, translated on a ribosome, hooray! 
But where they go, that's the real quest, to pass the cell's ultimate test.

[Chorus] 
Oh, protein transport, a cellular dance, 
From the ribosome, they get their chance. 
To the cytosol, or to a membrane, 
Through pathways clear, again and again! 
With signals and pores, and chaperones too, 
The right destination, for me and for you!

[Verse] 
Some proteins stay, in the cytoplasm's flow, 
No special tag, nowhere else to go. 
Like enzymes busy, with tasks on their mind, 
Or structural scaffolding, of the cellular kind. 
But others need entry, to the nucleus grand, 
With NLS signals, held tight in their hand. 
Importin escorts them, through the NPC gate, 
A Ran-GTP push, sealing their fate!

[Chorus] 
Oh, protein transport, a cellular dance, 
From the ribosome, they get their chance. 
To the cytosol, or to a membrane, 
Through pathways clear, again and again! 
With signals and pores, and chaperones too, 
The right destination, for me and for you!

[Verse] 
Mitochondria call, with their own special plea, 
For power-producing proteins, for you and for me. 
Unfolded they journey, through TOM and then TIM, 
A membrane potential, making the trip quite prim. 
And peroxisomes waiting, for enzymes so keen, 
With PTS signals, a beautiful scene. 
Pex5 and Pex7, their cargo they haul, 
Into the matrix, standing up tall!

[Bridge] 
But some are too grand, for a post-translational trip, 
Co-translation's the answer, they make the right slip. 
An ER signal sequence, emerging so fast, 
SRP binds swiftly, translation held fast! 
To the ER membrane, the ribosome docks, 
Through Sec61's channel, defying all locks. 
Into the lumen they enter, with chaperones near, 
Folding and modifying, dispelling all fear.

[Chorus]
 Oh, protein transport, a cellular dance, 
From the ribosome, they get their chance. 
To the cytosol, or to a membrane, 
Through pathways clear, again and again! 
With signals and pores, and chaperones too, 
The right destination, for me and for you!

[Verse] 
From the ER to Golgi, in vesicles they glide, 
Through cis, medial, and trans, with modifications inside. 
At the Trans-Golgi Network, a sorting array, 
M6P for lysosomes, showing the way. 
Clathrin coats vesicles, to endosomes they fly, 
Releasing their cargo, beneath a low pH sky. 
For the membrane or secretion, a different design, 
Embedded or exiting, truly divine!

[Outro] So next time you're thinking, of what cells can do, 
Remember the proteins, and their journeys anew. 
From production to usage, a pathway so grand, 
The magic of transport, throughout the whole land! 
Yeah, protein transport, it never gives in! 
Let the cellular journey, forever begin!

songs.title: Brief Life
songs.tags: Melancholic. powerpop
songs.prompt:
[Melancholy Verse 1]
In a tiny world on a thrips' egg they stay,
Adactylidium mites come out to play.
From a single egg, a larva springs to life,
Feasting from within, they thrive without strife.

[Powerpop Chorus]
Adactylidium, a rapid life's spree,
Born to live and die in their tiny sea.
Mating in shadows, within their mother's shell,
A brief, fleeting life, but they do it well.

[Melancholy Verse 2]
Inside the thrips' egg, they grow so fast,
Sisters and a brother, their bond will last.
A male to love them, right from the start,
Mating in darkness, with all their heart.

[Powerpop Chorus]
Adactylidium, a rapid life's spree,
Born to live and die in their tiny sea.
Mating in shadows, within their mother's shell,
A brief, fleeting life, but they do it well.

[Bridge]
Mother bursts open, it's time to go,
Sisters search for eggs, the cycle flows.
The male, his journey done, fades away,
In the world of Adactylidium, that's their way.

[Powerpop Chorus]
Adactylidium, a rapid life's spree,
Born to live and die in their tiny sea.
Mating in shadows, within their mother's shell,
A brief, fleeting life, but they do it well.

[Melodic Outro]
In the smallest places, life finds its way,
Adactylidium mites, live another day.
Their story brief, but oh so grand,
In the world of mites, they take their stand.

songs.title: Ancient Guardians
songs.tags: Epic indie rock with spoken word elements and layered background vocals.
songs.prompt:
Verse 1:
Coded deep in every cell we own
Herv-K stands guard like ancient stone
(Human Endogenous Retro Virus K)
Protecting life when it's newly grown
A viral gift we've always known
Pre-Chorus:
What once invaded now defends
This is how the story bends
Evolution's strange design
Turning foes to friends divine
Chorus:
Ancient guardians in our blood
Waves of time and viral flood
What once attacked now helps us grow
Nature's secrets, now we know
Verse 2:
Watch it dance through embryonic days
(Herv-K, Herv-K)
Shielding new life in countless ways
From modern threats it helps us fight
Viral wisdom burning bright
Bridge:
(Spoken with building intensity)
H-E-R-V-K!
Once our enemy
Now protects what we can't see
Life's first moments, safe and free
Final Chorus:
Ancient guardians in our code
Show us how far we have rode
Through evolution's twisting stream
Protecting every embryo's dream

songs.title: DNA Dance
songs.tags: Pop
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
In the nucleus, the magic starts to weave,
Double helix forming, strands that interleave.
Nucleotide connections, life's own trance,
Come and join the rhythm, it's the DNA dance.

[Pre-Chorus]
Every gene a story, every twist a chance,
Encoded in the sequence, the great romance.

[Chorus] [Catchy Hook]
DNA dance, feel the genes enhance,
In this cellular groove, let the life advance.
Every cell in step, in this grand expanse,
Join the bio beat, in the DNA dance.

[Verse 2]
Replication rhythm, strands unwind and pair,
Transcription tunes play, proteins in the air.
From the smallest cells to the largest plants,
All of life is moving to the DNA dance.

[Pre-Chorus]
Helical harmony, in every glance,
Twisting into life, in a mystic trance.

[Chorus] [Catchy Hook]
DNA dance, feel the genes enhance,
In this cellular groove, let the life advance.
Every cell in step, in this grand expanse,
Join the bio beat, in the DNA dance.



[Bridge]
Chromosomes are grooving, in the dance of fate,
Mutations make the music, changing up the state.
Genetic variations, give the tune its stance,
Evolution's heartbeat, in the DNA dance.

[Chorus] [Big Finish]
DNA dance, feel the genes enhance,
In this cellular groove, let the life advance.
Every cell in step, in this grand expanse,
Join the bio beat, in the DNA dance.

[Outro] [Fade to End]
Life's own choreography, in a mystic trance,
Forever we are moving, to the DNA dance.

songs.title: Spermatogenesis V3
songs.tags:
Funk
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE INFORMATION
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Funk]
[STYLE: Groovy, Upbeat, Energetic, Rhythmic]
[MOOD: Playful, Exciting, Vibrant, Fun]
[VOCALS: Male, Soulful, Dynamic]
[ARRANGEMENT: Syncopated rhythms, Punchy basslines, Horn sections]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Bass guitar, Electric guitar, Horns, Drums, Keyboards]
[TEMPO: Medium-fast, 100-120 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Crisp, Clean, Funky, High-energy, Produced by Nile Rodgers]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Bridge, Chorus, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: High-energy throughout, Dynamic shifts in bridge]
[EMOTIONS: Joy, Excitement, Celebration, Groove]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Short Instrumental Intro]

[Verse 1] [Groovy]
In the seminiferous tubules, it all begins,
Germ cells dividing, where the life spins.
Mitosis and meiosis, stages they go through,
From spermatogonia to something new.

[Pre-Chorus]
Chromosomes shuffle, genes recombine,
Creating gametes, one of a kind.

[Chorus] [Funky Hook]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

[Verse 2] [Groovy]
Primary spermatocytes take the stage,
Meiotic divisions, the genetic gauge.
Secondary spermatocytes split once more,
Turning into spermatids, ready to explore.

[Pre-Chorus]
Form and function, they start to align,
Tail and head developing, by design.

[Chorus] [Funky Hook]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

[Bridge]
In the epididymis, they learn to swim,
Gaining mobility, getting fit and trim.
Stored and waiting, for the right cue,
Ready for action, to meet the ovum too.

[Chorus] [Big Finish]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

[Outro] [Fade to End]
From germ cell to gamete, in a cellular bliss,
The wonder of life, through spermatogenesis.

songs.title: Marvels of Male Anatomy (Remastered)
songs.tags: Funk
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
The penis and the scrotum, on the outside seen (seen),
Glans and shaft together, working as a team (team).
The foreskin, it protects, with care and grace (grace),
Its function and purpose, let’s embrace (embrace).

[Pre-Chorus]
With intricate design, and complex role (role),
The male genitalia, a marvel to behold (behold).

[Chorus]
Oh, the anatomy, let’s celebrate (celebrate),
The wonders of the body, intricate and great (great).
From the testes to the prostate, all parts play (play),
A vital role in life, every single day (day).

[Verse 2]
The urethra runs through, for urine and seed (seed),
Dual-function marvel, meeting every need (need).
The testes in the scrotum, where sperm is made (made),
And testosterone, the hormonal cascade (cascade).

[Pre-Chorus]

[Chorus]


[Bridge]
The prostate adds the fluid, to nourish and protect (protect),
In seminal vesicles, more fluids are ejected (ejected).
Epididymis stores the sperm, till they mature (mature),
In the journey of creation, each step is sure (sure).

[Verse 3]
The bulbourethral glands, they play their part (part),
Providing lubrication, right from the start (start).
During arousal, they come alive (alive),
Ensuring comfort, helping bodies thrive (thrive).

[Pre-Chorus]
With intricate design, and complex role (role),
The male genitalia, a marvel to behold (behold).

[Chorus]
Oh, the anatomy, let’s celebrate (celebrate),
The wonders of the body, intricate and great (great).
From the testes to the prostate, all parts play (play),
A vital role in life, every single day (day).

[Outro]
Respect and understanding, let's aim to show (show),
The male genitalia, let our knowledge grow (grow).

songs.title: Unexpected Hit
songs.tags:
Indie Pop, Quirky, Unique
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE INFO
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Indie Pop, Quirky Pop]
[STYLE: Unique, Catchy, Celebratory]
[MOOD: Surprised, Joyful, Triumphant]
[VOCALS: Male and Female, Excited, Playful]
[ARRANGEMENT: Unconventional rhythms, Catchy hooks, Harmonized vocals]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Electric guitar, Synthesizers, Drums, Bass, Ukulele, Handclaps]
[TEMPO: Moderate to Fast, 120-140 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Bright, Clear, High-energy, Quirky elements]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Bridge, Chorus, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: High-energy throughout, with dynamic builds and quirky breakdowns]
[EMOTIONS: Surprise, Joy, Celebration]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Intro]
(Bright and energetic instrumental intro with quirky ukulele strums, handclaps, and upbeat synthesizers)

[Male]
[Verse 1]
Started with a melody, a simple tune,
Just for fun, in my room.
Sang my heart out, let it flow,
Little did I know how far it would go.

[Female]
[Pre-Chorus]
Shared it with my friends, they sang along,
Took a chance, played my song.
Posted it online, just to see,
And the world joined in, unexpectedly.

[Chorus - Both]
I made this song, but I never thought it would be a hit,
Now we're dancing to the rhythm, can't believe it.
From my heart to the world, it's a dream come true,
Singing loud, feeling proud, all because of you.

[Female]
[Verse 2]
Woke up one morning, notifications blew,
Messages and likes, from people I never knew.
Streamed around the globe, a viral delight,
Now we're all together, singing through the night.

[Male]
[Pre-Chorus] 
Tuned in the joy, feeling the beat,
Every note, oh, so sweet.
Thankful for the love, it's so surreal,
This melody's magic, can't believe it's real.

[Chorus - Both]
I made this song, but I never thought it would be a hit,
Now we're dancing to the rhythm, can't believe it.
From my heart to the world, it's a dream come true,
Singing loud, feeling proud, all because of you.

[Bridge - Both]
Dreams can fly, on wings of sound,
From a humble start, to the crowd.
Thank you all, for the chance to shine,
This unexpected hit, now it's yours and mine.

[Breakdown - Instrumental]
(Quirky instrumental breakdown with ukulele, handclaps, and playful synth melodies)

[Chorus - Both]
I made this song, but I never thought it would be a hit,
Now we're dancing to the rhythm, can't believe it.
From my heart to the world, it's a dream come true,
Singing loud, feeling proud, all because of you.

[Chorus - Both]
I made this song, but I never thought it would be a hit,
Now we're dancing to the rhythm, can't believe it.
From my heart to the world, it's a dream come true,
Singing loud, feeling proud, all because of you.

[Outro - Both]
(Instrumental layers build, then gradually fade)
Thank you all, for this unexpected ride,
Together we’ll keep the music alive.
From my room to your hearts, it’s been a trip,
I made this song, but I never thought it would be a hit.

(Fade out with celebratory instrumental, emphasizing quirky elements and unique sounds)

songs.title: Neuroscience of Love (Remastered)
songs.tags: Synth-pop, Neo-soul
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
There's a spark that's electric, a chemical light.

[Verse 1]
In the brain, it's a dance, dopamine in play,
With a rush and a glance, it can sweep us away.
Serotonin sings a song of serene,
While oxytocin binds us, unseen.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.

[Chorus]
Neuro-science of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.

[Verse 2]
In the limbic’s embrace, emotions intertwine,
Every kiss, every trace, in patterns we define.
With each memory stored, and each moment we share,
Our neurons explore, and love’s everywhere.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.



[Chorus]
Neuro-science of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.

[Bridge]
Chemical reactions, they pave the way,
In the maze of the mind, where our feelings stay.
With endorphins high, and the pathways clear,
In the neuro-science of love, we conquer fear.


[Outro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
Love's a journey of the brain, in the chemical light.

songs.title: Company Script Blues
songs.tags: Industrial Blues with heavy factory rhythms and a raw, powerful female vocal Incorporates industrial sounds, pneumatic beats, and smoky guitar riffs
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
*factory whistle blows*
*heavy machinery whirring*
[Low blues guitar riff]
[Verse 1]
Six AM train's running right on time
Another day working HD's production line
Got my ID and my company scrip
Trying to break free from this corporate grip
Breathe in that smoke from the refinery stacks
While my paycheck keeps me right on these tracks
[Pre-chorus]
They say my granddaddy worked these same machines
Mama too, now it's in my genes
But at night...
[Chorus]
I sing these company script blues
Paying rent with Hurston's dues
Every credit that I make
Is another one they take
But my voice rises above the factory news
Singing these company script blues
[Verse 2]
Worker's Respite fills up every night
Folks trying to drink till the timing's right
I take that stage and I sing their pain
Stories of freedom through the acid rain
HD owns the air that we breathe
But they can't own the songs up my sleeve
[Pre-chorus]
One more paycheck, one more show
Saving up till I can go
Watch me now...
[Bridge]
*pneumatic pistons and assembly line rhythms*
(When that whistle blows)
(Freedom's coming slow)
But these wings ain't meant for factory floors
Got my eyes set on those hangar doors
[Chorus]
Singing these company script blues
Paying rent with Hurston's dues
Every credit that I make
Is another one they take
But my voice rises above the factory news
Singing these company script blues
[Verse 3]
There's a Freelancer with my name
Sitting in dreams above this smoke and flame
One day soon I'll break these chains
Leave these smog-filled labor pains
Till then I'll sing for all those souls
Trapped in Hurston's corporate holds
[Final Chorus]
These are my company script blues
More than Hurston's paid-up dues
Every song that I create
Is my ticket through that gate
Soon my voice will spread the breaking news
No more company script blues
[Outro]
*factory sounds fading*
[Weary workers humming]
When that last whistle blows
Watch how far this songbird goes

songs.title: The Constrictor's Ballad (The Convergent Giants)
songs.tags: [Dramatic Ballad / Cinematic Country-Rock. This song has a powerful, driving rhythm with a classic country-rock guitar tone and massive, layered orchestral strings for a cinematic feel. The male vocal should be strong and passionate, delivering the facts like an epic narrator. The mix must be grand and expansive, with deep bass and climactic percussion to underscore the mythological scale of the constrictors' tale.]
songs.prompt:
[Intro] [Epic Cinematic Orchestration] [Deep electric guitar riff] [Building Intensity]
Down in the swampy basin, where the Amazon flows wide,
Lies a legend, thick and heavy, with nowhere left to hide.
The Anaconda, emerald green, a river dwelling queen,
The world’s heaviest serpent, a true aquatic machine.
But travel far across the ocean, across the eastern seas,
To jungles dark and deep in Asia, swaying in the trees,
You'll find the Python, long and slender, with a dazzling, net-like skin,
The world’s longest constrictor, where their ancient tale begins.

[Chorus] [Powerful Male Vocal] [Driving drums and synth bass] [Climactic]
Oh, the Constrictor’s Ballad, a squeeze that takes the breath,
From two mighty hunters, who conquer life and death.
They both use muscle power, with no venom in the bite,
And heat-sensing pits to find their prey, searching in the night.
But the families diverged, seventy million years ago,
A great split in the Cretaceous, when the dinosaurs walked below.

[Verse 2] [Acoustic guitar takes lead] [Stripped Back]
The Anaconda's built for water, it’s where she rules supreme,
Her eyes are perched right on her head, like a log in a murky stream.
She’s of the Boa family (Boidae), a lineage that’s old,
She gives live birth (viviparous), a truth that must be told.
The Python hails from Pythonidae, a separate, sturdy tree,
She’s an Old World mother, known for her maternal heat.
She'll coil around her clutch of eggs, oviparous and grand,
To shake and shiver through the night, a guardian of the land.

[Bridge] [Key change - Building Intensity] [Emotional Swell] [Layered Strings and Echoing Percussion] [Orchestral Build]
In the grand march of Squamata, though they look so much the same,
Their ancient cousins split apart, to play a different game.
The similarities they share, in strength and head and scale,
Is Convergent Evolution, a magnificent, wild trail.
Two masters of the ambush, in two separate hemispheres,
Evolving parallel perfection, conquering their fears.
They keep their vestigial legs, those tiny pelvic spurs,
A common ancestor's relic, a truth that still endures.

[Outro] [Return to Powerful Male Vocal and full band] [Powerful Outro]
So hail the bulky Anaconda, hail the lengthy Python, too,
Constrictor royalty, from the Old World to the New.
The same raw power, different homes, in a story ages long,
That's the Constrictor’s Ballad, their beautiful, mighty song.
[Fade Out] [Massive snake hiss effect] [Final guitar chord]

songs.caption: This song explores Convergent Evolution using the Anaconda (New World, Boidae family) and the Python (Old World, Pythonidae family). Despite splitting 70 million years ago, both evolved the same traits: immense constricting power, heat-sensing pits, and similar head shapes. The track highlights their key differences—Anacondas give live birth (viviparous), Pythons lay eggs (oviparous)—while noting their shared ancestry via vestigial pelvic spurs. #ConvergentEvolution #Anaconda #Python #Evolution

songs.title: Mitochondria Blues
songs.tags: soulful slow tempo blues
songs.prompt:
[Verse]
In the deep depths of my cells
There's a story untold
Mitochondria
The powerhouse
Where my spirit unfolds
They work so hard
Day and night
To keep me alive
But sometimes I feel like I'm losing the drive

[Verse 2]
These little organelles
They don't get enough credit
Without them
I wouldn't be standing where I'm headed
But oh
The mitochondria blues got me feeling low
They're working so hard
But I've got nothing to show

[Chorus]
Oh
The mitochondria blues
They're deep inside my soul
These little powerhouses
They keep me whole
But sometimes
I'm weary
And I'm feeling weak
The mitochondria blues
They make me speak

songs.title: The Elephant in the Fridge
songs.tags: Folk-Country Ballad
songs.prompt:
Verse 1:
Well I opened up my icebox
On a cold and frosty day
Shoved an elephant right in there
Like it was a bale of hay
(Boom-chicka, boom-chicka)
Verse 2:
Then along came Mister Giraffe
Said "I need a place to stay"
So I took that elephant right on out
To let the tall guy have his way
(Boom-chicka, boom-chicka)
Chorus:
There's footprints in my pâté
(Oh lord, there's footprints in my pâté)
Got a pachyderm situation
That's drivin' me crazy
When your fridge is full of wildlife
Life gets mighty hazy
And there's footprints in my pâté today
Verse 3:
They had themselves a foot race
Round the old savannah track
But that giraffe he couldn't make it
'Cause he was trapped behind my Tupperware stack
(Boom-chicka, boom-chicka)
[Final Chorus - slower, with feeling]
There's footprints in my pâté
(Lord have mercy, footprints in my pâté)
Got a pachyderm situation
That's drivin' me crazy
When your fridge is full of wildlife
Life gets mighty hazy
And there's footprints... in my... pâté... today
(Spoken in deep voice)
And that's the story of why I switched to butter.
