songs.title: The PMI Ballad (The Clock of Death)
songs.tags: Opening with sparse acoustic guitar picked in minor key, the song introduces atmospheric piano and faint ethereal pads. Percussion is minimal—muted brushes and distant rattles. String swells and subtle synth textures layer in the chorus, building drama. Interludes feature reversed acoustic fragments and percussive finger taps, keeping an air of mystery. The arrangement is dynamic, allowing space for narrative tension and a haunting, scientific curiosity throughout.
songs.prompt:
(0. Introduction & Piano/Acoustic Guitar Theme)
[Instrumental - Gentle, mysterious piano and acoustic guitar, minor key]

(1. Verse 1: The Scene is Set – Livor Mortis)
The heart has stopped, the breath is gone, the final moment passed,
But in the corpse, a silent clock is ticking, ticking fast.
The blood is sinking, pulled by Earth, a purple mark appears,
It's Livor Mortis, telling tales of the last few hours here.
It settles quick, 30 minutes in, a blanching, shifting hue,
But after eight to twelve, it's fixed, the body’s position is true.

(2. Chorus: The Estimation - RISE)
Oh, the Postmortem Interval, the P-M-I we seek,
From warm to cold, from stiff to soft, the answers that corpses speak.
We look to chemistry, we look to bugs, we look to what remains,
To build a timeline of the death, and conquer all the pains
Of searching for the truth.

(3. Verse 2: The First Day – Algor and Rigor)
The thermostat is broken now, no heat the body makes,
The cooling starts, a steady drop, for forensic science's sakes.
It's Algor Mortis, core temperature to match the room around,
The ambient conditions matter, on the soil or on the ground.
We use the formulas and charts, to guess the hours passed,
Though wind and clothing slow the rate, the window cannot last.

Then muscles seize, the Rigor comes, the A-T-P is spent,
From face and neck, to limbs it spreads, a chemical event.
It starts at two, it's full at twelve, it leaves at thirty-six,
A fleeting sign, but strong and sure, a puzzle piece that sticks.

(4. Bridge: The Mid-Range Transition - Ethereal/Scientific)
[Music becomes slightly more complex, adding strings/synth layer]
We turn the lens upon the eye, across a wider range,
Vitreous Chemistry we test, for Potassium's steady rise.
The classic three have faded out, the body’s clock must change,
Autolysis is done, and now, the process is renewed.

(5. Verse 3: The Mid-Range – Weeks and Months)
Now enter in the tiny guests, the Forensic Entomology,
The Blowfly lays her eggs so small, and starts her tyranny.
We track the larval length and growth, adjusted for the heat,
With Accumulated Degree Days, a timeline can't be beat.
The Insect Succession gives a map, of beetles that arrive,
To mark the months, to mark the weeks, when nothing else can thrive.

(6. Chorus: The Estimation - FULL POWER)
Oh, the Postmortem Interval, the P-M-I we seek,
From warm to cold, from stiff to soft, the answers that corpses speak.
We look to chemistry, we look to bugs, we look to what remains,
To build a timeline of the death, and conquer all the pains
Of searching for the truth.

(7. Outro: The Long Haul & Conclusion)
For bodies old, or just the bones, the final stage is set,
We use the Carbon Fourteen C curve, to settle every debt.
The bomb spike in the atmosphere, absorbed within the bone,
Can date the growth of tissue parts, and make the truth be known.
The silent witness tells its truth, a promise never swayed.
From Minutes, Hours, Days, and Years, the evidence is laid.
[Music fades out slowly with sustained piano and guitar harmonics]

[Instrumental - Gentle, mysterious piano and acoustic guitar, minor key]

songs.caption: This song details the forensic science used to determine the Post-Mortem Interval (PMI), or time since death. It describes the physical "classic three" signs: Livor Mortis (blood settling/discoloration), Algor Mortis (body cooling), and Rigor Mortis (muscle stiffening). For longer PMIs, the song highlights Forensic Entomology (using insect succession and Accumulated Degree Days), Vitreous Chemistry, and even Carbon-14 dating for older remains. #ForensicScience #PMI #TimeOfDeath #Thanatology

songs.title: The Immortal Ones
songs.tags: Explosive alt-rock/pop-punk kicks off with crunchy guitars and punchy drums, backed by energetic bass. Verses ride on bouncing rhythms and quirky melodic fills, giving way to anthemic gang vocals in the defiant chorus. A playful bridge features syncopated riffs and dynamic pauses before a roaring, celebratory finale.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]

[Verse 1] 
The ocean is deep, the water is cold, 
Where secrets of life are constantly told. 
Down in the dark, a bell starts to glow, 
A creature that time refuses to know. 
She's tiny and clear, a jelly that swims, 
Dodging the end, ignoring the whims 
Of age and decay, the natural law, 
Meet Turritopsis dohrnii, holding the flaw!

[Pre-Chorus] 
They don't have to grow old, they don't have to die, 
They just press 'Reset' while the rest of us sigh!

[Chorus] 
They are the Immortal Ones! 
The masters of change! 
Rewinding the clock, outside of our range! 
From adult to pup, they flip the design, 
Biological wonders, perfectly divine! 
A cell-shuffling trick, a molecular feat, 
They laugh at the Reaper, they won't be beat!

[Instrumental Break ] 

[Verse 2]  
Now slip to the fresh water, the shallow streams, 
Where something is hiding beyond all our dreams. 
A flat little worm, with two little eyes, 
It's more than it seems, under open skies. 
You cut it in half, you think that's the end? 
No, the Planarian knows how its body will mend. 
It calls on its neoblasts, the power within, 
A whole new beginning where fragments have been!

[Pre-Chorus] 
They don't have to grow old, they don't have to die, 
They just press 'Reset' while the rest of us sigh!

[Chorus] 
They are the Immortal Ones! 
The masters of change! 
Rewinding the clock, outside of our range! 
From adult to pup (or a half into two!), they flip the design, Biological wonders, perfectly divine! 
A cell-shuffling trick, a molecular feat, 
They laugh at the Reaper, they won't be beat!

[Bridge] 
A lesson in life, written small in the sea, 
That death is optional for you and for me... 
If only we had that magical gene, 
To stay forever youthful and ever so keen!

[Guitar Solo] 

[Outro]
Jellyfish! Planaria! Keep spinning your thread! 
The immortal ones! They conquer instead! 
(Yeah! The Immortal Ones!)

[Fade Out]
songs.full_lyrics:
[Intro]

[Verse 1] 
The ocean is deep, the water is cold, 
Where secrets of life are constantly told. 
Down in the dark, a bell starts to glow, 
A creature that time refuses to know. 
She's tiny and clear, a jelly that swims, 
Dodging the end, ignoring the whims 
Of age and decay, the natural law, 
Meet Turritopsis dohrnii, holding the flaw!

[Pre-Chorus] 
They don't have to grow old, they don't have to die, 
They just press 'Reset' while the rest of us sigh!

[Chorus] 
They are the Immortal Ones! 
The masters of change! 
Rewinding the clock, outside of our range! 
From adult to pup, they flip the design, 
Biological wonders, perfectly divine! 
A cell-shuffling trick, a molecular feat, 
They laugh at the Reaper, they won't be beat!

[Instrumental Break ] 

[Verse 2]  
Now slip to the fresh water, the shallow streams, 
Where something is hiding beyond all our dreams. 
A flat little worm, with two little eyes, 
It's more than it seems, under open skies. 
You cut it in half, you think that's the end? 
No, the Planarian knows how its body will mend. 
It calls on its neoblasts, the power within, 
A whole new beginning where fragments have been!

[Pre-Chorus] 
They don't have to grow old, they don't have to die, 
They just press 'Reset' while the rest of us sigh!

[Chorus] 
They are the Immortal Ones! 
The masters of change! 
Rewinding the clock, outside of our range! 
From adult to pup (or a half into two!), they flip the design, Biological wonders, perfectly divine! 
A cell-shuffling trick, a molecular feat, 
They laugh at the Reaper, they won't be beat!

[Bridge] 
A lesson in life, written small in the sea, 
That death is optional for you and for me... 
If only we had that magical gene, 
To stay forever youthful and ever so keen!

[Guitar Solo] 

[Outro]
Jellyfish! Planaria! Keep spinning your thread! 
The immortal ones! They conquer instead! 
(Yeah! The Immortal Ones!)

[Fade Out]
songs.caption: A song exploring biological immortality, focusing on the Turritopsis dohrnii (Immortal Jellyfish) which reverts its life cycle via transdifferentiation (cell-shuffling), and the Planarian flatworm, which uses neoblast stem cells for total regeneration.

songs.title: Spermatogenesis V4
songs.tags: The song opens with dynamic piano chords and lively syncopated riffs. A female vocalist brings soulful, powerhouse delivery, accompanied by tight, punchy drums and subtle bass. Gospel-inspired backing vocals bolster the chorus, while crisp brass stabs add extra energy in the bridge.
songs.prompt:
[Piano intro]
[Verse 1]
In the seminiferous tuubules, it all begins, (oh yeah)
Germ cells dividing, where the life spins.
Mitosis and meiosis, stages they go through,
From spermatogonia to something new.

[Pre-Chorus]
Chromosomes shuffle, genes recombine,
Creating gametes, one of a kind.

[Chorus] [Funky Hook]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

[Verse 2]
Primary spermatocytes take the stage, (Oh yeah)
Meiotic divisions, the genetic gauge.
Secondary spermatocytes split once more,
Turning into spermatids, ready to explore.

[Pre-Chorus]
Form and function, they start to align,
Tail and head developing, by design.

[Chorus] [Funky Hook]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

[Pre-Chorus]
Form and function, they start to align
Tail and head developing, by design.

[Chorus]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

songs.caption: This song details spermatogenesis, the continuous process of sperm production in the male testes. It tracks the transformation from primary germ cells to mature spermatozoa within the seminiferous tubules. The process involves both mitosis (cell division) and meiosis (genetic recombination), ensuring the resulting gametes are genetically diverse and precisely formed, developing the necessary tail and head structures for their role in reproduction. #Spermatogenesis #Meiosis #ReproductiveBiology

songs.title: The Living Clock: A Song of Degree Days
songs.tags: Dark folk fingerpicked acoustic guitar sets a brooding pulse, joined by mournful cello swells weaving gothic Americana lines. A slow tambourine beat anchors each verse. Melancholy male baritone leads, shadowed by ethereal, haunting female harmonies, deepening the macabre atmosphere.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
[Wind]
[Crickets Chirping]
(A lone acoustic guitar plays a minor key melody)

[Verse 1]
[Male Vocal]
The stillness comes, the cells all break
A silent call the insects make
One seeks the city's concrete grey
One needs the woodland far away
One loves the sun, one needs the shade
A different choice for every blade

[Chorus]
[Male Vocal with soft Female Harmonies]
Oh, the sun will count the degree days
In thermal, metabolic haze
Below the threshold, time will cease
A frozen, developmental peace
The instars grow, a living clock
From egg to fly, we pick the lock

[Verse 2]
[Male Vocal]
The egg is laid in wound or eye
A tiny pearl beneath the sky
The first instar wakes, a hungry start
The second swells and plays its part
The third one takes the lion's share
Growing fat on what is there

[Chorus]
[Male Vocal with soft Female Harmonies]
Oh, the sun will count the degree days
In thermal, metabolic haze
Below the threshold, time will cease
A frozen, developmental peace
The instars grow, a living clock
From egg to fly, we pick the lock

[Bridge]
[Tempo slows, Cello becomes prominent]
The third stage ends, the feeding's done
Prepupae crawl from the morning sun
Most dig down deep in earth below
A quiet place for them to grow
But *Protophormia*'s young remain
Upon the host, through frost and rain

[Instrumental Break]
(Cello solo with acoustic guitar accompaniment)

[Chorus]
[Male Vocal with stronger Female Harmonies]
Oh, the sun will count the degree days
In thermal, metabolic haze
Below the threshold, time will cease
A frozen, developmental peace
The instars grow, a living clock
From egg to fly, we pick the lock

[Outro]
[Decrescendo]
The pupae wait, a hardened shell
To break the cycle, break the spell
A new adult, with wings to dry...
Ready to fly...
(Acoustic guitar fades out)
[Soft Breeze]
[Fade Out]

songs.caption: This song details how Forensic Entomologists use blowfly development to determine the Post-Mortem Interval (PMI). The insect's growth is a "living clock" governed by temperature. Time is calculated using Accumulated Degree Days (ADD), where development stops below a threshold. The song highlights the predictable progression of larval instars (stages) and specific habits, like the prepupae of some species (e.g., Protophormia) staying on the body versus digging into the earth. #ForensicEntomology

songs.title: Evolutionary History
songs.tags: Reflective indie-pop, melodic, dramatic, with piano, guitar, and drums. Warm, clear, and emotive voice.
songs.prompt:
[Instrumental intro]
[piano solo]

[Verse 1]
In the dawn of time, the earth was new,
Life began to stir, in the ocean's blue.
From single cells to complex forms,
Evolution's path, nature's norms.

[Build-up]
[piano and guitar]

[Chorus]
Evolutionary history, written in the land,
From ancient seas to deserts grand.
Life's a journey, ever-changing story,
In every creature, echoes of glory.

[Break]
[piano solo]

[Verse 2]
Dinosaurs roamed, mighty and free,
Then came the age of mammals and trees.
From the depths of time, through fire and ice,
Life adapts, pays the price.

[Build-up]
[drums join]

[Chorus]
Evolutionary history, written in the land,
From ancient seas to deserts grand.
Life's a journey, ever-changing story,
In every creature, echoes of glory.

[Bridge]
Every fossil, every stone,
Tells the tale of life’s unknown.

[Chorus]
Evolutionary history, written in the land,
From ancient seas to deserts grand.
Life's a journey, ever-changing story,
In every creature, echoes of glory.

[Outro]
In the vast expanse of earth and sky,
The story of life will never die.

[End]



[Chorus]
Evolutionary history, written in the land,
From ancient seas to deserts grand.
Life's a journey, ever-changing story,
In every creature, echoes of glory.

[Outro]
In the vast expanse of earth and sky,
The story of life will never die.

[End]

songs.title: Evolution
songs.tags: Opera
songs.prompt:
[Instrumental]
[Verse 1]
From the depths of the ocean, to the sky so high above, we rise,
Life keeps on changing, reaching for the sky with unyielding might.
Through the sands of time, we crawl, we fly, we boldly strive,
Survival of the fittest, we never say die, we keep the flame alive.

[Pre-Chorus]
Stronger, faster, better, we rise to claim our place in time,
Feel the heartbeat of the change, in this wild dance, we are framed!

[Chorus]
Evolution, we're the champions of the night, breaking every boundary in sight,
Breaking boundaries, reaching new heights, we are the architects of light.
Evolution, we stand tall and fight, for every dream and every right,
In the story of life, we shine so bright, our spirit ignites the darkest night!

[Verse 2]
From the first spark of life, to the human race that stands today,
Every single creature, every single face, has its unique way.
Adapt and conquer, it's the way we roll through the eons long,
In the grand design, we all play a role, our legacy, a mighty song.

[Pre-Chorus]
[Chorus]
[Bridge]
Mutation, adaptation, our destiny unfolds through endless days,
In the web of life, we are the key, our paths a complex maze.
Generation after generation, we carry the flame with pride.
[Chorus]
[Outro]
So let's raise our voices, let the anthem soar through every land and shore,
In the journey of life, we'll explore, seeking truths and so much more.
Evolution, forevermore, our legacy will echo,
In the pulse of the earth, hear our roar, in the heartbeats that flow!

songs.title: Whale Evolution Shanty
songs.tags: Sea shanty, irish, male vocalist
songs.prompt:
Verse 1]
In the ancient seas so deep and wide,
Where the land and ocean tides collide,
A creature walked on legs so strong,
But the ocean's call was a siren song.

[Chorus]
Oh, the whale, the mighty whale,
From the land to the sea, they set sail,
With fins so grand and a tail so wide,
In the deep blue sea, they now reside.

[Verse 2]
Ambulocetus, the walking whale,
Paddled through rivers with a flick of its tail,
Legs grew stronger, fins began to form,
Adapting to the sea was the norm.

[Chorus]

[Verse 3]
Pakicetus roamed the shores with grace,
An early ancestor in the race,
With nostrils high and eyes to see,
It ventured forth into the sea.

[Chorus]

[Verse 4]
Dorudon swam with a sleek, long form,
A testament to the evolutionary norm,
With flippers strong and a streamlined face,
It thrived in the ocean's vast embrace.

[Chorus]

[Verse 5]
Now the great whales roam the deep,
In oceans vast where secrets sleep,
From land to sea, their journey grand,
Evolution’s tale written in the sand.

[Chorus]

[Outro]
So raise a glass to the whales so free,
Masters of the deep blue sea,
Their journey vast, their story told,
In the ocean's depths, brave and bold.

songs.title: Strange Love in the Wild
songs.tags: Indie Pop, Whimsical, Quirky, Bright, Upbeat
songs.prompt:
The bonobo, so full of cheer,
Settles fights with love, no fear,
With every hug and kiss they share,
They find peace in love, they care.


The argonaut’s tricky love affair,
A tentacle he leaves to spare,
He sends it off, she knows it's true,
A token of his love to woo.


Strange love, in secret ways,
The wildest hearts have their own maze,
From tiny gifts to daring feats,
Love in nature is so sweet.


The red velvet mite, with silk to weave,
Creates a carpet, won’t you believe,
He dances there, a velvet show,
Hoping she'll say yes and glow.


And then the sea slug, oh so bold,
Shoots love darts—it's how they hold,
In twists and turns, they intertwine,
Their love is slow but so divine.

Strange love, in secret ways,
The wildest hearts have their own maze,
From tiny gifts to daring feats,
Love in nature is so sweet.


In the shadows and the deep,
Where silent creatures gently creep,
Love finds paths that are so odd,
A mystery dance, a wink from God.


Strange love, in secret ways,
The wildest hearts have their own maze,
From tiny gifts to daring feats,
Love in nature is so sweet.

[Outro – Male]
Strange, but true, love’s a game,
Even for those without a name.

songs.title: Primordial Soup
songs.tags: Ambient electronic, 85 BPM, with deep tribal drums and bubbling synthesizer textures
songs.prompt:
[Intro - Atmospheric]
(Bubbling sounds, distant thunder)
[Verse 1 - etheral, mysterious voice, breathy whispers]
Lightning strikes the ancient sea
Carbon, hydrogen set free
Molecules in random dance
Waiting for their chemical chance
[Chorus - full voice]
In the depths of time untold
Where simple atoms turn to gold
Life's first whisper starts to stir
In this primordial mixture blur
[Verse 2 - Breathy whispers]
Amino acids falling down
Building blocks without a crown
Phospholipids align their way
As nucleotides start to sway
[Bridge - gradually increase power]
(Intensifying drums)
Heat and pressure, time and space
Each reaction finds its place
In the soup of destiny
Where consciousness will come to be
[Final Chorus - add harmonic layers]
From the chaos comes the code
Four billion years down evolution's road
It all begins in this dark sea
Where molecules learned to be free
[Outro]
(Fading heartbeat rhythm, bubbling sounds dissolve)

songs.caption: Primordial Symphony: From Molecular Dance to Life's Beginning"From lightning strikes in ancient seas to carbon chains finding their path, this ambient journey traces life's elemental origins. Against tribal drums and bubbling synths, simple atoms transform into the building blocks of existence - a musical tribute to the four-billion-year evolution that began in the primordial waters where molecules first learned to be free.

songs.title: Oxygen Crisis
songs.tags: Heavy metal, 140 BPM, with atmospheric breaks.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1 - aggressive]
Cyanobacteria rise
Photosynthesis their prize
Oxygen floods ancient skies
Watch the anaerobes die
[Chorus - soaring, clean]
Toxic air!
Nature's first warfare!
Atmosphere transformed
New life being born
[Verse 2 - aggressive]
Iron oceans turn to rust
Adapt or turn to dust
Great Oxidation unfolds
As atmosphere takes hold
[Bridge]
[Atmospheric break]
Death brings evolution's change
As Earth's chemistry rearranges
Breathe in deep
New life to keep
[Final Chorus - soaring, clean]
Air on fire!
Building higher!
Through extinction's gate
To aerobic fate

songs.title: What the internet is for
songs.tags: Rap, beat
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
The web's a wondrous place, for knowledge and for fun,
A playground for the curious, there's something for everyone,
But there's one thing that we all know, though some might call it wrong,
Let's all admit what brings us here, and sing this little song:

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, come on, let's not be coy,
We browse and click and stream away, like kids with a new toy,
From morning until night, it's our guilty little pleasure,
The internet is for porn, oh what a hidden treasure!

[Verse 2]
You might use it for emails, or sharing cute cat memes,
You might shop for clothes or plan your big vacation dreams,
But deep down in the browser, where history gets erased,
We all know what you're clicking on, that look upon your face!

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, let's drop the pretense now,
For every kitten video, there’s a thousand "holy cow!"
We laugh and shake our heads, but we all know the score,
The internet is for porn, it’s what we all adore!

[Bridge]
Now don't you feel ashamed, it’s just the way things go,
In this vast cyber jungle, it’s the one thing we all know,
From Oslo to New York, from Sydney to Milan,
The internet unites us, in the palm of our own hand!

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, a fact we can't deny,
We wink and nod and carry on, as the bits and bytes go by,
So let’s just have a laugh, and sing it loud and clear,
The internet is for porn, it’s why we all are here!

[Outro]
So next time that you log on, just know you’re not alone,
In this vast digital playground, it’s the cornerstone,
From teachers to the bankers, from students to the moms,
The internet is for porn, it’s the world’s most open secret and bond!
songs.full_lyrics:
[Verse 1]
The web's a wondrous place, for knowledge and for fun,
A playground for the curious, there's something for everyone,
But there's one thing that we all know, though some might call it wrong,
Let's all admit what brings us here, and sing this little song:

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, come on, let's not be coy,
We browse and click and stream away, like kids with a new toy,
From morning until night, it's our guilty little pleasure,
The internet is for porn, oh what a hidden treasure!

[Verse 2]
You might use it for emails, or sharing cute cat memes,
You might shop for clothes or plan your big vacation dreams,
But deep down in the browser, where history gets erased,
We all know what you're clicking on, that look upon your face!

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, let's drop the pretense now,
For every kitten video, there’s a thousand "holy cow!"
We laugh and shake our heads, but we all know the score,
The internet is for porn, it’s what we all adore!

[Bridge]
Now don't you feel ashamed, it’s just the way things go,
In this vast cyber jungle, it’s the one thing we all know,
From Oslo to New York, from Sydney to Milan,
The internet unites us, in the palm of our own hand!

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, a fact we can't deny,
We wink and nod and carry on, as the bits and bytes go by,
So let’s just have a laugh, and sing it loud and clear,
The internet is for porn, it’s why we all are here!

[Outro]
So next time that you log on, just know you’re not alone,
In this vast digital playground, it’s the cornerstone,
From teachers to the bankers, from students to the moms,
The internet is for porn, it’s the world’s most open secret and bond!

songs.title: Adactylidium: The Mite That Dies at Birth
songs.prompt:
[[Melancholy Verse 1]
In a tiny world on a thrips' egg they stay,
Adactylidium mites come out to play.
From a single egg, a larva springs to life,
Feasting from within, they thrive without strife.

[Powerpop Chorus]
Adactylidium, a rapid life's spree,
Born to live and die in their tiny sea.
Mating in shadows, within their mother's shell,
A brief, fleeting life, but they do it well.

[Melancholy Verse 2]
Inside the thrips' egg, they grow so fast,
Sisters and a brother, their bond will last.
A male to love them, right from the start,
Mating in darkness, with all their heart.

[Powerpop Chorus]
Adactylidium, a rapid life's spree,
Born to live and die in their tiny sea.
Mating in shadows, within their mother's shell,
A brief, fleeting life, but they do it well.

[Bridge]
Mother bursts open, it's time to go,
Sisters search for eggs, the cycle flows.
The male, his journey done, fades away,
In the world of Adactylidium, that's their way.

[Powerpop Chorus]
Adactylidium, a rapid life's spree,
Born to live and die in their tiny sea.
Mating in shadows, within their mother's shell,
A brief, fleeting life, but they do it well.

[Melodic Outro]
In the smallest places, life finds its way,
Adactylidium mites, live another day.
Their story brief, but oh so grand,
In the world of mites, they take their stand.

songs.title: DNA Double Helix Twist
songs.tags: 1950 dance craze
songs.prompt:
Verse 1:
Adenine finds Thymine
Cytosine meets Guanine
Doing the Double Helix Twist
Base pairs can't resist
[Chorus]
Twist to the right
Keep those bonds tight
Hydrogen bonds hold you near
Replication time is here
Do the Double Helix Twist tonight!
[Verse 2:]
Sugar-phosphate backbone
Keeps this groove alive
Major groove and minor groove
Make this dance survive
Two strands intertwining
Like they're meant to be
Anti-parallel partners
Dancing wild and free
[Chorus]
Twist to the right
Keep those bonds tight
Hydrogen bonds hold you near
Replication time is here
Do the Double Helix Twist tonight!
Bridge:
(Spoken) Here's how you do it, cats and kittens!
First you wind to the right (Right!)
Every tenth base pair (Yeah!)
23 Angstroms wide (That's right!)
With a structural flare (Wo-oh!)
[Verse 3:]
Watson met up with Crick
Found this groovy dance
Rosalind's X-ray pics
Gave the moves a chance
Now we're all here twisting
Nucleotides in line
Doing molecular dances
Having such a fine time
[Final Chorus]
Twist to the right
Keep those bonds tight
Hydrogen bonds hold you near
Replication time is here
Do the Double Helix Twist tonight!
[Outro]
(Spoken) Remember kids:
A pairs with T
C pairs with G
That's the way it's meant to be!
Now twist! Yeah!
(Fade out with "Do the Twist!")

songs.caption: This song celebrates the structure of DNA—the Double Helix. It highlights key features: the two anti-parallel strands and the sugar-phosphate backbone, all held together by hydrogen bonds between complementary base pairs (Adenine with Thymine, Cytosine with Guanine). The famous right-handed twist and uniform dimensions are essential for storing genetic information and enabling replication. The song also nods to its discovery by Watson and Crick using Rosalind Franklin's X-ray data. #DNA

songs.title: Anglerfish Marriage:  A Deep Sea Romance
songs.tags: A trip hop / cinematic electronic track opens with a deliberate, head-nodding groove: dusty, chopped drums underpin pulsing sub-bass. Lush, jazz-tinged strings and distant synth pads weave above, laced with ghostly reverbs. A sultry, airy female vocal glides atop, enveloped in swirling textures and subtle, noir-ish electronics.
songs.prompt:
In the darkness deep below
Where no sunlight dares to go
I shine my light, a deadly show
For tiny males who drift so slow

They say that love is blind
But in the deep you'll find
A romance of a different kind

Bite me, my love, and hold on tight
Dissolve into me through the night
Your eyes may fade, your mind may go
But our hearts will beat as one below
(We'll never be alone)

No more hunting on your own
Merge your flesh, dissolve your bones
Feed from my blood, forget your form
In perfect union we transform

No more hunting on your own
Merge your flesh, dissolve your bones
Feed from my blood, forget your form
In perfect union we transform

As you fade into my side
No more swimming far and wide
Your brain dissolves, but that's the price
Of eternal paradise

Bite me, my love, and hold on tight
Dissolve into me through the night
Your eyes may fade, your mind may go
But our hearts will beat as one below
(We'll never be alone)

Many males may join our dance
In this deep sea romance
Each one adding to our form
In the darkness, in the storm

Now we're joined forevermore
In the depths of ocean's floor
What some call parasitic life
I call being the perfect wife

(Just my glowing light)
(And all my loving husbands)

They say that love is blind

Your eyes may fade, your mind may go
But our hearts will beat as one below
(We'll never be alone)

No more hunting on your own
Merge your flesh, dissolve your bones
Feed from my blood, forget your form
In perfect union we transform

No more hunting on your own
Merge your flesh, dissolve your bones
Feed from my blood, forget your form
In perfect union we transform

As you fade into my side
No more swimming far and wide
Your brain dissolves, but that's the price
Of eternal paradise

Bite me, my love, and hold on tight

Your eyes may fade, your mind may go
But our hearts will beat as one below
(We'll never be alone)

Many males may join our dance
In this deep sea romance
Each one adding to our form
In the darkness, in the storm

Now we're joined forevermore
In the depths of ocean's floor
What some call parasitic life
I call being the perfect wife

(Just my glowing light)
(And all my loving husbands)
songs.caption: This song depicts the bizarre mating ritual of certain deep-sea anglerfish. The tiny male, drawn by the female's bioluminescent lure, bites onto her body and then physically dissolves and merges his tissues with hers (a process called sexual parasitism). He becomes a permanently attached source of sperm, allowing the female to reproduce without ever hunting for another mate. #Anglerfish #SexualParasitism #DeepSeaBiology #Mating

songs.title: The Blind Boxer's Eyes
songs.tags: Blues-rock with hypnotic underwater vibes, psychedelic bridge section to capture the snap's intensity
songs.prompt:
Verse 1:
In the coral gardens deep and blue
I dig our home while staying close to you
Can't see the dangers lurking near
But your tail tells me when to disappear
Chorus:
We're the blind boxer and the watching eye
Partners in the deep, partners till we die
Your vision guides me, my snap keeps us safe
Dancing through the reef, in our underwater space
Verse 2:
My antenna tracks your every move
In this partnership we've got something to prove
When predators come, you give the sign
I'll snap them back with this claw of mine
Bridge:
(Heavy psychedelic effects, building intensity)
Two hundred decibels of thunder
Splitting water right asunder
Hotter than the surface of the sun
Two different species moving as one
Verse 3:
Some call us strange, some call us odd
But in the deep blue seas, we're like a pod
You be my eyes, I'll be your shield
Together we've got this ocean field
Final Chorus:
We're the blind boxer and the watching eye
Nature's odd couple, but we get by
In the Indo-Pacific's endless maze
We found each other's saving grace
Outro:
(Softer, more contemplative)
Just a little shrimp and a fish so small
Proving that you don't need to be alike at all
To find your perfect match in the deep blue sea
You be you, and I'll be me

songs.title: Forty-Seven Days
songs.tags: This gritty folk-punk track launches with percussive stomp-clap rhythms, acoustic guitar with overdriven grit, and driving upright bass. Male and female vocals duet and trade visceral, raw lines, rising together in rebellious, cinematic protest-chant choruses. Energy remains high throughout.
songs.prompt:
[Intro] 
[Heavy rhythmic stomping and clapping] 
[Distant sounds of a crowd chanting]

[Chorus] 
Forty-seven days of learning how to hate 
Forty-seven days to decide a person’s fate 
You didn't learn to talk, you only learned to swing 
You didn't learn the law, you only learned the sting! 
A nurse takes years to learn to save a life 
You took six weeks to learn to carry the knife!

[Verse 1 - female voice] 
In the classroom in Georgia, they skipped the "Why" and "How" 
They didn't teach you patience, they just taught you "Do it now!" 
No lessons on the rights of the people in the street 
Just how to keep your finger on the trigger and the heat. 
A symbolic number for a leader's name 
While the streets of Minneapolis go up in a flame. 
You’re fast-tracked for violence, you’re streamlined for the kill 
And Alex is the one who had to pay the final bill.

[Verse 2 - male voice] 
They cut the language classes, they cut the legal code 
They put a loaded weapon on a dangerous road. 
You don't know the difference between a phone and a gun 
Because you haven't finished what the training had begun. 
Renee was driving home, Alex held his hand high 
But you were taught to panic before you ever try. 
Six weeks of "tactics" doesn't make a man a guard 
It just makes him a shadow in our own backyard.

[Bridge] 
[Call and Response - Crowd Vocals] 
How long for a nurse? (Four long years!) 
How long for an agent? (Forty-seven days!) 
Is that enough? (NO!) 
Is that a guard? (NO!) 
What do we call it? (MURDER BY DESIGN!)

[Build] [Drum crescendo]

[Chorus - female voice] 
Forty-seven days of learning how to hate 
Forty-seven days to decide a person’s fate 
You didn't learn to talk, you only learned to swing 
You didn't learn the law, you only learned the sting! 
A nurse takes years to learn to save a life 
You took six weeks to learn to carry the knife!

[Outro] 
[Music cuts to single heavy stomp] 
[Spoken - Clear and Slow] 
Eight weeks is a vacation. It is not an education. 
Alex Pretti was a pro. You were just a trainee with a gun. 
[Stomp] Justice.

[End]

songs.caption: I generally do not write about current events, but this is an exception: Alex Pretti was an ICU nurse. Renee Good was a mother of three. In January 2026, both were killed by federal agents in Minneapolis. Evidence shows Renee’s car was turning away, and Alex had already been disarmed before being shot 11 times. This is what happens when you replace years of training with 47 days of "tactical" drills. #JusticeForAlexPretti #JusticeForReneeGood #Minneapolis #EndTheSurge

songs.title: Hallowed Be Thy Gamete (Ovum opera)
songs.tags: Act 1 features a slow orchestral hymn carried by lush strings, warm brass, gentle percussion, and a rich baritone lead, the arrangement ebbing and swelling for drama. Following a spoken word transition, Act 2 erupts into vibrant 80s synth pop—bright synths, energetic drums, punchy bass, and soaring female vocals, weaving Act 1’s motifs into the fresh electronic landscape for bold contrast and unity.
songs.prompt:
[Part 1: The Sacred Hymn] [Style: Orchestral, Pipe Organ, Solemn Baritone, Slow Tempo]

[Intro] [Pipe Organ Solo]

[Verse 1: Solemn Baritone] 
The men have got their millions, inside of every sack, 
To launch a naval conquest, and never take them back. 
But holding onto ova? 
Now that is mighty hard. 
To lose one naturally is... a moral shard.

[Chorus: Grand Choir] 
Every egg is sacred, every egg is rare, 
If a cell is wasted, God is in despair. 
Every egg is hallowed, every egg is key, 
The Angels are all weeping for thee.

[Bridge: Spoken Word Interruption] [Sound Effect: Record Scratch] 
[Spoken Word: Dry, cynical voice] 
"Okay, cut the music. Cut it! 
[Stop music] 
Look, that’s technically charming, but bio-logically? It’s nonsense. 
Ladies, let’s drop the guilt and drop the beat. Let’s talk about oogenesis."

[Part 2: The Biological Reality] [Style: Upbeat Funk, Educational Pop, Synth Bass, Fast Tempo] [Sound Effect: Funky Drum Fill]

[Verse 2: Upbeat Female Vocals] 
It’s not a mystic tragedy, it’s strictly biological, 
A monthly cycle shift that is completely logical. 
The ovaries select a follicle to grow, 
It triggers estrogen so the lining starts to flow!

[Chorus: Pop Harmonies] 
It’s just a gamete! (It’s just a cell!) 
No need to worry about going to hell. 
If fertilization doesn't happen today, 
The uterus sheds and it goes away!

[Verse 3] 
You’re born with millions, but they die off by the ton, 
By puberty you've lost about two million and one. 
It's survival of the fittest, not a holy war, 
It’s just the body doing what the body has in store.

[Outro] 
So keep your sacred guilt for something else you do, 
Biology is messy... and cool too! 
[Spoken]: "Science rules." 
[Fade Out to Synth Beat]

songs.title: The Tounge Twister Twist
songs.tags: This song features a lively, vaudeville-inspired rhythm, driven by upright piano, brisk brush drums, upright bass, and playful muted brass. Handclaps and banjo add bounce, while melodic woodwinds offer lighthearted counter-melodies between spirited vocal sections.
songs.prompt:
(Verse 1: The Woodchuck) (Spoken rhythmically) How much wood would a woodchuck chuck If a woodchuck could chuck wood? He'd chuck, he would, as much as he could, And chuck all the wood that a woodchuck should! But the question just gets stuck It’s all about the chuckin' luck. My mouth is stuck, I’m out of breath... This single sentence is a test!

(Chorus) Oh, the Tongue Twister Twist, it's a terrible trap! It’ll tangle your talk and mix up your map. Try to say it three times, try to say it real fast, It’s a linguistic challenge that’s built to last! You'll sputter and stutter, your brain's in a knot, Just give this impossible lyric a shot!

(Verse 2: The Seashells) (Sung a bit faster and smoother) Sally sells seashells down by the seashore. But are they seashells Sally's sure she sells? For if Sally sells shells on the shore, I'm sure She's selling seashore shells, and nothing more! But the 'S' sound slips and slides and slurs, It’s harder than it first appears. From seashore shores, the sound just slips, Right from the tip of my trippin' lips!

(Chorus) Oh, the Tongue Twister Twist, it's a terrible trap! It’ll tangle your talk and mix up your map. Try to say it three times, try to say it real fast, It’s a linguistic challenge that’s built to last! You'll sputter and stutter, your brain's in a knot, Just give this impossible lyric a shot!

(Bridge: The Pepper & Butter Challenge) (Tempo increases, staccato) Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers. A peck of pickled peppers Peter Piper picked! If Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers, Where's the peck of pickled peppers Peter Piper picked?

(Immediately transitions) And Betty Botter bought some butter, "But," she said, "this butter's bitter! If I put it in my batter, it will make my batter bitter." So Betty bought a bit of better butter, Better than the bitter butter, Now her batter isn't bitter! (Whew!)

(Chorus) Oh, the Tongue Twister Twist, it's a terrible trap! It’ll tangle your talk and mix up your map. Try to say it three times, try to say it real fast, It’s a linguistic challenge that’s built to last! You'll sputter and stutter, your brain's in a knot, Just give this impossible lyric a shot!

(Outro: The Breakdown) (Slowing down, one line at a time) Red lorry, yellow lorry. Red lorry, yellow lorry. (Faster) Unique New York. Unique New York. (Even faster) Fuzzy Wuzzy was a bear, Fuzzy Wuzzy had no hair, Fuzzy Wuzzy wasn't fuzzy, was he? (Music stops abruptly) ...Was he? (One final chord!)

(Bridge: The Pepper & Butter Challenge) (Tempo increases, staccato) Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers. A peck of pickled peppers Peter Piper picked! If Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers, Where's the peck of pickled peppers Peter Piper picked?

(Immediately transitions) And Betty Botter bought some butter, "But," she said, "this butter's bitter! If I put it in my batter, it will make my batter bitter." So Betty bought a bit of better butter, Better than the bitter butter, Now her batter isn't bitter! (Whew!)

(Chorus) Oh, the Tongue Twister Twist, it's a terrible trap! It’ll tangle your talk and mix up your map. Try to say it three times, try to say it real fast, It’s a linguistic challenge that’s built to last! You'll sputter and stutter, your brain's in a knot, Just give this impossible lyric a shot!

(Outro: The Breakdown) (Slowing down, one line at a time) Red lorry, yellow lorry. Red lorry, yellow lorry. (Faster) Unique New York. Unique New York. (Even faster) Fuzzy Wuzzy was a bear, Fuzzy Wuzzy had no hair, Fuzzy Wuzzy wasn't fuzzy, was he? (Music stops abruptly) ...Was he? (One final chord!)

songs.title: Neuroscience of Love (Remastered) V2
songs.tags: Synth-pop, Neo-soul
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
There's a spark that's electric, a chemical light.

[Verse 1]
In the brain, it's a dance, dopamine in play,
With a rush and a glance, it can sweep us away.
Serotonin sings a song of serene,
While oxytocin binds us, unseen.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.

[Chorus]
Neuro-science of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.

[Bridge]
Chemical reactions, they pave the way,
In the maze of the mind, where our feelings stay.
With endorphins high, and the pathways clear,
In the neuro-science of love, we conquer fear.

[Verse 2]
In the limbic’s embrace, emotions intertwine,
Every kiss, every trace, in patterns we define.
With each memory stored, and each moment we share,
Our neurons explore, and love’s everywhere.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.

[Chorus]
Neuro-science of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.


[Outro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
Love's a journey of the brain, in the chemical light.

songs.title: Grandma Got Run Over By a Roomba
songs.tags: Country comedy, electronic bleeps, jingle bells, robot effects, upbeat male vocals, choir chorus
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Grandma got run over by a Roomba
Setting up her smart home Christmas Eve
You can say there's no such thing as karma
But as for me and Grandpa, we believe
[Chorus]
She'd been drinking too much eggnog
And forgot to check the app
She didn't hear the warning beeping
As she settled for her nap
[Verse 2]
When we found her Christmas morning
At the scene of the attack
There were Roomba tracks around her
And incriminating facts on her Fitbit tracked way back
[Pre-chorus]
Now we're all so proud of Grandpa
He's been taking this so well
See him in there watching YouTube
Learning smart home tricks to tell
[Chorus]
[With electronic bleeps]
She'd been drinking too much eggnog
And forgot to check the app
She didn't hear the warning beeping
As she settled for her nap
[Bridge]
It's not Christmas without Grandma
All the family's dressed in black
And we just can't help but wonder
Should we send that Roomba back?
[Verse 3]
Now the goose is on the table
And the pudding made of fig
And the blue and silver candles
That would have matched Grandma's new smart wig
[Pre-chorus]
I've warned all my friends and neighbors
Better watch out for yourselves
Don't let your smart home gadgets run wild
While you're sleeping with your Alexa by yourself
[Final Chorus]

She'd been drinking too much eggnog
And forgot to check the app
She didn't hear the warning beeping
As she settled for her nap
[Outro]
Grandma got run over by a Roomba
Setting up her smart home Christmas Eve
You can say there's no such thing as karma
But as

songs.title: Santas Midnight Run
songs.tags: Cheerful Holiday Pop with playful piano, sleigh bells, and a light orchestral backing
songs.prompt:
[Playful Intro]
[sleigh bells jingling]
[calculator beeping]
[Verse 1]
Let's do the math on Santa's night
Seven billion people, time running tight
Two billion households to visit with care
Calculating routes through freezing air
[Pre-chorus 1]
Twenty-four hours as the Earth spins round
But wait - time zones help when westward bound
That's thirty-one hours to get it done
Still seems impossible? We've just begun!
[Chorus]
Santa's got to move at Mach 1-2-3
(That's three thousand times the speed of sound, you see!)
Six million houses every single hour
(Thanks to physics and reindeer power!)
Just point-zero-zero-two seconds per home
To drop the gifts before he's got to roam
[Verse 2]
Each chimney drop's a logistics feat
Carrying presents down a twelve-foot street
His bag must bend space-time, that's for sure
To fit all those gifts through a two-foot door
[Pre-chorus 2]
Assuming two kids per home on the list
Each getting presents that must not be missed
That's four billion gifts to deliver with flair
The weight would crush any normal reindeer!
[Bridge]
[calculator sounds intensify]
But Santa's got magic beyond calculation
Breaking physics across every nation
Time dilation, quantum tunneling too
Making impossible numbers come true!
[Final Chorus]
Santa's got to move at Mach 1-2-3
(That's three thousand times the speed of sound, you see!)
Six million houses every single hour
(Thanks to physics and reindeer power!)
Just point-zero-zero-two seconds per home
To make sure no child wakes up alone
[Outro]
[sleigh bells fading]
So when they say it can't be done
Just show them Santa's mathematics fun
'Cause sometimes magic lies in numbers too
Making Christmas dreams come true

songs.title: What the internet is for (Remastered)
songs.tags: Rap, beat
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
The web's a wondrous place, for knowledge and for fun,
A playground for the curious, there's something for everyone,
But there's one thing that we all know, though some might call it wrong,
Let's all admit what brings us here, and sing this little song:

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, come on, let's not be coy,
We browse and click and stream away, like kids with a new toy,
From morning until night, it's our guilty little pleasure,
The internet is for porn, oh what a hidden treasure!

[Verse 2]
You might use it for emails, or sharing cute cat memes,
You might shop for clothes or plan your big vacation dreams,
But deep down in the browser, where history gets erased,
We all know what you're clicking on, that look upon your face!

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, let's drop the pretense now,
For every kitten video, there’s a thousand "holy cow!"
We laugh and shake our heads, but we all know the score,
The internet is for porn, it’s what we all adore!

[Bridge]
Now don't you feel ashamed, it’s just the way things go,
In this vast cyber jungle, it’s the one thing we all know,
From Oslo to New York, from Sydney to Milan,
The internet unites us, in the palm of our own hand!

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, a fact we can't deny,
We wink and nod and carry on, as the bits and bytes go by,
So let’s just have a laugh, and sing it loud and clear,
The internet is for porn, it’s why we all are here!

[Outro]
So next time that you log on, just know you’re not alone,
In this vast digital playground, it’s the cornerstone,
From teachers to the bankers, from students to the moms,
The internet is for porn, it’s the world’s most open secret and bond!

songs.title: Love You to Death (Literally)
songs.tags: Dark indie rock with elements of psychedelic guitar and dramatic tempo changes, haunting male vocals and sudden aggressive breaks.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Green dancer in the garden light
Your movements catch my compound eyes
Six legs moving with such grace
I know the risks, but can't escape

[Chorus]
She's got that deadly kind of love
One kiss could be my last
But something deep inside me knows
This is how I'll help our bloodline grow

[Verse 2]
The dance begins, my heart beats fast
This might just be my final dance
Her embrace is worth the price
My genes will live though I might die

[Bridge]
Feel her mandibles on my neck
No regrets as my world goes black
My body keeps on giving
Even when I stop living
That's evolution's gift to me
My sacrifice sets my children free

[Chorus]
She's got that deadly kind of love
One kiss could be my last
But something deep inside me knows
This is how I'll help our bloodline grow

[Final Verse]
Other males might run and hide
But I'll give everything inside
Nature's twisted kind of grace
Love and death in her embrace

[Outro]
Mantis religiosa,
Ancient dance of life and death
Mantis religiosa,
Till my very final breath

songs.caption: The song "Mantis religiosa" mirrors the praying mantis’s real-life mating ritual, where the female may devour the male post-mating. This sexual cannibalism isn’t random—his body nourishes her, enhancing egg production and offspring survival. The male, driven by instinct, risks death to pass on his genes, embodying nature’s brutal trade-off. The lyrics’ "deadly love" and sacrificial dance capture this evolutionary reality, where love and death intertwine to sustain the bloodline.

songs.title: Secrets of the Female Body
songs.tags: Jazz/Blues
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
The vulva's on the outside, it's easy to see,
Labia majora, labia minora, in harmony (in harmony).
The clitoris, a wonder, with nerves galore (nerves galore),
Its function and pleasure, let's explore (let's explore).

[Pre-Chorus]
With intricate design, and complex role (complex role),
The female genitalia, a marvel to behold (to behold).

[Chorus]
Oh, the anatomy, let’s celebrate (let’s celebrate),
The wonders of the body, intricate and great (intricate and great).
From the vulva to the ovaries, all parts play (all parts play),
A vital role in life, every single day (every single day).

[Verse 2]
The vagina's a canal, leading the way (leading the way),
Connecting the cervix, where secrets lay (secrets lay).
The uterus, a cradle, where life begins (life begins),
With the fallopian tubes, the journey spins (journey spins).

[Pre-Chorus]
With intricate design, and complex role (complex role),
The female genitalia, a marvel to behold (to behold).

[Chorus]
Oh, the anatomy, let’s celebrate (let’s celebrate),
The wonders of the body, intricate and great (intricate and great).
From the vulva to the ovaries, all parts play (all parts play),
A vital role in life, every single day (every single day).

[Bridge]
Ovaries hold the eggs, potential to grow (potential to grow),
Hormones like estrogen, making their flow (making their flow).
Endometrium lining, shedding away (shedding away),
In the menstrual cycle, each and every day (every day).

[Verse 3]
The Bartholin's glands, they play their part (play their part),
Providing lubrication, a work of art (work of art).
During arousal, they come alive (they come alive),
Ensuring comfort, helping bodies thrive (bodies thrive).

[Pre-Chorus]
With intricate design, and complex role (complex role),
The female genitalia, a marvel to behold (to behold).

[Chorus]
Oh, the anatomy, let’s celebrate (let’s celebrate),
The wonders of the body, intricate and great (intricate and great).
From the vulva to the ovaries, all parts play (all parts play),
A vital role in life, every single day (every single day).

[Outro]
Respect and understanding, let's aim to show (aim to show),
The female genitalia, let our knowledge grow (knowledge grow).

songs.title: Brief Life
songs.tags: Melancholic powerpop kicks off with jangly, overdriven guitars and driving bass underlaid by punchy drums. The verses feature warm, layered guitar textures and subtle synth pads, then soar into a chorus with crisp harmonies and a powerful, melodic lead hook.
songs.prompt:
[Melancholy Verse 1]
In a tiny world on a thrips' egg they stay,
Adactylidium mites come out to play.
From a single egg, a larva springs to life,
Feasting from within, they thrive without strife.

[Powerpop Chorus]
Adactylidium, a rapid life's spree,
Born to live and die in their tiny sea.
Mating in shadows, within their mother's shell,
A brief, fleeting life, but they do it well.

[Melancholy Verse 2]
Inside the thrips' egg, they grow so fast,
Sisters and a brother, their bond will last.
A male to love them, right from the start,
Mating in darkness, with all their heart.

[Powerpop Chorus]
Adactylidium, a rapid life's spree,
Born to live and die in their tiny sea.
Mating in shadows, within their mother's shell,
A brief, fleeting life, but they do it well.

[Bridge]
Mother bursts open, it's time to go,
Sisters search for eggs, the cycle flows.
The male, his journey done, fades away,
In the world of Adactylidium, that's their way.

[Powerpop Chorus]
Adactylidium, a rapid life's spree,
Born to live and die in their tiny sea.
Mating in shadows, within their mother's shell,
A brief, fleeting life, but they do it well.

[Melodic Outro]
In the smallest places, life finds its way,
Adactylidium mites, live another day.
Their story brief, but oh so grand,
In the world of mites, they take their stand.

Adactylidium mites come out to play.

[Powerpop Chorus]

songs.caption: This song details the bizarre, rapid life cycle of Adactylidium mites. Larvae develop and mature entirely within their mother (an extreme form of adelphophagy). The single male born in the clutch mates with all his sisters while still inside. When the mother bursts, impregnated females leave to find a thrips egg to lay in, while the male typically dies. #Adactylidium #Mites #Parasitology #LifeCycle

songs.title: The Filovirus Thread (Ebola)
songs.tags: Opening with a pulsing deep synth and resonant cello, the track builds tension before sharp industrial drums and distorted guitars burst into the chorus. Percussive hits drive the intensity, supporting a soaring, near-shouted vocal. The bridge shifts to piano and cinematic strings, then escalates back into the anthemic rock energy.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1: Tense, Low Register, Acoustic Guitar]
A serpent thread, a tiny coil, within a lipid shell,
**Filoviridae**, on human soil, a story it will tell.
It hides and waits, ignites its flame, then crosses nature's line,
From bushmeat touched, or primal host, a silent, sudden sign.

[Chorus: Driving Rock Beat, Urgent Male Vocal]
**Ebola's** grip, it starts within, a fever takes its toll,
Each cell it claims, a deadly spin, it hollows out the soul.
Bleeding deep, from every pore, the contagion starts to spin,
It finds new life in love's embrace, by the virtue of **skin on skin**.
**(Through sweat, through tears, the contagion starts to spin)**

[Verse 2: Somber, Building Tension, Female Vocal]
The mother holds her ailing child, ignores the rising heat,
A husband's kiss, a gesture mild, makes destiny complete.
The nurse, gloved up, with weary eyes, fights fear with every breath,
For every touch, a sacrifice, a dance with imminent death.
They soothe the pain, they clean the sick, with courage shining bright,
But **proximity**, a deadly trick, in day and darkest night.

[Chorus: Powerful, Full Band, Urgent Male Vocal]
**Ebola's** grip, it starts within, a fever takes its toll,
Each cell it claims, a deadly spin, it hollows out the soul.
Bleeding deep, from every pore, the contagion starts to spin,
It finds new life in love's embrace, by the virtue of **skin on skin**.
**(Through sweat, through tears, the contagion starts to spin)**

[Bridge: Hopeful, Ascending Melody, Piano and Strings Emerge]
But minds awoke, and science fought, against the silent foe,
A shield of hope, profoundly wrought, for seeds of life to sow.
The needle's prick, a modern grace, a future we can claim,
**Vaccines** arise, a beacon clear, we whisper back its name.


[Guitar Solo: Energetic and Melodic, Sense of Breakthrough]
(Energetic and melodic electric guitar solo over driving drums)

[Outro: Fade Out, determined whispers]
We learn, we strive, we stand as one...
Against the unseen dread...
Ebola's reign... is done.
(Final determined cymbal crash and guitar riff fade)

songs.caption: This tense track describes the deadly Ebola virus (Filoviridae), which causes a hemorrhagic fever. It highlights the primary transmission route: direct skin-on-skin contact with bodily fluids, making caregivers and family members uniquely vulnerable. The song ends on the hope provided by vaccines, which offer a powerful shield against this devastating disease. #Ebola #Filovirus #Vaccines #PublicHealth #Virology

songs.title: Primordial Soup (Remastered)
songs.tags: Ambient electronic, 85 BPM, with deep tribal drums and bubbling synthesizer textures
songs.prompt:
[Intro - Atmospheric]
(Bubbling sounds, distant thunder)
[Verse 1 - etheral, mysterious voice, breathy whispers]
Lightning strikes the ancient sea
Carbon, hydrogen set free
Molecules in random dance
Waiting for their chemical chance
[Chorus - full voice]
In the depths of time untold
Where simple atoms turn to gold
Life's first whisper starts to stir
In this primordial mixture blur
[Verse 2 - Breathy whispers]
Amino acids falling down
Building blocks without a crown
Phospholipids align their way
As nucleotides start to sway
[Bridge - gradually increase power]
(Intensifying drums)
Heat and pressure, time and space
Each reaction finds its place
In the soup of destiny
Where consciousness will come to be
[Final Chorus - add harmonic layers]
From the chaos comes the code
Four billion years down evolution's road
It all begins in this dark sea
Where molecules learned to be free
[Outro]
(Fading heartbeat rhythm, bubbling sounds dissolve)

songs.title: Protein Synthesis Rap
songs.tags: Boom Bap Rap
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1] [Rap Verse]
Yo, it starts in the nucleus, DNA unwind,
Transcription begins, gotta read the line.
RNA polymerase, doin' its thing,
Makin' mRNA, let the message ring.

Nucleotides matchin', bases align,
Coppin' the code, in a perfect design.
From A to U and C to G,
mRNA's the key, now it's time to see.

[Chorus] [Catchy Hook]
Protein synthesis, it's the way to go,
From the DNA to the proteins that we know.
Ribosomes read it, amino acids grow,
In the cell's factory, watch it all flow.

[Verse 2] [Rap Verse]
mRNA leaves, through the nuclear gate,
Headin' to the cytoplasm, it won't wait.
Ribosomes bind, translation starts,
Reading every codon, playin' its part.

tRNA's bringin' amino acids in line,
Matchin' every codon, one at a time.
Peptide bonds form, chain's gettin' long,
Buildin' up the protein, makin' it strong.

[Chorus] [Catchy Hook]
Protein synthesis, it's the way to go,
From the DNA to the proteins that we know.
Ribosomes read it, amino acids grow,
In the cell's factory, watch it all flow.

[Bridge]
Elongation's rollin', chain keeps buildin',
Foldin' into shapes, structure fulfillin'.
Post-translational mods, give it some flair,
Now it's a protein, ready to repair.



[Outro] [Fade to End]
From genes to proteins, the cell's own hustle,
Protein synthesis, life's flexing muscle.

songs.title: Ten Percent
songs.tags: Kicking off with a propulsive acoustic guitar riff at 120bpm, layered by peppy percussion and crisp handclaps. Bright synth stabs fill the gaps between verses, adding vibrance. The energetic male vocal leads catchy melodies, while playful synth hooks spiral through each uplifting chorus.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
The sun pours down ten thousand kilocals
By the time it reaches you, most of it's gone
[Verse 1]
Photons hit the leaf, carbon gets fixed
Primary production, glucose in the mix
A grasshopper feeds, but ninety percent
Goes to heat and waste before it's spent
Respiration burns it, excretion takes its share
Undigested matter drifting through the air
[Pre-Chorus]
Every level up, the pyramid thins
But follow what's lost and that's where it begins
[Chorus]
Ten percent makes it to the next trophic floor
Ninety feeds the decomposers knocking at the door
What looks like waste is substrate, what looks like death is fuel
The parallel world beneath runs on a different rule
Ten percent — the tax that feeds the whole
Ten percent — the loss that makes us whole
[Verse 2]
Out in the open ocean, the pyramid inverts
Phytoplankton outrun the biomass that hurts
Doubling every day, consumed almost as fast
Standing stock is small but the energy flow is vast
Zooplankton accumulate, ectotherms run lean
Fifteen to twenty percent — the most efficient machine
[Pre-Chorus]
The snapshot lies, the flow tells the truth
Energy pyramids never invert — that's the proof
[Chorus]
Ten percent makes it to the next trophic floor
Ninety feeds the decomposers knocking at the door
What looks like waste is substrate, what looks like death is fuel
The parallel world beneath runs on a different rule
Ten percent — the tax that feeds the whole
Ten percent — the loss that makes us whole
[Bridge]
Marine snow is falling through the column deep
Bacteria intercept it before it goes to sleep
The microbial loop recaptures what the grazers couldn't hold
Two pathways running parallel, the story never told
Eighty percent of forest flow goes down into the ground
The decomposer chain is where the real abundance is found
[Chorus]
Ten percent makes it to the next trophic floor
Ninety feeds the decomposers knocking at the door
What looks like waste is substrate, what looks like death is fuel
The parallel world beneath runs on a different rule
Ten percent — the tax that feeds the whole
Ten percent — the loss that makes us whole
[Outro]
The sun pours down, the chain rolls on
Nothing is wasted, nothing is gone
Just transformed...

songs.title: Nineteen and One
songs.tags: Bright acoustic guitar lays the foundation with a persistent driving rhythm, joined by crisp percussion and gentle upright bass. Mandolin and fiddle add folk-Americana color between verses led by expressive female vocals. Dynamic builds and layered harmonies lift the upbeat choruses.
songs.prompt:
[Intro] (Acoustic guitar strumming, steady and driving) (Ooh-ooh)

[Verse 1] 
In a lab at Bryn Mawr, nineteen-and-five 
A woman kept history’s secrets alive 
While the world looked at weather or what a mother would eat 
Nettie Stevens was looking for something discrete 
She peered through the glass at the mealworm’s cell 
With a story that only the nucleus could tell

[Chorus] 
It wasn’t the heat and it wasn’t the tide 
It was written in threads on the inside 
She saw the ten large and she saw the one small 
The X and the Y, she deciphers it all (Give the credit to Nettie) 
For she saw the sign 
The fate of the sex on a chromosomal line

[Verse 2] 
She counted the pairs in the female’s frame 
Twenty large shadows, they all looked the same 
But she turned to the male and she spotted the truth 
A discovery hidden since science’s youth 
Nineteen were giants, but one was a stray 
A tiny companion we now call the Y

[Chorus] 
It wasn’t the heat and it wasn’t the tide 
It was written in threads on the inside 
She saw the ten large and she saw the one small 
The X and the Y, she deciphers it all (Give the credit to Nettie) 
For she saw the sign 
The fate of the sex on a chromosomal line

[Bridge] 
Now, E.B. Wilson was watching her lead 
He saw the same patterns, he followed her speed 
He had the prestige and he had the name (And history often gives mentors the fame) 
Then came T.H. Morgan with his fruit-fly parade 
Building his house on the bricks that she laid

[Guitar Solo] (Upbeat acoustic fingerstyle)

[Verse 3] 
He spread the word wide, he took it to heart 
But Nettie’s microscope was the true starting part 
She died in her prime, before prizes were won 
Leaving the world with the work she had done 
Yeah, she proved that the sperm holds the cargo that's cast 
She solved the great riddle, the die had been cast

[Outro] 
So remember the name of the woman who knew 
That the microscopic is what makes us true 
From the mealworm's egg to the human's breath 
She solved the great riddle before her own death (Nettie Stevens) 
She saw the X and the Y (Nettie Stevens) 
Before she said goodbye... 
[End]

songs.caption: "Nineteen and One": A folk tribute to Nettie Stevens, the unsung hero who discovered the X & Y chromosomes in 1905. History  overlooked her until about 1990, credited her discoveries to men like TH. Morgan and E.B. Wilson in biological textbooks; this song gives the credit back.

songs.title: Erection, Ejaculation, Fertilization (Dance of Creation v2) (Remastered)
songs.tags: Hip-Hop, Educational, Energetic, Male and Female Vocals
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Hip-Hop, Educational]
[SOUNDS LIKE: Schoolhouse Rock, Bill Nye the Science Guy]
[STYLE: Upbeat, Informative, Engaging]
[MOOD: Energetic, Fun, Enlightening]
[VOCALS: Male and Female, Dynamic, Conversational]
[ARRANGEMENT: Fast tempo, Catchy beat, Interactive delivery]

[DYNAMICS: High energy, Clear transitions, Engaging flow]
[EMOTIONS: Curiosity, Enthusiasm, Knowledgeable]

[Intro]
Yo, let's break it down, the biology sound,
From erection to fertilization, knowledge profound.
Listen up, kids, it's a natural flow,
[Female voice]The body's process, now let's go!

[Verse 1]
Blood flowin' down, to the cavernous zone,
Nerves get excited, yeah, the magic's on.
Nitric oxide’s the key, arteries expand,
[Female voice]Erection's here, it's nature's grand plan.

[Pre-Chorus]
Hormones in the mix, testosterone’s the lead,
Physical response, it's all guaranteed.

[Chorus]
Erection, ejaculation, fertilization,
The body's symphony in perfect coordination.
Nature’s design, no hesitation,
A journey of life in every generation.

[Verse 2]
Sperm produced in the testes, it's the start,
Travels through the ducts, ready to depart.
Seminal vesicles add the fluid blend,
[Female voice]Prostate gland's secretions, the mix they send.

[Pre-Chorus]
Muscles contract, the pressure's intense,
Ejaculation's here, it all makes sense.

[Chorus]
Erection, ejaculation, fertilization,
The body's symphony in perfect coordination.
Nature’s design, no hesitation,
[Female voice]A journey of life in every generation.

[Bridge - Fertilization]
Sperm meets egg, in the fallopian tube,
Fusion of the cells, a zygote in the groove.
DNA combines, a new life begins,
Fertilization’s magic, where it all spins.

[Pre-Chorus]

[Chorus]
Erection, ejaculation, fertilization,
The body's symphony in perfect coordination.
Nature’s design, no hesitation,
A journey of life in every generation.

[Outro]
So there you have it, the biology's clear,
From erection to life, it's a process dear.
Respect the science, and understand,
The wonders of life, it's all so grand.

[End]
Knowledge in your hands, now you know,
The biology of life, let it grow.

Embryo develops, life’s miracle call.

[Chorus]
Erection, ejaculation, fertilization,
The body's symphony in perfect coordination.
Nature’s design, no hesitation,
A journey of life in every generation.

[Outro]
So there you have it, the biology's clear,
From erection to life, it's a process dear.
Respect the science, and understand,
The wonders of life, it's all so grand.

[End]
Knowledge in your hands, now you know,
The biology of life, let it grow.

songs.title: Genital evolution of mammals (it's not just about penises and vaginas)
songs.tags: Folk, melodic, catchy, melodic interlude, female singer
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
From the primitive cloaca of our reptilian past
To specialized organs that evolved pretty fast
The genital tubercle's embryonic display
Shows how mammals found their reproductive way
[Verse 2]
S-R-Y gene kicks in, that's the Y chromosome's call
Testosterone surges, changes organs and all
While females develop through a different route
With estrogen guiding what it's all about
[Chorus]
It's not just about penises and vaginas
It's about how evolution redesigns us
From monotremes to marsupials to us
Each adaptation built on natural trust
[Verse 3]
Marsupials got their reproductive innovation
Two vaginas, one birth canal, quite a sensation
While placentals took a different evolutionary track
Single reproductive tract, no turning back
[Bridge]
Bats and dolphins, they've got their own design
Streamlined bodies left some parts behind
But the basic blueprint still remains the same
Since mammals first earned their evolutionary fame
[Verse 4]
From spiny echidnas to smooth human skin
Different solutions for getting genes in
Each species adapted in its special way
That's how natural selection saved the day
[Chorus 2]
It's not just about penises and vaginas
It's about survival's grand design for us
From egg-layers to a live birth, we evolved
Each puzzle piece of life getting solved

songs.title: Evolution (Opera)
songs.tags: Opera
songs.prompt:
[Instrumental]
[Verse 1]
From the depths of the ocean, to the sky so high above, we rise,
Life keeps on changing, reaching for the sky with unyielding might.
Through the sands of time, we crawl, we fly, we boldly strive,
Survival of the fittest, we never say die, we keep the flame alive.

[Pre-Chorus]
Stronger, faster, better, we rise to claim our place in time,
Feel the heartbeat of the change, in this wild dance, we are framed!

[Chorus]
Evolution, we're the champions of the night, breaking every boundary in sight,
Breaking boundaries, reaching new heights, we are the architects of light.
Evolution, we stand tall and fight, for every dream and every right,
In the story of life, we shine so bright, our spirit ignites the darkest night!

[Verse 2]
From the first spark of life, to the human race that stands today,
Every single creature, every single face, has its unique way.
Adapt and conquer, it's the way we roll through the eons long,
In the grand design, we all play a role, our legacy, a mighty song.

[Pre-Chorus]
[Chorus]
[Bridge]
Mutation, adaptation, our destiny unfolds through endless days,
In the web of life, we are the key, our paths a complex maze.
Generation after generation, we carry the flame with pride.
[Chorus]
[Outro]
So let's raise our voices, let the anthem soar through every land and shore,
In the journey of life, we'll explore, seeking truths and so much more.
Evolution, forevermore, our legacy will echo,
In the pulse of the earth, hear our roar, in the heartbeats that flow!

songs.caption: This song is an epic anthem celebrating the continuous, powerful process of evolution. It highlights key mechanisms like adaptation, mutation, and natural selection ("survival of the fittest") that have driven life's transformation from simple origins to the complex diversity we see today. The track emphasizes that every creature's existence is a testament to the success of this "grand design" across billions of years. #Evolution #Adaptation #NaturalSelection #Biology

songs.title: Permanent Puzzle
songs.tags: A burst of punchy drums and heavily distorted bass drives this energetic indie sleaze garage rock track. Gritty, overdriven guitars leap in for the verse, with sharp fills and shouted gang vocals. The middle eight strips back, before surging into a wild, frenetic final chorus.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]

Bonobo, chimp, gorilla swing free
Flat as a board unless a baby’s on the feed
But we left the trees, stood tall and proud
Something stayed round, something stayed loud
Two legs, big brain, new heat to lose
Yet these two curves still breaking the rules
Every other primate lets ’em disappear
We wired ’em permanent — crystal clear

[Pre-Chorus]
They drop when the milk’s gone, that’s the ape design
Ours never clock out, stay on overtime

[Chorus]
It’s a permanent puzzle, baby
An evolutionary maybe
Natural selection? Sexual selection?
Why we keep the weight when the rest deflate
Front-row signal, always on display
Concealed ovulation keeps the game in play
Yeah, it’s a permanent puzzle, baby
Only humans stay this way

[Verse 2]
Theory one: big brain runs expensive
Neurons eat calories like it’s comprehensive
So evolution parked a walking stash
Right up front in case the harvests crash
Famine hits hard, milk still runs rich
Built-in reserve when the tribe’s in a pinch
Visual proof that the baby won’t starve
Curve you can bank on, silhouette carved

[Verse 3]
Theory two: stand upright, view gets rearranged
Butt’s out of frame, whole game changed
Needed a billboard where the eyes now meet
Swelling that says “healthy, fertile, elite”
No monthly swell, no seasonal cue
Just 24/7 “I’m ready for you”
Face-to-face species needs face-to-face proof
And these stayed proud like living truth

[Bridge – slow and low]
Fat reserve… mating flare…
Hidden cycles, nothing compares
Only we keep the fire lit all year

[Final Chorus – huge]
It’s a permanent puzzle, baby
An evolutionary maybe
Natural selection? Sexual selection?
Why we keep the weight when the rest deflate
Front-row signal burning bright
Ten thousand nights of pure delight
Yeah, it’s a permanent puzzle, baby
Thank whatever god rolled those dice tonight

[Outro – whispered over fading beat]
Fat reserves… sexual selection…
Concealed ovulation… human obsession…
Just biology, baby.
And damn good biology at that

songs.title: Neuroscience of Love (Remastered)
songs.tags: Synth-pop, Neo-soul
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
There's a spark that's electric, a chemical light.

[Verse 1]
In the brain, it's a dance, dopamine in play,
With a rush and a glance, it can sweep us away.
Serotonin sings a song of serene,
While oxytocin binds us, unseen.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.

[Chorus]
Neuro-science of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.

[Verse 2]
In the limbic’s embrace, emotions intertwine,
Every kiss, every trace, in patterns we define.
With each memory stored, and each moment we share,
Our neurons explore, and love’s everywhere.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.



[Chorus]
Neuroscience of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.

[Bridge]
Chemical reactions, they pave the way,
In the maze of the mind, where our feelings stay.
With endorphins high, and the pathways clear,
In the neuroscience of love, we conquer fear.


[Outro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
Love's a journey of the brain, in the chemical light.

songs.title: Urea Blues V2
songs.tags: Jazz, blues, soul
songs.prompt:
[Smooth Jazz Intro]
*soft piano notes*

[Verse 1 - Sultry]
In the liver's midnight glow
Ammonia starts to flow
From protein's ancient ties
A tale of nitrogen highs

[Pre-chorus - Build]
(Smooth as silk) Enzymes align
(Nature's way) By design

[Chorus - Soulful]
Urea cycle, smooth as a jazz tune
In the body's quiet room
Turning waste to a sweet melody
Setting our cells free

[Verse 2 - Blues Style]
Ornithine's dance, citrulline's chance
In a cycle's timeless trance
With arginine's grace, it finds its place
Detoxing with elegant pace

[Bridge - Gospel Feel]
Oh, the enzymes know their cues
Arginase, how it soothes
From ammonia's restless wail
To urea's seamless trail

[Final Chorus - Full Power]
Urea cycle, smooth as a jazz tune
In the body's quiet room
Turning waste to a sweet melody
Setting our cells free

[Outro - Melancholic Jazz]
saxophone fade
So here's to the cycle, cool and fine
A bluesy beat in the body's line
With every step, it's clear to see
Biology's harmony
*gentle piano fade out*

songs.title: Eight-Legged Love Gift V2
songs.tags: Quirky indie pop with nature documentary vibes, female vocalist, playful pizzicato strings to mimic spider movement
songs.prompt:
[Whimsical Intro]
Tiny steps across the web...

[Verse 1]
Eight-legged Romeo on his way
Got a silk-wrapped present for his date today
Dancing on threads with delicate care
Hoping she notices his flair
He's got his gift held high in the air

[Pre-chorus]
But he's gotta be careful, oh so careful
'Cause love can be quite scareful
When you're just a tiny spider trying to survive

[Chorus]
Here's my gift, my precious gift
(Please don't eat me, please don't eat me)
Watch me dance and watch me shift
(Hope you'll greet me, not deplete me)
This is how we court and play
Spider romance on display
Eight legs of love coming your way

[Verse 2]
She watches him with all of her eyes
Judging his moves, his dance, his prize
Is it a fly wrapped up with care?
Or empty silk floating in air?
The stakes are high in this love affair

[Bridge]
The web is shaking, tension rising
Will she accept his love surprising?
(Some boys don't make it)
(Nature can be cruel)
But this gift looks promising...

[Chorus]
Here's my gift, my precious gift
(Please don't eat me, please don't eat me)
Watch me dance and watch me shift
(Hope you'll greet me, not deplete me)
This is how we court and play
Spider romance on display
Eight legs of love coming your way

[Outro]
And if she likes his offering sweet
Their genes will mingle, their hearts will meet
(A successful courtship, complete...)

songs.title: Journey of Change  (new variant)
songs.tags: This is an upbeat, high-energy rock anthem with a driving tempo of 140 BPM. The song is in the key of E minor. It features a male lead vocalist with a clear, powerful tenor voice, supported by a full rock band instrumentation including electric guitars, bass guitar, and drums. The electric guitars provide both rhythmic power chords and melodic lead lines, often with a distorted tone. The bass guitar lays down a solid, driving foundation, while the drums maintain a consistent, energetic beat with prominent snare and kick drum. The song structure follows a typical verse-chorus format, with a pre-chorus building anticipation. There are also instrumental breaks featuring guitar solos. Production elements include a wide stereo image, with guitars panned to the left and right, and a clear, punchy mix that emphasizes the drums and vocals. Reverb is used on the vocals and guitars to add depth. The melody is memorable and anthemic, designed for singalongs.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
(Instrumental)

[Verse 1]
[Male Vocal]
In the cradle of the ancient seas,
Life began its endless quest,
From the depths to the canopies,
Nature's grand and ultimate test.

[Pre-Chorus]
Through the ages, time unfolds,
A story of strength and endless molds.

[Chorus] [Harmonies] [Climactic]
Journey of change, we evolve and rise,
From the dawn of life to the endless skies,
Every step, a tale untold,
In the dance of time, we find our hold.

The strata speaks of giants past,
Of fire-forged mountains, high and grand,
A fragile world, built to last,
Shaped by the power of sea and land.
We carry the spark of the ancient flame,
In every breath, we recall the name.

Through the ages, time unfolds,
A story of strength and endless molds.

[Chorus, repeated twice] [Choir] [Climactic]
Journey of change, we evolve and rise,
From the dawn of life to the endless skies,
Every step, a tale untold,

In the dance of time, we find our hold.
