We carry the spark of the ancient flame,
In every breath, we recall the name.

[Break] [Instrumental Break] [Crescendo]
(Heavy, driving guitar riff and pounding drums build in volume and complexity.)

[Pre-Chorus]
Through the ages, time unfolds,
A story of strength and endless molds.

[Chorus] [Harmonies]
Journey of change, we evolve and rise,
From the dawn of life to the endless skies,
Every step, a tale untold,
In the dance of time, we find our hold.

[Guitar Solo] [Layered Arrangement]
(A soaring, virtuosic guitar solo over a dense, orchestral rock arrangement.)

[Hook] [Harmonies]
The ancient pulse beats on and on!
We rise from the dust until the dawn!

[Pre-Chorus]
Through the ages, time unfolds,
A story of strength and endless molds.

[Chorus, repeated twice] [Choir] [Climactic]
Journey of change, we evolve and rise,
From the dawn of life to the endless skies,
Every step, a tale untold,
In the dance of time, we find our hold.

Journey of change, we evolve and rise,
From the dawn of life to the endless skies,
Every step, a tale untold,
In the dance of time, we find our hold.

[Outro] [Powerful Outro] [Fade Out]
(Drums hit hard, guitars ring out and distort, followed by a final cymbal crash and long reverb trail, fading to silence.)
songs.caption: This powerful track is an anthem about the colossal scope of Evolution and Earth history. It combines the biological journey—from the ancient seas to complex life—with the geological forces that have shaped our world, referencing the creation of mountains and the survival of species through vast ages. It celebrates the constant adaptation and transformation inherent in life, emphasizing that the "ancient pulse" of change is carried forward in every living thing. #Evolution #EarthScience #Geology

songs.title: Carbon Fixation
songs.tags: Indie electronic with pulsing synths and playful rhythms. Classical science-inspired melodies with modern production - gentle, yet driven by the rhythm of biological processes.
songs.prompt:
VERSE 1:
In the garden of leaves where the sunlight falls
A molecular miracle starts to unfold
A protein complex with a singular goal
To capture the carbon that keeps us all whole
RuBisCO waiting, patient and slow
The most abundant protein the world will ever know

CHORUS:
Carbon fixation, silent and sweet
Turning light to sugar, an ancient feat
The breath of the planet in enzyme form
RuBisCO working since life was born

VERSE 2:
Billions of years of evolution refined
Still inefficient, but keeping us alive
Carbon dioxide locked in its grasp
Converting to glucose with every clasp
Photorespiration, its fatal flaw
Mistaking oxygen in nature's imperfect law

(CHORUS)

BRIDGE:
Eight large subunits, eight small
A symphony of atoms in perfect protocol
In chloroplasts of green where photons dance
RuBisCO gives life its second chance
The Calvin cycle turns like clockwork wheels
A billion trillion times while the planet reels

VERSE 3:
Scientists dream of improvements to make
Redesigning what evolution couldn't fix
Feeding our future with engineered grace
As our carbon rises at alarming rates
Ancient enzyme holding our fate
The bridge between worlds inorganic and great

(CHORUS)

OUTRO:
Carbon fixation, cycle divine
RuBisCO turning chaos to design
Carbon fixation, cycle divine
RuBisCO turning chaos to design

songs.title: Whale's Gift
songs.tags: Soft Female voice, Ethereal ambient pop with whale song influences, deep bass swells, and flowing melodic lines. Inspired by marine documentary soundtracks with a modern indie edge.
songs.prompt:
From the depths they rise and dive
Gentle giants keeping oceans alive
Iron-rich nutrients in their wake
Feeding cycles they help to make
Through the thermocline they glide with grace

Phytoplankton bloom and grow
In the currents down below
Where whales dance and play

They fertilize the seas
With every deep-sea breeze
Nature's gardeners of the ocean blue
Converting nitrogen into life anew
The whale pump keeps turning, keeps turning

Diving deep into midnight zones
Rising up through water columns they roam
Their waste feeds microscopic stars
Plankton forests near and far
Creating oxygen for us all to breathe

Marine snow begins to fall
Nutrients feeding one and all
In this dance of life

They fertilize the seas
With every deep-sea breeze
Nature's gardeners of the ocean blue
Converting nitrogen into life anew
The whale pump keeps turning, keeps turning

Circle of life in marine snow
From the depths where few dare to go
Giants sustaining the tiniest things
In this perfect harmony

Life flows on in endless seas
Thanks to gentle leviathans beneath
The whale pump keeps turning
Keeps turning...

songs.title: Six Legs to Succes
songs.tags: Educational Pop Rock with playful synth elements,featuring clear enunciation for learning purposes. Upbeat tempo with quirky instrumental breaks to maintain engagement.
songs.prompt:
[Soft Instrumental Intro]
[buzzing sounds] Bzzzzz, bzzzzz 
[wing flutters] Flp-flp-flp
[Verse 1]
Three body segments, that's where we'll start
Head, thorax, abdomen - each plays its part
Exoskeleton shields them day and night
Chitin armor, keeps them strong and tight
[Pre-chorus]
And when you look closer, there's so much more
Let me show you what each part is for
[Chorus]
Six legs for walking (That's what makes an insect)
Two antennae sensing (Everything they need to know)
Compound eyes seeing (Thousands of small lenses)
That's the insect way to go!
[Verse 2]
On their head you'll find complex compound eyes
Mandibles for eating, they're built so wise
Simple eyes called ocelli, usually three
Help them track the sunlight perfectly
[Bridge]
(Spoken with cricket chirps)
Some have wings, one pair or two
Forewings, hindwings, helping them move through
The air with grace, would you believe?
Some can fold them like origami
[Verse 3]
Thorax bears the legs and sometimes wings
Muscles power all their moving things
Spiracles for breathing line their side
Tracheal tubes where oxygen resides
[Pre-chorus]
Now let's dive deeper, there's more to explore
The abdomen's secrets behind nature's door
[Chorus]
Six legs for walking (That's what makes an insect)
Two antennae sensing (Everything they need to know)
Compound eyes seeing (Thousands of small lenses)
That's the insect way to go!
[Bridge 2]
[Melodic Interlude]
*wing flutters gentle buzzing*
[Final Verse]
Abdomen holds organs tucked inside
Digestive tract and heart reside
Some have stingers, others have more
Like ovipositors near the core
[Outro]
[Gentle Fade]
Three segments working in harmony
That's insect anatomy
(That's insect anatomy...)
*soft buzzing fades*

songs.title: Evolutions Journey
songs.tags: progressive rock
songs.prompt:
Title: Evolution's Journey

Intro:
In nature's grand design, where mysteries unfold,
Evolution's tale, in whispers bold.

Verse 1: Mutations
From tiny mutations, a world can change,
DNA's dance, in a beautiful range.
A single twist, a new path to explore,
In life's great book, a new chapter in store.

Chorus:
Evolution's journey, a story untold,
Through mutations and changes, life takes hold.
From simple starts to complex arrays,
Nature's masterpiece, in wondrous displays.

Verse 2: Gene Duplications
Genes double up, crafting new roles,
In life's orchestra, they play vital roles.
New functions arise, in this genetic spree,
Fueling diversity, as far as the eye can see.

Chorus:
Evolution's journey, a story untold,
Through mutations and changes, life takes hold.
From simple starts to complex arrays,
Nature's masterpiece, in wondrous displays.

Bridge: Evo-Devo and Endosymbiosis
Evo-devo guides the forms we see,
From embryo to the tallest tree.
Endosymbiosis, where life entwines,
Cells within cells, creating new designs.

Verse 3: Unified Harmony
From ocean depths to skies above,
Evolution's path, a tale of love.
Mechanisms of change, in harmony,
Crafting the tapestry of life's symphony.

Outro:
In every heartbeat, in every breath,
The story of evolution, defying death.

songs.title: The Quantum Green (Photosynthesis Song)
songs.tags: A driving pop/rock track bursts open with bright electric guitars, snappy drums, and a perky bassline. Verses use syncopated claps, swirling synths, and animated keys to accentuate scientific themes. Choruses explode with layered harmonies and melodic lead guitar, keeping energy high and playful.
songs.prompt:
[Intro: Instrumental]
(electric guitar, snappy drums, perky bassline]

[Verse 1: Male Vocal]
In the thylakoid membrane deep, where the light-harvesting pigments sleep,
A photon hits, and that's the start, of the Photo Part, the art of the heart.
It's P680, the special pair, catching sunshine from the air.
They kick an electron, high-energy state, splitting H2O to seal its fate.
Oxygen released, a cosmic sigh, as the E-T-C train goes marching by.

[Chorus, male vocals]
Oh, the Quantum Green, it's more than you see, a wave function probability!
No drunken sailor's random hop, but a coherent search that won't stop.
The exciton's delocalized, a wavelike plea, to find the Reaction Center with near-unity!
Then Tunneling helps the charge fly, through a barrier that chemistry would deny.
It's the weird mechanics, small and fast, making the sun's great energy last!

[Verse 2: male vocal]
Through plastoquinone and Cyt b6f, the electron falls, losing its breath.
But energy lost is energy won, as protons are pumped, the work is begun.
A gradient builds, a charge disparity, driving the rotor of ATP clarity.
The mighty ATP Synthase spins around, making the cell's main currency sound!
Electron re-boosted at P700's light, to make the powerful N-A-D-P-H—the reducing might.

[Chorus: male vocals]
Oh, the Quantum Green, it's more than you see, a wave function probability!
No drunken sailor's random hop, but a coherent search that won't stop.
The exciton's delocalized, a wavelike plea, to find the Reaction Center with near-unity!
Then Tunneling helps the charge fly, through a barrier that chemistry would deny.
It's the weird mechanics, small and fast, making the sun's great energy last!

[Bridge: Male vocals]
Now A-T-P and N-A-D-P-H leave the scene, heading to the Stroma, where the synthesis is keen.
The Calvin Cycle starts its flow, to fix the CO2, and help the plant grow.
RuBisCO's the enzyme, slow and vast, adding carbon to Ru-B-P to make the cycle fast.
It takes a lot of energy, nine A-T-P and six N-A-D-P-H so fine, to make one G-3-P that's worth the climb!

[Outro: male vocal]
From coherence to sugar, in light and in dark,
The green machines leave their quantum mark.
Hey! A-D-P and N-A-D-P-plus are back! Ready to ride the quantum track!

songs.caption: This song details the Light-Dependent Reactions of photosynthesis. It explains how Photosystem II (P680) splits water, launching an electron through the ETC to power ATP Synthase. Crucially, the song highlights Quantum Biology: the initial energy (exciton) transfer is a coherent wave function, ensuring near-perfect efficiency. The products, ATP and NADPH, then power the Calvin Cycle (carbon fixation). #QuantumBiology #Photosynthesis #ATP #CalvinCycle #Biochemistry

songs.title: The Hitchhiker's Voyage
songs.tags: Acoustic guitar and warm upright bass anchor the folk ballad, joined by brushed snare and delicate strings. Verses flow narratively with call-and-response harmonies, transitioning to a pop-influenced chorus featuring glockenspiel, layered voices, and crisp claps for an inviting, educational tone.
songs.prompt:
(Intro - Plucked acoustic guitar, a little mysterious)
(Verse 1) 
In the 1890s, the hulls were made of steel, 
We ditched the solid ballast, the rocks and scrap we’d wheel. 
We’d just pump in the ocean, a brilliant, fast solution, 
Never guessed we’d started a biological pollution. 
A sip of water from the port, to keep the ship in trim, 
No one saw the danger, invisible and grim.

(Chorus) 
Oh, the hitchhiker's voyage, on a dark and watery road, 
A biological time bomb, a terrible payload. 
A million tiny larvae, a microscopic soup, 
We never saw them coming, 'til the ecosystem drooped. 
The story of the ballast, a warning, a tale, 
When a foreign army sets its silent, deadly sail.

(Verse 2) 
For a hundred years it happened, beneath our very eyes, 
Until the day in '88, we got a big surprise. 
In the North American Great Lakes, a tiny mussel found, 
The Zebra Mussel army was on new and fertile ground. 
It came from the Black Sea, in a single ship's-load, 
It clogged the city water pipes, and all the rivers flowed... 
With billions of invaders, the native species fell, 
That "brilliant" solution had become a living hell.

(Chorus) 
Oh, the hitchhiker's voyage, on a dark and watery road, 
A biological time bomb, a terrible payload. 
A million tiny larvae, a microscopic soup, 
We never saw them coming, 'til the ecosystem drooped. 
The story of the ballast, a warning, a tale, 
When a foreign army sets its silent, deadly sail.

(Verse 3) 
The Black Sea had its own plague, a story just as grim, 
The American Comb Jelly, on eggs and larvae slim. 
It ate the entire food web, the anchovies were gone, 
The European Green Crab was on the Western dawn. 
From Frisco to Australia, the damage was so clear, 
The problem we'd ignored was the one we had to fear.

(Bridge) 
We finally had the "smoking gun," the proof was plain to see, 
The world woke up and shouted, "This cannot be!" 
But the ships sail every ocean, the problem's global-sized, 
You can't just build a single wall, the world realized. 
It took the IMO, the nations gathered 'round, 
To fight the silent enemy that never made a sound.

(Verse 4) 
In 2004, a treaty's text was signed, 
The BWM Convention, for all of humankind. 
But it took twelve years of waiting, 'til 2017, 
For the law to finally kick in, and change the whole routine. 
Now every single modern ship has filters and a light, 
A powerful UV sterilizer shining in the night. 
It zaps the tiny aliens and stops them in their track, 
'Cause once they're in, there is no turning back.

(Chorus) 
Oh, the hitchhiker's voyage, on a dark and watery road, 
A biological time bomb, a terrible payload. 
A million tiny larvae, a microscopic soup, 
We never saw them coming, 'til the ecosystem drooped. 
The story of the ballast, a warning, a tale, 
When a foreign army sets its silent, deadly sail.

(Outro) 
The water's clean, the coast is clear, the hitchhikers are gone. 
A lesson learned the hardest way, from dusk until the dawn. 
Acoustic guitar fades out)

songs.title: The Sunspot Archivist (Remastered)
songs.tags: Acoustic Folk style opens with an intimate fingerpicked guitar, later joined by subtle piano accents and sparse upright bass. The storyteller’s warm, clear vocal sits front and center. Light brush percussion and gentle harmonies enrich the chorus, highlighting the honest, timeless mood.
songs.prompt:
(Verse 1)
In a quiet Norwegian attic, where the dust had a long reign,
Sat a thousand glass-plate negatives, protected from the sun and the rain.
From '54 to '86, a silent, steady gaze,
Captured sunspots dancing through a thousand golden days.
They were ghosts of light and knowledge, tucked away from view,
Waiting for a patient hand to see them through.

(Chorus)
Oh, what a tale the sun would tell,
Of a dedicated hand, breaking the silent spell.
Saving history, one image at a time,
A sunspot's story, rescued from the crime
Of being forgotten, a treasure in the dark,
Now just a funding away from leaving its digital mark.

(Verse 2)
With a brush and gentle cleanser, he worked with careful grace,
Wiping scratches, smudges from each historical face.
A painter’s patience, a scientist’s soul,
Making a broken puzzle feel completely whole.
He saved the solar cycle, the flares and the quiet times,
Forgetting all the money, and the nickel-and-dime crimes.

(Chorus)
Oh, what a tale the sun would tell,
Of a dedicated hand, breaking the silent spell.
Saving history, one image at a time,
A sunspot's story, rescued from the crime
Of being forgotten, a treasure in the dark,
Now just a funding away from leaving its digital mark.

(Bridge)
He used his own money when the grant ran out, you see,
A simple act of love for what the future ought to be.
A sunspot's silent sigh, a scientist's old dream,
Now waiting for a signal, from a philanthropic stream.
To bring them to the public, for all the world to find,
The passion of a sunspot archivist for all of mankind.

(Outro)
Oh, the sun still burns and the sun still shines,
But now its past is preserved in digital lines.
So lend a hand, let the story be known,
For a harvest of light that he saved all alone.
Yeah, he saved all alone...

Of being forgotten, a treasure in the dark,

(Verse 2)
With a brush and gentle cleanser, he worked with careful grace,
Wiping scratches, smudges from each historical face.
A painter’s patience, a scientist’s soul,
Making a broken puzzle feel completely whole.
He saved the solar cycle, the flares and the quiet times,
Forgetting all the money, and the nickel-and-dime crimes.

(Chorus)
Oh, what a tale the sun would tell,
Of a dedicated hand, breaking the silent spell.
Saving history, one image at a time,
A sunspot's story, rescued from the crime
Of being forgotten, a treasure in the dark,
Now just a funding away from leaving its digital mark.

(Bridge)
He used his own money when the grant ran out, you see,
A simple act of love for what the future ought to be.
A sunspot's silent sigh, a scientist's old dream,
Now waiting for a signal, from a philanthropic stream.
To bring them to the public, for all the world to find,
The passion of a sunspot archivist for all of mankind.

(Outro)
Oh, the sun still burns and the sun still shines,
But now its past is preserved in digital lines.
So lend a hand, let the story be known,
For a harvest of light that he saved all alone.
Yeah, he saved all alone...

songs.title: The Insect Timeline (Remastered)
songs.tags: Dark indie rock with elements of progressive metal, haunting female vocals, scientific narration breaks, Include gentle buzzing sounds, microscope clicks, and specimen jar sounds in the backing track,
songs.prompt:
[Instrumental Intro]
[Spoken Verse]
Through compound eyes we see the truth
In death's garden, life takes root
First wave settlers tell their tale
Time of death? They cannot fail
[Pre-chorus]
Blowflies sense decay
Within the hour, they make their way
Laying eggs in precise locations
Nature's clock in operation
[Dark Chorus]
The insect timeline never lies
Successive waves before our eyes
Each species has its time to show
What happened twenty days ago
What the body needs to show
[Verse]
Colonizers stage by stage
Writing death upon this page
Calliphoridae leads the way
Then dermestids have their say
Temperature, humidity, seasonal arrays
[Bridge]
[Spoken Bridge]
Pupae cases tell the story
Larval instars mark the phases
Succession patterns paint the picture
Of time's passage, leaving traces
[Powerful Chorus]
The insect timeline never lies
Successive waves before our eyes
Each species has its time to show
What happened twenty days ago
What the body needs to show
[Outro]
[Whispered]
Through metamorphosis we learn
When life's final page did turn
Evidence wings its way to light
Nature's clock keeps time just right

[Pre-chorus]

songs.caption: This song details Forensic Entomology, the science using the insect timeline to estimate the Post-Mortem Interval (PMI). Blowflies (Calliphoridae) are the first on the scene, colonizing a body in predictable successive waves. Their larval stages and pupae are crucial for dating, as development is precisely tracked based on environmental factors like temperature. #ForensicEntomology #TimeOfDeath #Decomposition

songs.title: Networked Mind
songs.tags: Electronic ambient fusion with organic percussion elements. Flowing synthesizer patterns that gradually interconnect and build complexity, mirroring the slime mold's network development.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
No neurons firing, no brain to guide
Just simple cells with nothing to hide
Yet somehow solving puzzles with ease
Finding the patterns humans can't see
Yellow tendrils reaching through the dark
Mapping solutions, leaving their mark

[Chorus]
Networked mind, collective intelligence
Flowing through the pathways of least resistance
No thoughts, no plans, just elegant design
The wisdom of the ancient slime

[Verse 2]
Tokyo subway, they recreated
Optimal patterns, underestimated
A single cell with multiple nuclei
Distributed logic, biological taxi
Memory without neurons
Learning without lessons

[Chorus]
Networked mind, collective intelligence
Flowing through the pathways of least resistance
No thoughts, no plans, just elegant design
The wisdom of the ancient slime

[Bridge]
Four hundred million years of silent evolution
Solving problems without a revolution
What secrets hide in your primitive form?
Intelligence that breaks the norm

[Final Chorus]
Networked mind, collective intelligence
Flowing through the pathways of least resistance
No thoughts, no plans, just elegant design
The wisdom of the ancient slime
The wisdom hiding in the slime

songs.title: Stages of Unbecoming (Remastered)
songs.tags: Educational, Progressive dark ambient, melodic
songs.prompt:
[VERSE I] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the FRESH stage

[Female singer]
Stillness descends like a veil
As the final breath exhales
The clock has stopped, yet time moves on
Chemical whispers spread the news
First responders take their cues
The blowflies circle, sensing change
Drawn to secrets rearranged

[VERSE II] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the BLOATED stage

[Female singer]
Beneath the skin, a storm arises
Microscopic enterprises
Gases build and tissues stretch
The temple swells with hidden force
Life and death change intercourse
A grotesque cradle now appears
Nursery of the pioneers

[VERSE III] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the ACTIVE DECAY stage

[Female singer]
The fortress falls, the gates give way
Legions feeding night and day
A writhing blanket, white and blind
Consuming what was left behind
The heat of countless hungry mouths
Transforms the host to feeding grounds
While predators hunt those who feast
The hunters becoming the hunted beasts

[VERSE IV] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the ADVANCED DECAY stage

[Female singer]
The crowd disperses, work half-done
The loudest voices now are gone
What remains draws different eyes
Specialized and patient tribes
The slower craftsmen take their turn
On remnants others would not learn
To value or to recognize
As worthy of their enterprise

[VERSE V] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the DRY REMAINS stage

[Female singer]
Now only whispers of what was
Bone and hair and final dust
The specialists complete the task
With microscopic tools that last
Through time's persistent, patient hand
Returning all unto the land
The cycle ends where it begins
The fallen rise through life again

songs.title: Dance of the Bees
songs.tags: The folk-pop song opens with fingerpicked acoustic guitar and agile light percussion, creating a buoyant, playful groove. Violin weaves bright melodic lines, while a gentle buzzing synth adds an inventive, organic texture. Instrumentation layers build subtly, keeping the mood upbeat and lively.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]  
In the golden hive where the sunlight streams,  
A tiny dancer shares her dreams.  
With a wiggle and a turn on the honeycomb floor,  
She tells her sisters where nectar’s stored.  

[Chorus]  
Waggle to the left, waggle to the right,  
Follow the angle, follow the light.  
Circle back around, try it once again,  
The dance of the bees will show you where to spin.  

[Verse 2]  
Short little wiggle means the food is near,  
A longer run says it’s farther, dear.  
The angle of the dance points out the way,  
To flowers in the field on a summer’s day.  

[Chorus]  
Waggle to the left, waggle to the right,  
Follow the angle, follow the light.  
Circle back around, try it once again,  
The dance of the bees will show you where to spin.  

[Bridge]  
Buzzing and humming, sisters take flight,  
Guided by the sun in the warm daylight.  
The hive comes alive with a sweet refrain,  
Nature’s own compass in a waggle chain.  

[Final Chorus]  
Waggle to the left, waggle to the right,  
Follow the angle, follow the light.  
Circle back around, feel the buzzing sound,  
The wiggle dance of bees makes the world go round.

songs.title: The Four Rooms of Mind
songs.tags: Progressive rock with jazz undertones. Dynamic shifts between sections represent states of knowledge. Atmospheric synths, clean guitars, and contemplative vocals.
songs.prompt:
[Atmospheric Intro]
*swirling synths*
(Walking through the halls of mind)

[Verse 1 - Known Knowns]
[Confident]
Here's what I know I know
Like numbers in a row
Facts lined up on my shelf
Truths I tell myself
Clear as crystal, bright as day
These are things I know to say

[Pre-chorus 1]
But step into the mist
There's more that exists

[Verse 2 - Known Unknowns]
[Thoughtful]
Here's what I know I don't
Questions that I own
Mysteries I see
Waiting to be free
At least I know they're there
These gaps in what I share

[Pre-chorus 2]
[Building Tension]
But deeper still we go
To what we don't know we don't know

[Verse 3 - Unknown Unknowns]
[Dark and Mysterious]
Blind spots in the dark
Questions with no mark
How can we perceive
What we can't conceive?
Dancing with the void
Knowledge yet unveiled

[Bridge]
[Jazz-Influenced]
But wait, there's one more door
One we often ignore
A treasure chest of mind
Memories left behind

[Verse 4 - Unknown Knowns]
[Ethereal]
Skills we never learned
Wisdom never earned
Knowledge hiding there
In muscles, bones, and air
Things we've always known
But never thought to show

[Chorus]
[Complex]
Four rooms in my mind
Some clear, some blind
Known knowns light the way
Known unknowns make me stay
Unknown unknowns teach me grace
But unknown knowns hide their face
(Hide their face, show their grace)

[Outro]
[Contemplative Fade]
In these four rooms we dwell
Each with tales to tell
Some we know we know
Some we'll never show
Some we know we don't
Some we can't yet own
And some we've always known
But haven't yet been shown...
*fade to gentle piano*

songs.title: Proton Gradient (Remastered)
songs.tags: Progressive Rock with ambient electronic elements, building layers of complexity like biochemical pathways
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
Where the deep vents rise
Ancient chimneys to the skies
Alkaline meets acid sea
Setting proton gradients free
[Verse 1]
In the labyrinth of pores
Where temperature and pH scores
Create the power to begin
As protons flow within
Natural gradients emerge
Through mineral walls they surge
Nature's first battery alive
Helping molecules survive
[Pre-chorus 1]
Not just random chemistry
But structured energy
Flows that organize
As first life starts to rise
[Chorus]
PROTON GRADIENTS FLOW!
(Living power)
MEMBRANES START TO GROW!
(Building higher)
This is how we learned to be
Converting energy
[Verse 2]
Iron-sulfur clusters form
Creating pathways to transform
C-O-2 to building blocks
As metabolism unlocks
Simple cells still bear the signs
Of these ancient carbon lines
Universally conserved
From the path life first observed
[Bridge]
One ancient symbiosis
Changed everything we know
Mitochondria united
Teaching cells to grow
Now every complex creature
Shares this common core
The power plant within us
From three billion years before
[Chorus 2]
ENERGY SETS US FREE!
(Driving forward)
COMPLEXITY DEGREE BY DEGREE!
(Growing stronger)
From deep sea to you and me
Through shared biochemistry
[Final Verse]
Life's not just about the code
But energetic roads
That power every living thing
From the smallest cells we bring
The story of our past
In each proton path that's cast
[Outro - spoken]
[Spoken deep male voice]
In gradients we trust
The force that built all of us

songs.title: Nature's Logic Gates
songs.tags: Progressive electronic with hints of ambient science-documentary style background, featuring clear vocals.  contemplative synth patterns and rhythmic percussion mimicking biological processes.
songs.prompt:
[Atmospheric Intro]
*cellular sounds heartbeat rhythm*
[Verse 1]
DNA reads like binary code
If-then statements in cellular mode
When glucose drops, the switches flip
Enzymes activate, take another sip
Of energy from a different source
That's biological logic on its course
[Pre-chorus]
It's not just random
There's method in the madness
Each cell's got a program
Running with exactness
[Chorus]
Biological thinking
(Nature's computation)
Every cell is linking
(Perfect automation)
Input to output, decision trees grow
That's how all living systems flow
[Verse 2]
Immune system's got its logic gates
If pathogen, then activate
B cells multiply, antibodies rise
T cells mobilize, recognize
Pattern matching in your blood
Defense systems understood
[Bridge]
(Spoken with neural firing sounds)
Neural networks in the brain
Processing like computer chains
Each synapse a logic gate
Thinking at lightning rates
[Verse 3]
Evolution's algorithm plays
Testing fitness through the days
If environment changes, then adapt
Or species chances might collapse
Natural selection's Boolean test
Survive or fail, nature knows best
[Pre-chorus 2]
From genes to neurons
From cells to populations
Nature's patterns turn
To smart computations
[Chorus]
Biological thinking
(Nature's computation)
Every cell is linking
(Perfect automation)
Input to output, decision trees grow
That's how all living systems flow
[Bridge 2]
[Electronic Interlude]
*cellular division sounds*
DNA replication patterns
[Verse 4]
Plant roots solve complex equations
Finding water through soil nations
Slime molds map efficient routes
Without a brain to figure out
Nature's logic finds a way
To optimize each passing day
[Outro]
[Gradual Build]
From molecules to ecosystems whole
Logic gates in every living soul
(Nature's computations never cease...)
*fading heartbeat*
*cellular sounds dissolve*

songs.title: What the internet is for
songs.tags: A punchy hip hop beat built on crisp drums, deep 808 bass, and layered chopped vocal samples. The verses flow over minimal instrumentation—tight hi-hats, sparse synth stabs—while the hook adds melodic keys or brass for impact. The track ends with a dynamic instrumental breakdown.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
The web's a wondrous place, for knowledge and for fun,
A playground for the curious, there's something for everyone,
But there's one thing that we all know, though some might call it wrong,
Let's all admit what brings us here, and sing this little song:

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, come on, let's not be coy,
We browse and click and stream away, like kids with a new toy,
From morning until night, it's our guilty little pleasure,
The internet is for porn, oh what a hidden treasure!

[Verse 2]
You might use it for emails, or sharing cute cat memes,
You might shop for clothes or plan your big vacation dreams,
But deep down in the browser, where history gets erased,
We all know what you're clicking on, that look upon your face!

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, let's drop the pretense now,
For every kitten video, there’s a thousand "holy cow!"
We laugh and shake our heads, but we all know the score,
The internet is for porn, it’s what we all adore!

[Bridge]
Now don't you feel ashamed, it’s just the way things go,
In this vast cyber jungle, it’s the one thing we all know,
From Oslo to New York, from Sydney to Milan,
The internet unites us, in the palm of our own hand!

[Chorus]
The internet is for porn, a fact we can't deny,
We wink and nod and carry on, as the bits and bytes go by,
So let’s just have a laugh, and sing it loud and clear,
The internet is for porn, it’s why we all are here!

[Outro]
So next time that you log on, just know you’re not alone,
In this vast digital playground, it’s the cornerstone,
From teachers to the bankers, from students to the moms,
The internet is for porn, it’s the world’s most open secret and bond!

songs.tags: Progressive Rock
songs.prompt:

(Instrumental - sweeping guitar riffs, steady drumbeat)

[Verse 1]
In the cradle of the ancient seas,
Life began its endless quest,
From the depths to the canopies,
Nature's grand and ultimate test.

[Pre-Chorus]
Through the ages, time unfolds,

songs.title: Animal Attraction Rap
songs.tags: Conscious rap, 80-100 BPM, mellow beat, strong bass, light percussion, piano/strings, reflective, smooth transitions
songs.prompt:
Verse 1
Yo, let's dive in the wild where the creatures thrive,
Mating practices diverse, every species got their vibe.
From the jungle to the ocean, from the sky to the ground,
Animals got their game, it's amazing and profound.

Peacocks strut their stuff, with feathers so grand,
Males flashin' colors, tryin' to take a stand.
While seahorses twist it, the males carry the load,
Birthin' baby seahorses, in their unique mode.

Chorus
Diversity, diversity, in the animal kingdom,
Mating styles so varied, every species got wisdom.
From rituals to dances, and colors that gleam,
Nature's got its ways, it's a beautiful dream.

Verse 2
Birds of paradise, man, they got that flair,
Dancin' in the trees, puttin' on a show up there.
Penguins got romance, with a rock for a gift,
Searchin' for a mate, it's a natural drift.

Spider seduction, webs are where it's at,
Males approach with caution, or they might end flat.
Elephants got patience, courtship in slow motion,
Touchin' trunks so gently, showin' love and devotion.

Chorus
Diversity, diversity, in the animal kingdom,
Mating styles so varied, every species got wisdom.
From rituals to dances, and colors that gleam,
Nature's got its ways, it's a beautiful dream.

Bridge
Bees buzzin' around, a queen to impress,
Drones on the mission, givin' it their best.
Lions fight for pride, only strongest can breed,
In the circle of life, it's a powerful creed.

Verse 3
Frogs croakin' loud, serenades in the night,
Calls to attract, in the moonlight.
Octopus romances, with arms intertwined,
Deep under the sea, where the love's confined.

Each species unique, with a story to tell,
In the grand scheme of life, they all fit so well.
From complex rituals to simple, pure love,
It's a testament to nature and the power thereof.

Chorus
Diversity, diversity, in the animal kingdom,
Mating styles so varied, every species got wisdom.
From rituals to dances, and colors that gleam,
Nature's got its ways, it's a beautiful dream.



Verse 4
Now let's talk 'bout humans, we're part of this too,
From ancient tribes to modern cities, love's what we pursue.
Cultures and customs, so many ways to connect,
With hearts intertwined, our bonds reflect.

Some seek true love, while others play the field,
In the dance of romance, emotions revealed.
Online connections, long-distance ties,
Through thick and thin, under various skies.

Outro
So let's celebrate life, in all its forms and hues,
From the tiniest insect to the massive kangaroos.
Mating in the wild, it's a sight to behold,
A dance of evolution, with stories untold.

songs.title: Dicrocoelium: The Lancet Fluke's Tale
songs.tags: Educational  rap, beat-heavy
songs.prompt:
VERSE 1:
Microscopic traveler, born in murky streams
Dicrocoelium dendriticum, more ancient than dreams
Swimming free with my forked tail, a microscopic knight
Cutting through the water like a phantom in the night
First host conquered easily, inside the snail I grow
Transforming into cercaria, ready for the show

CHORUS:
From water to snail to ant to sheep
Dicrocoelium's journey, cunningly deep
I'm the lancet fluke, nature's grand design
Generations of parasites stand in my line
(Survival through the ages, my legacy divine)

VERSE 2:
Expelled from the snail now, seeking my next stage
The unsuspecting Formica ant becomes my second cage
I seize control of ganglia, rewire the brain
Force it to climb grass blades again and again
Locked in jaws at sunset, waiting patiently
For grazing sheep to come and unknowingly free me

CHORUS:
From water to snail to ant to sheep
Dicrocoelium's journey, cunningly deep
I'm the lancet fluke, nature's grand design
Generations of parasites stand in my line
(Survival through the ages, my legacy divine)

BRIDGE:
Dicrocoelium dendriticum, my scientific name
Mind-altering maestro of evolutionary fame
I turn the ant's existence into a waking dream
Climbing every evening as the sun begins to set
A sacrificial offering with no hint of regret
Three hosts I commandeer in my relentless quest
A parasite's journey that nature deemed the best

VERSE 3:
Inside the sheep's bile ducts, I find my final home
Through tissues and through blood vessels I happily roam
To liver, bile duct, intestine – my kingdom to claim
Reproducing endlessly is my ultimate aim
Eggs released in feces, back to water they go
The cycle of Dicrocoelium, the only life I know

CHORUS:
From water to snail to ant to sheep
Dicrocoelium's journey, cunningly deep
I'm the lancet fluke, nature's grand design
Generations of parasites stand in my line
(Survival through the ages, my legacy divine)

OUTRO:
A parasite's purpose is simply to survive
Through manipulation and cunning, I keep my kind alive
Not evil, not malicious, just playing nature's part
Dicrocoelium dendriticum, a biological art
Hijacking behaviors with precision so keen
The ultimate puppeteer, unseen but never unseen

songs.title: Metamorphosis - Becoming New
songs.tags: Upbeat indie pop with classical orchestral breaks mirroring transformation stages. Dynamic tempo changes with thoughtful lyrics - think educational yet emotionally resonant, with chorus hooks perfect
songs.prompt:
VERSE 1:
Hatching from an egg so small
A hungry caterpillar crawls
Exoskeleton constrains its growth
Molting is the only hope
Four or five instars to go
Each one larger than before
Eating leaves with mighty jaws
Storing energy for what's in store

CHORUS:
Metamorphosis, nature's great disguise
Metamorphosis, one life becomes two lives
Complete transformation, DNA's dictation
Metamorphosis, evolution's grand surprise

VERSE 2:
Hormones surge, the signal's clear
Juvenile growth must disappear
The caterpillar seeks a place
To hang and enter resting state
Enzymes break down tissues now
Imaginal discs begin to grow
Inside the chrysalis or cocoon
Liquid reorganization blooms

(CHORUS)

BRIDGE:
No simple growing larger
But death and resurrection
Cells programmed for destruction
While others form anew
Hemolymph circulating
Through structures rearranging
The brain rewires completely
For life with different view

VERSE 3:
Wings unfold where none existed
Compound eyes where once were simple
Proboscis forms for sipping nectar
Where chewing mandibles once lectured
From crawler bound to single plants
To pollinator free to dance
Different organism, shared DNA
Metamorphosis had its way

(CHORUS)

OUTRO:
From butterfly to beetles
From moths to honey bees
From maggots into house flies
From larvae to fireflies
Holometabolous wonders
Complete transformation
Metamorphosis – becoming
Nature's innovation

songs.caption: This song details Holometabolous Metamorphosis, the complete transformation seen in insects like butterflies. It describes the larval stage (e.g., caterpillar) focused on eating and molting (shedding its exoskeleton). Inside the chrysalis or cocoon, the original body is broken down, and specialized imaginal discs grow into the adult form (wings, compound eyes). This "complete transformation" results in two distinct lives from shared DNA. #Metamorphosis #Entomology #Caterpillar #Holometabola

songs.title: Palmer
songs.tags: Rock
songs.prompt:
"
På en øy der solen alltid skinner,
Palmer vaier, brisen synger minner.
Kokosnøtter faller fra toppen av trær,
Vi lever livet her, uten noen bekymringer.


Rock n' roll blant palmer og kokos,
Rytmen av havet og bølgenes kor.
Gitaren skriker, trommene dundrer,
Vi danser under stjernene, hører vinden sus.


Strandfest, sand mellom tærne,
Kokosdrinker, vi har ingen barrierer.
Musikken sprer seg som ild i natten,
Palmer svever, vi er fanget i rytmen.


Rock n' roll blant palmer og kokos,
Rytmen av havet og bølgenes kor.
Gitaren skriker, trommene dundrer,
Vi danser under stjernene, hører vinden sus.


Solnedgangen maler himmelen i gull,
Vi er fri, lar musikken ta kontroll.
Palmer og kokos, vårt paradis,
Vi synger høyt, mister oss i hver eneste viss.


Rock n' roll blant palmer og kokos,
Rytmen av havet og bølgenes kor.
Gitaren skriker, trommene dundrer,
Vi danser under stjernene, hører vinden sus.


Palmer vaier, natten blir til dag,
Vi lever evig, i en rockende magi.
Kokos og rock n' roll, vårt evige slag,
Under himmelen, i vårt paradis, her er vi fri."

songs.title: Bury Me On A Backroad (Cover)
songs.tags: Somber acoustic guitar and upright bass set a moody Folk Noir foundation. Sparse chamber pop elements enter—mournful strings, brushed percussion, muted piano. Delicate woodwinds add subtle counterpoint in the bridge, while dynamics swell and recede, preserving the haunting, intimate feel.
songs.prompt:
[Intro][A solitary, melancholic piano chord progression in C minor, joined by a sad cello line]
(Oh, that's just Brandon)

[Chorus] [Very slow, somber acoustic folk ballad tempo, 65 BPM. Worn, emotional male singing with deep resonance]
I might never come back home
So if I die, draped in all of my gold
Bury me on a back road
In a flyover state with no name on the stone
'Cause I don't want nobody crying at my grave
I don't want my family to go through that pain
So bury me on a back road
In a flyover state with no name on the stone

Yeah, bury me on a back road
'Cause I'm chasing the wind, and might never come back home
Can't believe that I made it this far, 'cause I really came up out the mud like a tadpole
This ain't only for me, my city in the passenger seat, they might want me to crash though
Taking shots, but I'm right at the glass, put the ball in my hand and I'm breaking the backboard
Got a dream and some cash and a tank full of gas, and a past that I'll never let go of
Raise a glass to the nights I ain't die but came close when I think back, dog, I get choked up
If the stars don't shine, I'ma still find light in the fire from the bridges I burned up

songs.title: Endless  Dream
songs.tags: Lofi, Chilled, Ambient, Downtempo, Female Vocals, ethereal
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Chilled Lofi, Ambient, Downtempo]
[SOUNDS LIKE: Tycho, Bonobo, Nujabes]
[STYLE: Relaxing, Atmospheric, Lush, Clean]
[MOOD: Calm, Serene, Reflective, Dreamy]
[VOCALS: Female, Ethereal, Background]
[ARRANGEMENT: Slow tempo, Laid-back groove, Ethereal textures, Clean guitar melodies]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Clean electric guitar, Synthesizers, Ambient pads, Subtle percussion]
[TEMPO: Slow, 70-90 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Lo-fi aesthetic, Warm tones, Soft compression, Analog warmth, Masterpiece, Perfectly Recorded, Produced by Emancipator]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Bridge, Chorus, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: Gentle throughout, Gradual builds and releases, Smooth transitions]
[EMOTIONS: Peacefulness, Contemplation, Tranquillity, Nostalgia]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Intro]
[Ethereal instrumental with ambient pads and soft percussion]

[Verse 1]
In the realm where dreams take flight,
Morpheus reigns, the endless night,
With stars in eyes, a helm of might,
Crafting dreams till morning light.

[Pre-Chorus]
[Softly] In shadows deep, where whispers stay,
The endless siblings find their way.

[Chorus]
Dream and Death, Desire's call,
Despair and Delirium, they bind us all,
Destiny's path, Destruction's fall,
The Endless stand, eternal thrall.

[Verse 2]
Death with kindness guides us on,
An ankh that shines till night is gone,
Desire's form, both fair and strong,
In hearts of all, they sing their song.

[Pre-Chorus]
[Softly] In shadows deep, where whispers stay,
The endless siblings find their way.

[Chorus]
Dream and Death, Desire's call,
Despair and Delirium, they bind us all,
Destiny's path, Destruction's fall,
The Endless stand, eternal thrall.

[Bridge]
Delirium's laugh, a chaotic tune,
Once Delight, now lost in moon,
Despair's grey eyes, a hook's sharp croon,
Destiny's book, our fates in rune.

[Chorus]
Dream and Death, Desire's call,
Despair and Delirium, they bind us all,
Destiny's path, Destruction's fall,
The Endless stand, eternal thrall.

[Outro]
[Ethereal instrumental with ambient pads and soft percussion, fading out]

songs.title: Cargo Haulin’ Blues
songs.tags:
Blues Rock, Laid-back, Groovy,
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE INFO
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Blues Rock, Southern Blues]
[SOUNDS LIKE: B.B. King, Eric Clapton]
[STYLE: Laid-back, Groovy, Bluesy]
[MOOD: Reflective, Cool, Determined]
[VOCALS: Male, Soulful, Deep]
[ARRANGEMENT: Medium tempo, Blues shuffle, Slide guitar]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Electric guitar, Bass, Drums, Hammond organ]
[TEMPO: Medium, 90-110 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Warm, Analog feel, Bluesy]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Chorus, Verse, Chorus, Bridge, Chorus, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: Smooth and steady, Builds in intensity]
[EMOTIONS: Reflection, Determination, Resilience]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Intro]
(Slide guitar and Hammond organ intro)

[Verse 1]
Out in the black, where the stars don’t shine,
Haulin’ cargo, passin’ the time.
Every jump’s a gamble, every run’s a fight,
Space hauler’s blues in the dead of night.

[Chorus]
Cargo haulin’, through the void so wide,
Keepin’ on truckin’, got nothin’ to hide.
Stars above and the planets below,
In the verse, we take it slow.

[Verse 2]
From ArcCorp to Hurston, the credits flow,
Haulin’ loads from port to port, ya know.
Fuelin’ up and flyin’ high,
In the verse, we reach for the sky.

[Chorus]
Cargo haulin’, through the void so wide,
Keepin’ on truckin’, got nothin’ to hide.
Stars above and the planets below,
In the verse, we take it slow.

[Bridge]
Haulin’ goods through cosmic lanes,
Star Citizen, we make our names.
In the verse, we find our way,
Day by day, we earn our pay.

[Chorus]
Cargo haulin’, through the void so wide,
Keepin’ on truckin’, got nothin’ to hide.
Stars above and the planets below,
In the verse, we take it slow.

[Outro]
(Slide guitar and Hammond organ fade out)

songs.title: Flagellar Symphony: The Evolution of Motion
songs.tags: progressive rock
songs.prompt:
Verse 1
In the primordial oceans deep and wide,
Microbial life began to thrive inside.
A simple cell, with purpose undefined,
Seeking ways to move, to grow, to find.

Pre-Chorus
Through ancient genes and natural selection,
A marvel born from pure perfection.
A structure small, yet power profound,
The bacterial flagellum, life unbound.

Chorus
Spin and twirl, the flagellum’s might,
Propelling life through day and night.
From simple parts, complexity,
A tale of evolution, wild and free.

Verse 2
From proteins formed in ancient days,
A rotary engine in wondrous ways.
The hook, the filament, all aligned,
In synchronized dance, finely designed.

Pre-Chorus
Adaptations through the eons past,
Survival’s dance, both slow and fast.
A testament to nature’s art,
The flagellum’s role, a vital part.

Chorus
Spin and twirl, the flagellum’s might,
Propelling life through day and night.
From simple parts, complexity,
A tale of evolution, wild and free.

Bridge
In every spin, a story told,
Of ancient mysteries, brave and bold.
Innovation from the smallest start,
The flagellum’s beat, life’s rhythmic heart.

Chorus
Spin and twirl, the flagellum’s might,
Propelling life through day and night.
From simple parts, complexity,
A tale of evolution, wild and free.

Outro
Now we see, through science bright,
The flagellum’s path, its endless flight.
A journey through the depths of time,
The evolution of motion, so sublime.

songs.title: Oogenesis
songs.tags: Soul, female voice
songs.prompt:
[Piano]
[Verse 1]
In the womb it begins, a journey so grand,
O'ogonia forming, life’s delicate strand.
Mitosis creating, the starting of life,
In the fetal ovary, potential is rife.

[Pre-Chorus]
Chromosomes pair up, crossing over so fine,
Creating an ovum, with a future design.

[Chorus] [Soulful Hook]
O'ogenesis, where the life ignites,
In the quiet cells, under soft moonlight.
From o'ogonia to ova, shining bright,
In the dance of life, it all feels right.

[Verse 2]
At birth she carries millions inside,
Primary o'ocytes, in a dormant tide.
Resting in follicles, waiting their turn,
Through childhood years, patiently they yearn.

[Pre-Chorus]
Hormones signal, pathways align,
Bringing new cycles, in rhythm and time.

[Chorus] [Soulful Hook]
O'ogenesis, where the life ignites,
In the quiet cells, under soft moonlight.
From o'ogonia to ova, shining bright,
In the dance of life, it all feels right.

[Bridge]
Puberty awakens, the cycles commence,
Follicles maturing, breaking their silence.
Monthly an ovum, ready to release,
In the fallopian tube, the journey’s piece.

[Chorus] [Big Finish]
O'ogenesis, where the life ignites,
In the quiet cells, under soft moonlight.
From o'ogonia to ova, shining bright,
In the dance of life, it all feels right.

[Outro] [Fade to End]
From fetus to woman, in nature's sweet kiss,
The miracle of life, through o'ogenesis.

songs.title: Spermatogenesis
songs.tags: female singer, piano, energetic, soul
songs.prompt:
[Piano intro]
[Verse 1]
In the seminiferous tubules, it all begins,
Germ cells dividing, where the life spins.
Mitosis and meiosis, stages they go through,
From spermatogonia to something new.

[Pre-Chorus]
Chromosomes shuffle, genes recombine,
Creating gametes, one of a kind.

[Chorus] [Funky Hook]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

[Verse 2]
Primary spermatocytes take the stage,
Meiotic divisions, the genetic gauge.
Secondary spermatocytes split once more,
Turning into spermatids, ready to explore.

[Pre-Chorus]
Form and function, they start to align,
Tail and head developing, by design.

[Chorus] [Funky Hook]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.







[Bridge]
In the epididymis, they learn to swim,
Gaining mobility, getting fit and trim.
Stored and waiting, for the right cue,
Ready for action, to meet the ovum too.



[Chorus] [Big Finish]
Spermatogenesis, the cycle of life,
In the dance of cells, where the future's rife.
From a single germ to a sperm, so precise,
Creating life, in this funky slice.

[Outro] [Fade to End]
From germ cell to gamete, in a cellular bliss,
The wonder of life, through spermatogenesis.

songs.title: Neuroscience of Love
songs.tags: Synth-pop, Neo-soul
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
There's a spark that's electric, a chemical light.

[Verse 1]
In the brain, it's a dance, dopamine in play,
With a rush and a glance, it can sweep us away.
Serotonin sings a song of serene,
While oxytocin binds us, unseen.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.

[Chorus]
Neuroscience of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.

[Verse 2]
In the limbic’s embrace, emotions intertwine,
Every kiss, every trace, in patterns we define.
With each memory stored, and each moment we share,
Our neurons explore, and love’s everywhere.

[Pre-Chorus]
Synapses fire, connections made,
In this neural parade, we’re not afraid.



[Chorus]
Neuroscience of love, it's more than just a feel,
It's the circuits in our brain, making it all real.
From the cortex to the heart, we're wired from the start,
In the science of love, we play our part.

[Bridge]
Chemical reactions, they pave the way,
In the maze of the mind, where our feelings stay.
With endorphins high, and the pathways clear,
In the neuroscience of love, we conquer fear.


[Outro]
In the depths of our minds, where the neurons ignite,
Love's a journey of the brain, in the chemical light.

songs.title: Ages of Life
songs.tags: Reflective indie-pop, melodic, dramatic, with piano, guitar, and drums. Warm, clear, and emotive voice.
songs.prompt:
[Instrumental intro]
[piano solo]

[Verse 1]
In the dawn of time, the earth was new,
Life began to stir, in the ocean's blue.
From single cells to complex forms,
Evolution's path, nature's norms.

[Build-up]
[piano and guitar]

[Chorus]
Evolutionary history, written in the land,
From ancient seas to deserts grand.
Life's a journey, ever-changing story,
In every creature, echoes of glory.

[Break]
[piano solo]

[Verse 2]
Dinosaurs roamed, mighty and free,
Then came the age of mammals and trees.
From the depths of time, through fire and ice,
Life adapts, pays the price.

[Build-up]
[drums join]

[Chorus]
Evolutionary history, written in the land,
From ancient seas to deserts grand.
Life's a journey, ever-changing story,
In every creature, echoes of glory.

[Bridge]
Every fossil, every stone,
Tells the tale of life’s unknown.

[Chorus]
Evolutionary history, written in the land,
From ancient seas to deserts grand.
Life's a journey, ever-changing story,
In every creature, echoes of glory.

[Outro]
In the vast expanse of earth and sky,
The story of life will never die.

[End]



[Chorus]
Evolutionary history, written in the land,
From ancient seas to deserts grand.
Life's a journey, ever-changing story,
In every creature, echoes of glory.

[Outro]
In the vast expanse of earth and sky,
The story of life will never die.

[End]

songs.caption: This instrumental track captures the grand, sweeping narrative of Evolutionary History. It spans the immense timescale from the origin of single-celled life in the ancient seas to the rise and fall of major groups, such as the dinosaurs and the subsequent age of mammals. The song emphasizes that every fossil and every creature today holds a part of this continuous, ever-changing story of adaptation and transformation. #EvolutionaryHistory #Paleontology #Adaptation #Biology

songs.tags: A vibrant pop anthem driven by vibrant stacked synths, pulsing four-on-the-floor drums, crisp bright guitars, and lively handclaps. Tight, rhythmic verses give way to pre-choruses rich with bold vocal harmonies, culminating in explosive, anthemic hook-filled choruses. The bridge scales back to minimal textures and vocals before a soaring final chorus with full instrumentation and maximal energy.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
They called it "junk," just clutter in our code,
A dusty old attic, a heavy genetic load.
A sliver of genes, they said, wrote the score,
The rest was just noise, not worth exploring more.
But now those shadows sing, their secrets ignite,
A hidden spark glowing in our DNA's light! (Aha!)

[Chorus]
It’s the Genome Remix, a vibrant new beat,
From ancient invaders, our story’s complete!
Those ERVs, once ignored, left behind,
Now flip the switch, rewiring our mind! (Whoa!)
In the strands of our DNA, a viral design,
Makes us human, bold and divine!

[Verse 2]
A retrovirus crashed, long ages ago,
Slipped into our cells, where life’s rivers flow.
In germline it lingered, from kin to kin,
Its wild roots tamed, new roles to begin.
Evolution’s a maestro, rewriting the art,
Old foes turned allies, now beating our heart! (Yeah!)

[Chorus]
It’s the Genome Remix, a vibrant new beat,
From ancient invaders, our story’s complete!
Those ERVs, once ignored, left behind,
Now flip the switch, rewiring our mind! (Whoa!)
In the strands of our DNA, a viral design,
Makes us human, bold and divine!

[Bridge]
(Woo!) LTRs are dancing, directing the show,
(Boom!) Syncytin-1 helps new life to grow!
From viral fusion, placentas take flight,
Turning old foes to a beacon of light.
In embryos sparking, our genes they command,
Crafting our future with a viral hand!

[Chorus]
It’s the Genome Remix, a vibrant new beat,
From ancient invaders, our story’s complete!
Those ERVs, once ignored, left behind,
Now flip the switch, rewiring our mind! (Whoa!)
In the strands of our DNA, a viral design,
Makes us human, bold and divine!

[Outro]
So when you gaze at the code in your core,
See the viruses that shaped evermore.
Not junk, but a symphony, woven so grand,
Life’s epic remix, forever to stand! (Alright!)

songs.title: Oxygen Crisis
songs.tags: Neo-Classical Crossover
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1 - aggressive]
Cyano-bacteria rise
Photosynthesis their prize
Oxygen floods ancient skies
Watch the an-aerobes die

[Chorus - soaring, clean]
Toxic air!
Nature's first warfare!
Atmosphere transformed
New life being born

[Verse 2 - aggressive]
Iron oceans turn to rust
Adapt or turn to dust
Great Oxidation unfolds
As atmosphere takes hold

[Bridge]
[Atmospheric break]
Death brings evolution's change
As Earth's chemistry rearranges
Breathe in deep
New life to keep

[Final Chorus - soaring, clean]
Air on fire!
Building higher!
Through extinction's gate
To aerobic fate
songs.full_lyrics:
[Verse 1 - aggressive]
Cyano-bacteria rise
Photosynthesis their prize
Oxygen floods ancient skies
Watch the an-aerobes die

[Chorus - soaring, clean]
Toxic air!
Nature's first warfare!
Atmosphere transformed
New life being born

[Verse 2 - aggressive]
Iron oceans turn to rust
Adapt or turn to dust
Great Oxidation unfolds
As atmosphere takes hold

[Bridge]
[Atmospheric break]
Death brings evolution's change
As Earth's chemistry rearranges
Breathe in deep
New life to keep

[Final Chorus - soaring, clean]
Air on fire!
Building higher!
Through extinction's gate
To aerobic fate
songs.caption: This song details the Great Oxidation Event (GOE), one of Earth's most significant evolutionary catastrophes. When early cyanobacteria began photosynthesis, they released massive amounts of oxygen into the atmosphere. This "toxic air" was poisonous to the existing anaerobic life, causing a mass extinction, but paved the way for the evolution of all oxygen-breathing (aerobic) organisms. #GOE #OxygenationEvent #Cyanobacteria #EarthHistory #Evolution

songs.title: The Discombobulated Dictionary
songs.tags: Upbeat indie pop with a quirky, intellectual vibe,playful piano, bouncy rhythm, clear enunciation for the wordplay. Think "educational novelty song" with a dash of linguistic wit.
songs.prompt:
[Short Piano Intro]
*keys twinkling*
[Verse 1]
Have you ever felt discombobulated?
Well here's a fact that's underrated
There's no such thing as combobulated
These words have got me fascinated
[Pre-chorus]
You can be disheveled all day long
But being heveled? That's just wrong!
[Chorus]
Oh, these words without their prefixes
Leave our dictionary in a fix
Like being gruntled instead of dis-
These roots are linguistic tricks!
[Verse 2]
They say you might be inept
But no one's ever just been ept
And while you're feeling nonchalant
There's no such thing as being chalant!
[Pre-chorus]
You can be unscathed after the storm
But being scathed was once the norm!
[Chorus]
Oh, these words without their prefixes
Leave our dictionary in a fix
Like being gruntled instead of dis-
These roots are linguistic tricks!
[Bridge]
(Spoken with scholarly tone)
Ineffable, incorrigible, unrequited too
These words had brothers once, it's true
But somewhere in the sands of time
Their lonely roots were left behind
[Final Chorus]
Oh, these words without their prefixes
Leave our dictionary in a fix
Now we're all discombobulated
By the words we've separated!
[Outro]
(Spoken playfully)
I guess you could say this song has left me... completely combobulated!
*piano trill*
[End]

songs.title: Multicellular Symphony
songs.tags: Orchestral rock, 90 BPM, featuring multiple vocal harmonies
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Single cells join the dance
Chemical signals enhance
Differentiation starts to flow
Each part knows where to go
[Chorus]
We are many, we are one
Division of labor has begun
Specialized, synchronized
Evolution's grand design
[Bridge - Multiple Voices - harmony]
[deep voice] "I'll be muscle" 
[mid voice] "I'll be bone"
[high voice] "I'll be neurons" 
"Together we've grown" (harmony)
[Verse 2]
Tissues form into a whole
Each cell playing its role
Complexity rises high
As simple forms say goodbye
[Final Chorus - Full Harmony]
Symphony of cells unite
Dancing in organic light
Many voices, single song
Making life grow strong
songs.full_lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Single cells join the dance
Chemical signals enhance
Differentiation starts to flow
Each part knows where to go
[Chorus]
We are many, we are one
Division of labor has begun
Specialized, synchronized
Evolution's grand design
[Bridge - Multiple Voices - harmony]
[deep voice] "I'll be muscle" 
[mid voice] "I'll be bone"
[high voice] "I'll be neurons" 
"Together we've grown" (harmony)
[Verse 2]
Tissues form into a whole
Each cell playing its role
Complexity rises high
As simple forms say goodbye
[Final Chorus - Full Harmony]
Symphony of cells unite
Dancing in organic light
Many voices, single song
Making life grow strong
songs.caption: This song celebrates the immense evolutionary leap to multicellular life. It highlights the critical processes involved: cell signaling and differentiation, where single cells communicate and specialize to form tissues and organs. This division of labor allows for greater size and complexity, creating synchronized bodies where "many are one" (muscle, bone, neurons) in a unified, cooperative system. #Multicellularity #CellBiology #Evolution #Differentiation

songs.title: Genital evolution of mammals (it's not just about penises and vaginas)
songs.tags: Folk, melodic, catchy, melodic interlude, female singer
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
From the primitive cloaca of our reptilian past
To specialized organs that evolved pretty fast
The genital tubercle's embryonic display
Shows how mammals found their reproductive way

[Verse 2]
S-R-Y gene kicks in, that's the Y chromosome's call
Testosterone surges, changes organs and all
While females develop through a different route
With estrogen guiding what it's all about

[Chorus]
It's not just about penises and vaginas
It's about how evolution redesigns us
From monotremes to marsupials to us
Each adaptation built on natural trust

[Verse 3]
Marsupials got their reproductive innovation
Two vaginas, one birth canal, quite a sensation
While placentals took a different evolutionary track
Single reproductive tract, no turning back

[Bridge]
Bats and dolphins, they've got their own design
Streamlined bodies left some parts behind
But the basic blueprint still remains the same
Since mammals first earned their evolutionary fame

[Verse 4]
From spiny echidnas to smooth human skin
Different solutions for getting genes in
Each species adapted in its special way
That's how natural selection saved the day

[Chorus 2]
It's not just about penises and vaginas
It's about how survival redesigns us
From egg-layers to a live birth, we evolved
Each puzzle piece of life getting solved

[Outro]
Through time and change, we came to be
Each form adapting differently
No perfect plan, just what survived
As nature's options multiplied

songs.caption: This song explores the fascinating evolution of mammalian reproductive anatomy. It traces origins from the primitive cloaca and highlights the role of genes like SRY and hormones (like testosterone and estrogen) in sex differentiation. It contrasts the unique two vaginas of marsupials with the single tract of placentals, showcasing how natural selection built on ancient blueprints to create incredible reproductive diversity. #Evolution #MammalianBiology #SRYGene #Anatomy

songs.title: The Sacculina's Course
songs.tags: A dark alternative rock track fuses heavy bass and pounding drums with layers of distorted electric guitars and icy atmospheric synths. Ominous drones set a melancholic cinematic tone, while soaring orchestral swells escalate tension. A strong male lead voices the verses, meeting an emotional female in a dramatic, intertwining duet.
songs.prompt:
Drifting in the endless blue, a nauplius in the sea
Shedding skin for something new, a destiny to be
A cyprid searching, born to seek, a shell of living stone
On armored leg, so mild, so meek, I find my future throne

The joint is where the journey starts, a needle I become
I shed the world and all my parts, my swimming days are done
Inject my soul, a cellular tide
Nowhere to run, nowhere to hide

I am the root inside your veins, the change you can't control
I wear your body, steal your reins, and hollow out your soul
You'll tend my young, you'll think they're yours, a puppet on a string
This is the Sacculina's course, the new life that I bring

A phantom life grows deep inside, a web of hungry thread
My own true self has slowly died, another blooms instead
My instincts change, my purpose shifts, my claws protect a growth
A strange maternal, twisted gift, a parasitic oath


I am the root inside your veins, the change you can't control
I wear your body, steal your reins, and hollow out your soul
You'll tend my young, you'll think they're yours, a puppet on a string
This is the Sacculina's course, the new life that I bring

Your abdomen will broaden now.
You'll clean the sac that's breaking through.
You are a mother, I'll show you how.
I'll make a female out of you.
[Eerie Whispers - Female Vocal in background]
(It's not mine... it's not mine... but I must protect it...) 


I AM THE ROOT INSIDE YOUR VEINS, THE CHANGE YOU CAN'T CONTROL!
I WEAR YOUR BODY, STEAL YOUR REINS, AND HOLLOW OUT YOUR SOUL!
YOU'LL TEND MY YOUNG, YOU'LL THINK THEY'RE YOURS, A PUPPET ON A STRING!
THIS IS THE SACCULINA'S COURSE, THE NEW LIFE THAT I BRING!

songs.caption: This song is about Sacculina, a parasitic barnacle. The larva (cyprid) seeks a crab host, sheds its shell, and injects its cellular body into the crab. Once inside, it grows a root-like structure that spreads through the host's body, absorbing nutrients. The parasite also castrates the crab and feminizes males, forcing them to protect and tend the parasite's egg sac as if it were their own brood. #Sacculina #Parasitism #Crab #Zombification #MarineBiology

songs.title: The Owl-Parrot's Song
songs.tags: A folk ballad led by intricate fingerpicked acoustic guitar, enriched with airy, atmospheric synths for a dreamlike layer. The narrative vocal is prominent and expressive. Deep hand drums provide a pulsing “boom-boom,” while metallic bells add “ching, ching” mystical detail throughout.
songs.prompt:
(Verse 1) 
In a land of green, by ferns embraced 
Aotearoa, a sacred place 
No ground-based hunter, no silent paw 
So a parrot evolved, and changed its law. 
It grew so heavy, it grew so wide 
Put its mighty wings away, kept them at its side 
The heaviest parrot, mossy and green 
A flightless, ancient, walking dream.

(Chorus) 
I am the Kākāpō, the parrot of the night 
I walk the forest floor, in the pale moonlight 
The islands hear my call, a deep and rumbling drum... 
[Low, rhythmic vocal] Boom... boom... boom... 
[Higher, metallic vocal] Ching! Ching! Ching! 
Hoping that a mate will come.

(Verse 2) 
I am the owl-parrot, I sleep all day 
When the sun has set, I go my way 
I climb the rimu trees with claws and beak 
To find the special, sacred fruit I seek. 
My feathers blend with leaf and stone 
But I'm tragically, fatally alone. 
My old defense, when I'm in fear 
Is just to freeze... and disappear.

(Chorus) 
I am the Kākāpō, the parrot of the night 
I walk the forest floor, in the pale moonlight 
The islands hear my call, a deep and rumbling drum... 
[Low, rhythmic vocal] Boom... boom... boom... 
[Higher, metallic vocal] Ching! Ching! Ching! 
Hoping that a mate will come.

(Verse 3) 
But ships arrived upon the shore 
Brought predators not seen before 
The stoat, the rat, the feral cat 
My ancient world was falling flat. 
And freezing still was no defense 
A fatal, stark, dark consequence. 
My kind was lost, a fading sound 
Until just fifty could be found. 
[Musical shift: more urgent, a little sadder] 
Just a skraak in the dark.

(Bridge) 
The humans who had brought the bane 
Were the only ones to stop the rain 
They made a vow, a sacred pact 
To find a way to bring us back. 
They built a refuge, predator-free 
On an island home, by the sea. 
They gave us names, they gave us food 
They saved our ancient, precious brood.

(Chorus) 
I am the Kākāpō, the parrot of the night 
I walk the forest floor, in the pale moonlight 
The islands hear my call, a deep and rumbling drum... 
[Low, rhythmic vocal] Boom... boom... boom... 
[Higher, metallic vocal] Ching! Ching! Ching! 
And now, my love... my love has come.

(Outro) 
Two hundred thirty-seven, we're strong and true 
[Softly] Two hundred thirty-seven... 
We walk the tracks our fathers knew. 
The ancient line is safe, at last. 
The future's here... the past is past. 
[The sound of the "Boom" and "Ching" fades out slowly]

songs.title: The Sunspot Archivist
songs.tags: Acoustic Folk style opens with an intimate fingerpicked guitar, later joined by subtle piano accents and sparse upright bass. The storyteller’s warm, clear vocal sits front and center. Light brush percussion and gentle harmonies enrich the chorus, highlighting the honest, timeless mood.
songs.prompt:
(Verse 1)
In a quiet Norwegian attic, where the dust had a long reign,
Sat a thousand glass-plate negatives, protected from the sun and the rain.
From '54 to '86, a silent, steady gaze,
Captured sunspots dancing through a thousand golden days.
They were ghosts of light and knowledge, tucked away from view,
Waiting for a patient hand to see them through.

(Chorus)
Oh, what a tale the sun would tell,
Of a dedicated hand, breaking the silent spell.
Saving history, one image at a time,
A sunspot's story, rescued from the crime
Of being forgotten, a treasure in the dark,
Now just a funding away from leaving its digital mark.

(Verse 2)
With a brush and gentle cleanser, he worked with careful grace,
Wiping scratches, smudges from each historical face.
A painter’s patience, a scientist’s soul,
Making a broken puzzle feel completely whole.
He saved the solar cycle, the flares and the quiet times,
Forgetting all the money, and the nickel-and-dime crimes.

(Chorus)
Oh, what a tale the sun would tell,
Of a dedicated hand, breaking the silent spell.
Saving history, one image at a time,
A sunspot's story, rescued from the crime
Of being forgotten, a treasure in the dark,
Now just a funding away from leaving its digital mark.

(Bridge)
He used his own money when the grant ran out, you see,
A simple act of love for what the future ought to be.
A sunspot's silent sigh, a scientist's old dream,
Now waiting for a signal, from a philanthropic stream.
To bring them to the public, for all the world to find,
The passion of a sunspot archivist for all of mankind.

(Outro)
Oh, the sun still burns and the sun still shines,
But now its past is preserved in digital lines.
So lend a hand, let the story be known,
For a harvest of light that he saved all alone.
Yeah, he saved all alone...

(Bridge)
He used his own money when the grant ran out, you see,
A simple act of love for what the future ought to be.
A sunspot's silent sigh, a scientist's old dream,
Now waiting for a signal, from a philanthropic stream.
To bring them to the public, for all the world to find,
The passion of a sunspot archivist for all of mankind.

(Outro)
Oh, the sun still burns and the sun still shines,
But now its past is preserved in digital lines.
So lend a hand, let the story be known,
For a harvest of light that he saved all alone.
Yeah, he saved all alone...
songs.caption: This song is inspired by the true story of saving the Harestua Solar Observatory's photo archive. A tribute to the quiet work of preserving decades of our sun's history, one glass plate at a time, so this scientific heritage is not lost to time.

songs.title: Wires in my mind, they hum and they weave (Remastered)
songs.tags: bass male voice, Acapella, spoken word, human beat box, beautiful female voice, duet, jazz, post-punk revival
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Wires in my mind, they hum and they weave,
(Patterns of thought, what do I believe?)
Voices in the air, they pull and they sway,
(Why do I feel what I feel today?)
Like circuits in code, my heart can be swayed,
(A whisper, a spark, and my truth might fade.)  
[Pre-Chorus]
They try to rewrite me, to break through my core,
Like machines in the dark, they keep wanting more.
But I’ll trace the roots, where my shadows begin,
To know who I am, gotta look deep within.  
[Chorus]
Why do we think, why do we feel?
What’s the truth, and what’s the real?
Guard my mind, build walls of light,
Question the stars in the endless night.
From code to soul, we’re wired the same,
Learning to rise, to master the game.
Why we believe, I’ll find the key,
To set my heart and my spirit free.  
[Verse 2]
Screens flash their lies, they twist and they turn,
(Feeding my fears, till my bridges burn.)
Like a machine, I could fall to their call,
(Hacked by the noise, till I lose it all.)
But I’ll ask the why, let the questions ignite,
(Cut through the fog, find my own light. ) 
[Pre-Chorus]
They try to control me, with words that deceive,
But I’ll guard my soul, choose what I believe.
From neurons to networks, we’re fragile, it’s true,
Yet strength comes from knowing what’s me, what’s you.  
[Chorus]
Why do we think, why do we feel?
What’s the truth, and what’s the real?
Guard my mind, build walls of light,
Question the stars in the endless night.
From code to soul, we’re wired the same,
Learning to rise, to master the game.
Why we believe, I’ll find the key,
To set my heart and my spirit free.  
[Bridge]
Epistemology, my compass, my guide,
Unravel the threads where the shadows hide.
Every thought, every tear, every choice that I make,
I’ll question, I’ll learn, for my freedom’s sake.
Like a system that’s locked, I’ll rewrite my own code,
With wisdom and heart, I’ll lighten the load.  
[Chorus]
Why do we think, why do we feel?
What’s the truth, and what’s the real?
Guard my mind, build walls of light,
Question the stars in the endless night.
From code to soul, we’re wired the same,
Learning to rise, to master the game.
Why we believe, I’ll find the key,
To set my heart and my spirit free.  
[Outro]
So I’ll ask, and I’ll seek, till the answers align,
My thoughts, my feelings, my truth divine.
From circuits to dreams, we’re searching, it’s clear,
Why we believe—it starts right here

songs.title: The Life of Scandinavian Eels
songs.tags: A Chilled Hifi, Ambient, Downtempo track unfolds at 80 BPM with warm, hi-fi production and analog textures. A clean electric guitar introduces lush melodic phrases over mellow synth pads. Ethereal female vocals float as airy layers, blending into smooth verses and dreamy choruses. Subtle snare taps and soft percussive accents add depth, building through gentle, gradual swells. The bridge brings textured ambient layers, then dissolves to a tranquil outro, maintaining serene energy and a cohesive, reflective feel throughout.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
(Soft ambient sounds with gentle guitar melodies)

[Verse 1]
In the Sargasso Sea, they are born, so small and so shy,
Tiny glass eels, beneath the northern sky,
From the ocean deep, they start their life,
A journey so long, full of mystery and strife.

[Chorus]
They swim through the night, guided by the moon,
In the brackish waters, they find their tune,
From the ocean deep, to the streams they go,
Scandinavian eels, in the twilight glow.

[Verse 2]
They grow and they change, through the years they roam,
In lakes and rivers, they make their home,
Elvers to yellow eels, their colors bloom,
In the calm and the still, they find their room.

[Chorus]
They swim through the night, guided by the moon,
In the brackish waters, they find their tune,
From the ocean deep, to the streams they go,
Scandinavian eels, in the twilight glow.

[Bridge]
When the time is right, they feel the call,
To the Sargasso Sea, they return them all,
Through the tides and waves, they make their way,
To the place of birth, where they’ll lay.

[Outro]
(Gentle fade with ambient pads and soft guitar)

Life’s a circle, they’ve shown us well,
In the waters deep, their stories tell,
From birth to return, a cycle complete,
The Scandinavian eels, in the deep sea meet.

songs.caption: This song celebrates the incredible life cycle and migration of the European Eel (Anguilla anguilla). They begin life in the Sargasso Sea then migrate across the Atlantic to grow for years in Scandinavian rivers and lakes (as yellow eels). They then undertake a final, mysterious return journey to the Sargasso Sea to reproduce, completing one of nature's great cycles. #EuropeanEel #EelMigration #MarineBiology #AnguillaAnguilla

songs.title: Beneath the corpse: The Shore Sexton Beetle
songs.tags: raspy, quick, Female singer, Dark ambient/industrial fusion with gothic folk elements; blends atmospheric drones, mechanical percussion and haunting strings to evoke the beetle's nocturnal world
songs.prompt:
[Verse]
Drawn to the scent that others avoid
I sense what's happened in the void
Where life has ceased, my work begins
Nekrodes littoralis - decay's kin
Black carapace gleaming in moonlight
Orange-tipped antennae sense what's right
Upon fallen giants I will crawl
Answering nature's solemn call

[CHORUS]
Beneath the corpse, I make my home
Through flesh and bone, I roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, I bask

[Verse]
My antennae quiver with delight
Sensing chemicals in the night
The perfect corpse, too large to bury
Unlike my cousins, I'm not wary
I carry mites upon my shell
Travelers with stories to tell
They fight the flies that would compete
Our alliance makes our work complete

[CHORUS]

[Verse]
Eggs I lay in this fertile ground
Where nutrients and shelter abound
My larvae feast on what remains
Nature's custodians, we take the reins
We're not monsters though you might recoil
Just recyclers of this mortal coil
Breaking down what once had breath
Transforming ending into life's next step

[BRIDGE]
The shore sexton's story seldom told
Our purpose ancient, necessary, bold
What seems macabre serves a greater good
Nature's balance perfectly understood
In twilight hours when shadows fall
I emerge ready for nature's call
Too large to bury, the carcass waits
My work begins as day abates

[CHORUS]

[Verse]
The giants fall and we arrive
With tiny allies that help us thrive
A partnership of give and take
A perfect system, no mistake
In the dance between life and demise
We are the link that harmonize
The carrion's end is just the start
Of nature's most beautiful art

[FINAL CHORUS]
Beneath the corpse, we make our home
Through flesh and bone, we roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, we'll always bask

songs.caption: This song is about the Shore Sexton Beetle (Necrodes littoralis). Its crucial role is carrion recycling—breaking down large, often coastal, remains.As a decomposer, the beetle is drawn to decay where it lays its eggs, allowing its larvae to feast on the nutrient-rich flesh. While the song mentions mites (a common trait among carrion beetles), the core science is its vital role in transforming dead matter into life, ensuring the ecosystem's cycle continues. #NecrodesLittoralis #CarrionBeetle
