songs.title: The Rust's Grand Cycle
songs.tags: An upbeat folk track featuring crisp acoustic guitar picking, playful light percussion, and a bright, upfront vocal delivery with a clear storytelling style. Touches of glockenspiel and subtle upright bass add whimsy, creating a lively, engaging, almost scientific-narrative atmosphere.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
From tiny spore, a story grand
A fungus grows across the land
But not so simple, not so plain
It needs two homes to rise again
A clever dance, a changing guise
Beneath the sun, beneath the skies
We call them rusts, for what they do
But their true nature's strange and new.

[Chorus]
Oh, the Rust's Grand Cycle, a fungal marvel's feat
Five spore stages, bittersweet
From barberry to fields of grain
Then back again to start the reign
Two different hosts, a fungal chain
To live and thrive through sun and rain.

[Verse 2]
Stage zero, on the barberry leaf 🌿
Spermogonia, bringing sweet relief
Not for infection, but a subtle plea
For genetic mixing, wild and free
Then aecia form, a cup so bright
With aeciospores, taking flight
Carried by the breeze, they make their way
To fields of wheat, to start their day.

[Chorus]
Oh, the Rust's Grand Cycle, a fungal marvel's feat
Five spore stages, bittersweet
From barberry to fields of grain
Then back again to start the reign
Two different hosts, a fungal chain
To live and thrive through sun and rain.

[Verse 3]
Now on the wheat, the uredinia bloom 🌾
Orange pustules, filling fields with gloom
Urediniospores, they spread with haste
Re-infecting, never gone to waste
This is the epidemic, fast and wide
As crops are battered, deep inside
But seasons turn, the wheat grows old
A new stage beckons, brave and bold.

[Chorus]
Oh, the Rust's Grand Cycle, a fungal marvel's feat
Five spore stages, bittersweet
From barberry to fields of grain
Then back again to start the reign
Two different hosts, a fungal chain
To live and thrive through sun and rain.

[Bridge]
The telia form, a darker hue
With teliospores, to see winter through ❄️
Thick-walled and dormant, they bravely wait
For spring's warm sun to seal their fate
They germinate, on damp, cool ground
Basidiospores are then unbound
To drift and land, on barberry new
And start the wondrous cycle through.

[Outro]
So marvel at this tiny pest
A life so complex, truly blessed
To find a way, through changing clime
A master of both space and time.
The rust's grand cycle, ever true...
(Music fades with a final strum)

songs.caption: This song details the fascinating, complex life of Rust Fungi (Pucciniales), which are plant pathogens. To complete their life cycle, many require two different hosts (heteroecious), such as barberry and wheat. The fungi cycle through five different spore stages (including aeciospores, urediniospores, and teliospores) to spread, persist through winter, and initiate epidemics. #RustFungi #PlantPathology #Heteroecious #FungalCycle #Botany

songs.tags:
Comedy, Quirky, Lighthearted,
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE INFO

In every new gadget, it's always there,

Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh,
Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh,
In the world of tech, here's the truth to see,
Porn's always ahead, in the industry.

[Outro]
Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh,
Oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh, oh,
In the world of tech, here's the truth to see,
Porn's always ahead, in the industry.

songs.title: The Pollination dance (Deception and Delight)
songs.tags: The track opens with bright acoustic guitar and crisp handclaps, layered with lively mandolin. Percussive foot stomps and upright bass drive an upbeat folk-pop groove. Whistled hooks and playful glockenspiel interject between verses, while harmonized vocals and subtle flute add a mysterious, whimsical layer. The bridge uses claps and unexpected pauses for a sneaky, clever feel.
songs.prompt:
[Folk Pop] [Playful Mystery]

[Intro] [Birdsong] [Whistling] [Soft Breeze]
[Verse 1] [Female Vocal]
Sunlight on the petals, colors bright and bold,
A sweet perfume is wafting, stories to unfold.
For buzzing little helpers, with fuzzy, tiny feet,
Offering a banquet, oh so sweet.

[Pre-Chorus]
Nectar in the chalice, pollen dust so fine,
A tempting invitation, a truly grand design.
From stamen to pistil, a journey light and free,
Nature's grand exchange, for all the world to see.

[Chorus] [Duet] [Harmonies]
Oh, the pollination dance, a symphony of bloom,
Rewards for the faithful, dispelling nature's gloom.
But some play their tricks, a clever, sly charade,
In this intricate ballet, a living masquerade!

[Verse 2] [Male Vocal]
Now, some cunning orchids, they don't play fair and square,
Mimicking a female, a false love affair.
A male bee, he's fooled, by scent and by the shape,
Trying to mate with petals, a very close escape!
No nectar offered, just a trick of the eye,
But pollen still attaches, as he buzzes on by.

[Bridge]
Then there are the cheaters, with proboscis sharp and long,
Piercing through the flower, where they don't quite belong.
Stealing all the nectar, without spreading any seed,
A robber's quick grab, fulfilling their own greed.
They bypass the anthers, leave the stigma untouched,
A clever little shortcut, not helping out much.

[Catchy Hook] [Harmonies]
It's the pollination dance, a cunning, complex art,
With sweet rewards and trickery, playing every part!

[Verse 3] [Female Vocal]
Yet still, the dance continues, through sunshine and through rain,
Most partnerships are honest, easing nature's strain.
From apple orchards to fields of golden grain,
Pollinators working, again and again.

[Break]
[Instrumental Break] [Melodic Interlude]

[Chorus, repeated twice] [Harmonies]
Oh, the 
pollination dance, a symphony of bloom,
Rewards for the faithful, dispelling nature's gloom.
But some play their tricks, a clever, sly charade,
In this intricate ballet, a living masquerade!

[Outro]
[Powerful Outro] [Instrumental Outro]
[Fade Out] [Birdsong] [Wind Howling]

songs.title: Beneath the corpse
songs.tags: raspy, quick, Female singer, Dark ambient/industrial fusion with gothic folk elements; blends atmospheric drones, mechanical percussion and haunting strings to evoke the beetle's nocturnal world
songs.prompt:
[Verse]
Drawn to the scent that others avoid
I sense what's happened in the void
Where life has ceased, my work begins
Neh-kro-deez lit-tor-ah-lis - decay's kin
Black carapace gleaming in moonlight
Orange-tipped antennae sense what's right
Upon fallen giants I will crawl
Answering nature's solemn call

[CHORUS]
Beneath the corpse, I make my home
Through flesh and bone, I roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, I bask

[Verse]
My antennae quiver with delight
Sensing chemicals in the night
The perfect corpse, too large to bury
Unlike my cousins, I'm not wary
I carry mites upon my shell
Travelers with stories to tell
They fight the flies that would compete
Our alliance makes our work complete

[CHORUS]

[Verse]
Eggs I lay in this fertile ground
Where nutrients and shelter abound
My larvae feast on what remains
Nature's custodians, we take the reins
We're not monsters though you might recoil
Just recyclers of this mortal coil
Breaking down what once had breath
Transforming ending into life's next step

[BRIDGE]
The shore sexton's story seldom told
Our purpose ancient, necessary, bold
What seems macabre serves a greater good
Nature's balance perfectly understood
In twilight hours when shadows fall
I emerge ready for nature's call
Too large to bury, the carcass waits
My work begins as day abates

[CHORUS]

[Verse]
The giants fall and we arrive
With tiny allies that help us thrive
A partnership of give and take
A perfect system, no mistake
In the dance between life and demise
We are the link that harmonize
The carrion's end is just the start
Of nature's most beautiful art

[FINAL CHORUS]
Beneath the corpse, we make our home
Through flesh and bone, we roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, we'll always bask

songs.title: The Bioelectric Code (A Song of Morphogenesis)
songs.tags: Synthwave pads pulse over a tight electronic groove, layered with crisp indie rock guitars. Verses use arpeggiated synths and concise drum patterns for a measured, explanatory tone. The chorus erupts with soaring synth leads, punchy drums, and driving bass, verging on anthemic urgency.
songs.prompt:
[Intro: Synth/Driving Beat]
(Start with a deep, pulsing synthetic sound, like an electrical signal)

[Verse 1: Explanatory, Mid-tempo]
From a single cell, a miracle takes hold,
A complex blueprint, a story to unfold.
The genome's in the nucleus, but it's not the only guide,
There's a **hidden conversation** deep inside.
Morphogenesis, that grand, self-assembly play,
Is run by signals that are more than just DNA.
It's the body's **collective mind**, without a skull,
A vast, decentralized network overhauling the hull.

[Pre-Chorus: Building Energy]
We study the tadpole, the planarian's strange power,
To rebuild a structure, in a single hour.
They know the **anatomical map**, a pattern memory,
Of where to put the pieces, for all the world to see!

[Chorus: Anthemic, High-Energy]
Oh, the **Bioelectric Code**, it's the pattern we seek,
A language of **voltage** that the cells must speak.
**Ion channels** are the gates, and the **membrane potential's** the key,
To build a form, from a worm to you and me!
It's not just a blueprint, but a **goal, a target state**,
A way to **self-repair** and improvise our fate.
This is **Levin's Law**, that the shape is a **computation**,
A soft biological AI, guiding body's construction.

[Verse 2: More Technical/Detail-Oriented]
The cells don't just divide, they're not merely drones,
They're **problem-solving**, speaking through ion flows.
We can **read this signal**, with channel-blocking drugs,
And tell the tissue **where to build**, pulling out the plugs.
From **Xenobots** constructing, to a new eye on a flank,
It's **basal cognition**, from the bottom of the rank.
The voltage pattern tells them: "You are the left side now,"
A pre-neural decision we're learning how, somehow

[Bridge: Dramatic, Slowing Tempo]
Cancer cells, they **disconnect**, a rogue signal starts to grow,
They lose the communal network, acting on their own.
But **re-wire the circuit**, restore the right voltage flow,
And you **reprogram the cancer**, where it simply can't go.
Unlocking the body's latent regenerative pretense,
This **collective intelligence** is our last defense!

[Chorus: Anthemic, High-Energy]
Oh, the **Bioelectric Code**, it's the pattern we seek,
A language of **voltage** that the cells must speak.
**Ion channels** are the gates, and the **membrane potential's** the key,
To build a form, from a worm to you and me!
It's not just a blueprint, but a **goal, a target state**,
A way to **self-repair** and improvise our fate.
This is **Levin's Law**, that the shape is a **computation**,
A soft biological AI, guiding body's construction.

[Outro: Fade Out with Echo/Synth]
Gotta crack that Bioelectric Code...
The **Bioelectric Code**... (Echoes)
⚡️ **Regenerate!** (Final synth hit)

songs.caption: This song explores the Bioelectric Code, the revolutionary idea that cells use voltage signals (controlled by ion channels) as a pre-neural communication layer. This code runs the non-genetic "software" of life: morphogenesis and regeneration. Researchers can read this signal and manipulate the body's "target state" to guide tissue construction (like in the planarian) and even reprogram cancer cells. #Bioelectricity #Regeneration #LevinLab #Morphogenesis

songs.title: The Unseen Alliance
songs.tags: A thoughtful, slightly melancholic yet ultimately triumphant folk-rock ballad. Start with a gentle acoustic guitar and subtle percussion. Vocals should be clear and narrative. Build intensity slightly in the choruses and bridge with more instrumentation (bass, light drums, maybe a distant strings pad).
songs.prompt:
(Verse 1)
In silent cells, a secret kept so long,
How simple life became a mighty throng.
A single thought, a whisper in the breeze,
Of ancient pacts, beneath primeval trees.
Lynn Margulis, with vision sharp and keen,
Saw tiny kin, where others had not been.

(Chorus)
Oh, the lonely road from hypothesis to light,
When truth takes flight, but can't yet reach its height.
Rejected words, a science standing still,
But deep inside, they trusted in the data's will.
An endosymbiotic dance, a union grand,
A revolution for this promised land.

(Verse 2)
Then Jostein Goksøyr, across the northern sea,
A parallel idea, wild and wondrous, free.
He penned his thoughts, a letter to Nature's page,
Ignored by many, a forgotten stage.
They spoke of mergers, prokaryotes embrace,
A larger host, securing evolution's pace.

(Chorus)
Oh, the lonely road from hypothesis to light,
When truth takes flight, but can't yet reach its height.
Rejected words, a science standing still,
But deep inside, they trusted in the data's will.
An endosymbiotic dance, a union grand,
A revolution for this promised land.

(Bridge - Music builds slightly, more pronounced drums/bass)
"Proteins must hold the key!" the elders cried,
"Not simple DNA, can't be denied!"
But mitochondria, chloroplasts, they held their own,
A tiny genome, on a separate throne.
The decades turned, the evidence grew strong,
Where molecular whispers hummed a different song.

(Guitar Solo - Melodic and slightly uplifting)
(Instrumental break, expressing the turning tide of acceptance)

(Chorus)
Oh, the lonely road from hypothesis to light,
When truth takes flight, and finally takes its height.
Accepted words, a science moving still,
The ancient pact, fulfilling nature's will.
An endosymbiotic dance, a union grand,
A revolution for this promised land.

(Outro - Music softens, fading acoustic guitar and a sustained gentle synth pad)
From whispered thoughts to textbooks, clear and bold,
A story of life, courageously told.
Margulis, Goksøyr, their insights now so true,
The unseen alliance, forever shining through.
(Fade out)

songs.title: The Formica Complex (A Wood Ant Song)
songs.tags: Brisk, galloping acoustic guitar forms the core, joined by lively fiddle and mandolin weaving playful countermelodies. Rhythmic upright bass pulses steadily beneath, while light percussion (shakers, brushes) nudges momentum. Instrumental breaks let the fiddle dance, evoking a lively village scene.
songs.prompt:
(Verse 1: Ecology) 
In the sun-dappled forest, where the pine needles fall, 
A great mound is rising, a biological hall. 
It's a fortress of twigs and a solar-powered dome, 
A million-strong family that’s building a home. 
We are the "keystone," the guardians in red, 
We hunt the invertebrates, make sure the brood gets fed. We climb up the treetops to "milk" aphid herds, 
For a sweet drop of honeydew, in unspoken words.

(Chorus) 
Oh, we’re the Wood Ants, in a tangled family tree, 
A cryptic species, a forest mystery. 
From a single mound of needles to a super-colony, 
That’s the secret science of our biology. 
Are you rufa? aquilonia? Or polyctena? 
We're a puzzle for your taxonomy.

(Verse 2: Biology & Social Life) 
Now, a nest's not a nest without a good queen, 
But our social structure is a whole different scene. 
Are you monogynous? 
Just one queen to rule? 
That’s the rufa standard, the old-fashioned school. 
She'll parasitize fusca, a cuckoo-ant's game, 
'Til her own workers hatch and take over the name. 
But polyctena and aquilonia? 
They’ve broken the mold, 
With hundreds of queens and a story untold. 
They bud off new nests, a sprawling domain, 
A polydomous empire, again and again.

(Verse 3: Morphology - The Hairy Problem) 
You think you can name us? 
You'd better look close, 
Get out your hand-lens, here’s the diagnosis. 
It all comes down to the hairs on the head, 
Pay attention now, to what I just said. 
If the back of the head is all shiny and bare, 
That’s polyctena, with nary a hair. 
If a fringe stands erect on her occiput's peak, 
That’s rufa you've found, the one that you seek. 
But wait! Check the eyes! 
Are they hairy to see? 
That's cold-loving aquilonia, from the high country.

(Chorus) 
Oh, we’re the Wood Ants, in a tangled family tree, 
A cryptic species, a forest mystery. 
From a single mound of needles to a super-colony, 
That’s the secret science of our biology. 
Are you rufa? aquilonia? Or polyctena? 
We're a puzzle for your taxonomy.

(Bridge: Evolution & The Supergene) 
But the hairs are a trick! 
The truth’s in the code, 
We all hybridize on this evolutionary road. 
We were split by the ice in the Pleistocene age, 
Now we meet and we mix on this forested stage. 
You see, the real reason for our different life, 
For the single-queen’s calm or the multi-queen's strife... 
It’s a block of our genes that won’t recombine! 
A SOCIAL SUPERGENE that draws the whole line.

There’s the M-haplotype (that’s for mono-gyne-style), 
And the P-haplotype (for the poly-gyne pile). 
It’s not just a choice, it’s a genetic command, 
This one piece of DNA that rules the whole land.

(Chorus) 
Oh, we’re the Wood Ants, in a tangled family tree, 
A cryptic species, a forest mystery. 
From a single mound of needles to a super-colony, 
That’s the secret science of our biology. 
Are you rufa? aquilonia? Or polyctena? 
We're a puzzle for your taxonomy.

(Outro) 
So look at the mound, that great feat of design, 
A city of sisters, a genetic line. 
A keystone, a farmer, a predator true... 
We're the Formica complex, 
We’re still evolving, 
We're a genomic puzzle... and we're coming through.

(Strum fades out)

songs.title: Just Another Christmas Rhyme
songs.tags: A lush orchestral arrangement drives this traditional 50’s Christmas song, with warm strings, gentle piano, brush-kit drumming, upright bass, and delicate sleigh bells. Rich four-part vocal harmonies enter for the chorus, while glockenspiel accents add festive sparkle throughout.
songs.prompt:
[Intro: Slow, dreamy, piano only] 
I’ve been waiting three hundred sixty-four days 
Just to say exactly what I’m gonna say... 
(Beat drop) (Sound of Sleigh Bells)

[Verse 1] 
Got the fire crackling on the TV screen 
Wearing the ugliest sweater that you've ever seen 
Grandma’s drinking eggnog, getting kinda loud 
And there’s a mystery fruitcake in the crowd 
I saw a red-nosed reindeer flying through the sky 
Or maybe that was just a plane passing by? 
Doesn't matter 'cause the spirit is here 
It’s the overly specific time of the year!

[Chorus: Explosive, energetic, choir backing] 
Oh! It’s snow, snow, snow! (Even though it’s raining) 
Meet me underneath the mistletoe! (I'm just complaining) 
We got stockings hung with excessive care 
There is glitter and ribbons everywhere 
Jingle bells, batman smells, hear the angels sing 
I’m doing every single Christmas thing! 
Yeah, every single Christmas thing!

[Verse 2] 
Now we’re walking in a winter wonderland 
With a peppermint mocha freezing in my hand 
Last Christmas, I gave you my heart (I did!) 
But this year I’m shopping at the glowing K-Mart 
Wrap the box, tie the bow, put it on the sled 
Visions of sugar plums attacking my head 
The cookies are burnt but Santa won't mind 
He’s leaving all the calories behind!

[Chorus: Explosive, energetic, choir backing] 
Oh! It’s snow, snow, snow! (Even though it’s raining) 
Meet me underneath the mistletoe! (No use complaining) 
We got stockings hung with excessive care 
There is glitter and ribbons everywhere 
Jingle bells, batman smells, hear the angels sing 
I’m doing every single Christmas thing!

[Bridge: Building tension, drum roll] 
He’s making a list (Checking it twice!) 
He’s checking the list (It’s cold as ice!) 
Better not pout, better not cry I’m telling you, telling you, telling you why... (Dramatic pause)

[Key Change: Shift Up] 
[Guitar Solo: Festive and distorted]

[Final Chorus: Maximum Volume, High Energy] 
IT’S SNOW, SNOW, SNOW! (Look at it falling!) 
Underneath the plastic mistletoe! (Santa is calling!) 
We got stockings hung with excessive care 
There is glitter and ribbons in my hair! 
Sleigh bells ring, ting-a-ling, let the register ring 
I’m doing every single Christmas thing!

[Outro] 
Yeah, we’re doing the tropes (Falalalala) 
Roasting the chestnuts (Falalalala) 
Happy Holidays, everyone! 
(Fade out with laughter and bells)

songs.title: The ghost of a mate..
songs.tags: Opening with fingerpicked acoustic guitar and airy mandolin, subtle upright bass joins, building a gentle folk texture. Sparse brushed percussion and haunting vocal harmonies add an ethereal, mysterious layer, while reverb-drenched glockenspiel and woodwinds provide an alluring, evolutionary intrigue.
songs.prompt:
[Melancholic Folk Ballad] [Mysterious Atmosphere]
[Soft Intro] [Eerie Whispers] [Ghostly Echoes]

(Verse 1)
[Female Vocal, slightly vulnerable]
In deserts deep, where sun beats down so hot,
An all female lineage, a forgotten, ancient spot.
Cnemidophorus neomexicanus, a name whispered low,
No male is needed for new life to grow.
A virgin birth, a clone from mother's grace,
Yet a shadow dance plays out in this lonely place.

[Pre-Chorus]
A strange compulsion, a ritual so profound,
Though no seed is planted, no true love is found.
One sister rises, with a "male" form she takes,
Mounting another, for reproduction's sake.

[Chorus] [Harmonies] [Melancholic Atmosphere]
Oh, the ghost of a mate, in the desert's soft light,
A memory of passion, in the pale moonlight.
Ovulation's trigger, a touch from a friend,
A silent reminder, where old pathways extend.
The mimicry of mating, a vestige held so tight,
For life to continue, in this peculiar night.

(Verse 2)
[Male Vocal, reflective]
They were born from hybrid, a genetic swirl,
Two species mingling, a new kind of girl.
Perhaps a lost male, his genes long since gone,
Left behind a trigger, from dusk until dawn.
A hormonal echo, from cycles long past,
A need for the pressure, to make the eggs last.

[Bridge] [Emotional Swell]
The benefits are clear, in a harsh, demanding land,
No search for a partner, with options close at hand.
Each one a perfect copy, adapted, strong, and true,
But the body remembers, what it once had to do.
Is it a leftover, from when true males roamed free?
A behavioral relic, for all the world to see.

[Catchy Hook] [Female Vocal with Male Harmonies]
The ghost of a mate, a silent, sacred bond,
For new life to awaken, in deserts beyond.

(Break)
[Instrumental Break] [Spectral Melody]

(Chorus, repeated twice) [Harmonies] [Melancholic Atmosphere]
Oh, the ghost of a mate, in the desert's soft light,
A memory of passion, in the pale moonlight.
Ovulation's trigger, a touch from a friend,
A silent reminder, where old pathways extend.
The mimicry of mating, a vestige held so tight,
For life to continue, in this peculiar night.

[Outro] [Powerful Outro] [Fade Out]
[Ominous Drone] [Whisper] "The ghost of a mate..."

songs.title: From Geology to Biology
songs.tags: A sweeping orchestral piece opens with lush strings and delicate woodwinds, gradually layering in brass and subtle percussion. The sections build dynamically, weaving countermelodies and dramatic crescendos, before resolving with a rich, full ensemble for a grand finish.
songs.prompt:
[Intro] [Soft Intro] 
From the dust... a whisper.
From the void... a pull.
Gravity's ghost, in the nebula's lull.
A cosmic beat, a slow collapse.
Setting the stage, closing the gaps.

[Verse 1]
Rock on rock, a violent smash.
Kinetic fire, a molten flash.
Planetesimals dance in a fiery haze,
Building a world in the Hadean daze.
Radioactive heart, glowing deep inside
Nowhere to run, nowhere to hide.
A sphere of magma, a formless mass,
Waiting for the future to come to pass.

[Chorus]
[Sudden Break into heavy distorted guitar riff][Climactic]
[Soaring Male Vocal with Harmonies] 
Gravity's the architect, the master hand
Sorting the elements across the land.
Heavy metal sinks, a downward drive
Iron and nickel, keeping the core alive.
That's the anode, the negative pole
Taking its toll, making the planet whole.
Light silicate floats, a crusty skin
The cathode waits for the life to begin.
A planetary battery, charged and deep
While the cosmos sleeps, its secrets keep.

[Verse 2]
[Driving, palm-muted guitar riff and double-bass drumming]
The liquid core, a churning, spinning sea
Powers the dynamo, for you and me.
A magnetic shield, a force field bright
Deflecting the solar wind, day and night.
Protecting the oceans, the atmosphere's breath
Saving the surface from a cosmic death.
Volcanoes rumble, the circuit's complete
Bringing the deep earth's fire to the street.
Redox potential, a chemical might
A gradient of power, burning bright.

[Chorus]
[Climactic]  [Powerful Vocals] [Harmonies] 
Gravity's the architect, the master hand
Sorting the elements across the land.
Heavy metal sinks, a downward drive
Iron and nickel, keeping the core alive.
That's the anode, the negative pole
Taking its toll, making the planet whole.
Light silicate floats, a crusty skin
The cathode waits for the life to begin.
A planetary battery, charged and deep
While the cosmos sleeps, its secrets keep.

[Bridge]
[Ethereal clean guitar melody with heavy reverb, reminiscent of flowing water]
Down in the dark, where the deep vents gleam
A new kind of life, a waking dream.
Alkaline plumes in an acid sea
A natural gradient, wild and free.
A proton-motive force, a gift of the stone
Life plugs right in, no longer alone.
A nested battery, a smaller design
Harnessing the power, so divine.

[Verse 3]
Then the membrane forms, a lipid wall
Answering biology's call.
The proton pump, a brand new tool
Breaking the old geochemical rule.
Pumping the ions, against the flow,
Making its own internal glow.
ATP synthase, a turbine spins
Where the biological work begins.
A tiny battery, self-contained
A new independence, now ordained.

[Guitar Solo]
[Melodic and technical guitar solo over the industrial riff, leading into the outro]

[Outro] [Powerful Outro] 
[Orchestral Build with layered synths and guitars][Crescendo] 
Unplugged from the vent, it starts to roam,
Carrying its power, making Earth its home.
From gravity's pull, to the planet's charge,
To the living cell, both small and large.
A nested battery, a cosmic rhyme,
Beating through the heart of space and time.
[Instrumental Break] 
Yeah... a nested battery...
From geology... to biology.

songs.title: Biology Rhapsody
songs.tags: Dynamic, eclectic,  reflective piano, strings , powerful guitar riffs, choir harmonies. Emotive, versatile voice
songs.prompt:
[Instrumental intro]
[piano and strings]

[Verse 1]
Is this the real life? Is this biology?
Caught in the web of life, with no escape from ecology.
Open your eyes, look up to the skies and see,
We’re just a part of this vast diversity.

[Break]
[guitar solo]

[Verse 2]
From the smallest cell, to the giant whale,
Evolution’s tale, through the ages we prevail.
Spiraling DNA, the blueprint of our genes,
Nature’s orchestra, playing in endless scenes.

[Build-up]
[drums join]
[choir harmonies]

[Chorus]
Symbiotic stories, predator and prey,
Every niche filled in nature’s grand ballet.
Life’s a journey, through the branches of the tree,
Adaptation’s key, to this grand mystery.

[Breakdown]
[soft piano]

[Verse 3]
Photosynthesis, the power of the sun,
Plants convert light, so life can run.
From deserts dry to the deepest seas,
Life finds a way, in harshest degrees.

[Bridge]
[piano and strings]
Life, it’s ever-changing, evolution’s rearranging,
Genetic drift and mutation, driving diversification.
Oh, the beauty of natural selection,
Shaping life’s intricate perfection.

[Rock Section]
[guitar riff]
[heavy drums]



[Bridge]
[piano and strings]
Life, it’s ever-changing, evolution’s rearranging,
Genetic drift and mutation, driving diversification.
Oh, the beauty of natural selection,
Shaping life’s intricate perfection.

[Rock Section]
[guitar riff]
[heavy drums]

[Verse 4]
Mendel’s peas and Darwin’s finches,
Nature’s clues, in scientific benches.
Genomes mapped, the secrets they hold,
In the language of life, stories unfold.

[Build-up]
[full band]

[Chorus]
Symbiotic stories, predator and prey,
Every niche filled in nature’s grand ballet.
Life’s a journey, through the branches of the tree,
Adaptation’s key, to this grand mystery.

[Instrumental interlude]
[guitar solo]
[orchestral strings]

[Outro]
In the end, we’re part of a greater plan,
Life’s rhapsody, from the sea to land.
No matter where we go, or what we see,
We’re forever linked, in this biology.

[End]

songs.title: Erection, Ejaculation, Fertilization (Dance of Creation v2)
songs.tags: Hip-Hop, Educational, Energetic, Male and Female Vocals
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Hip-Hop, Educational]
[SOUNDS LIKE: Schoolhouse Rock, Bill Nye the Science Guy]
[STYLE: Upbeat, Informative, Engaging]
[MOOD: Energetic, Fun, Enlightening]
[VOCALS: Male and Female, Dynamic, Conversational]
[ARRANGEMENT: Fast tempo, Catchy beat, Interactive delivery]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Drum machine, Bassline, Synth melodies]
[TEMPO: Fast, 110-130 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Crisp, Clear, Youthful, Modern]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Bridge, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: High energy, Clear transitions, Engaging flow]
[EMOTIONS: Curiosity, Enthusiasm, Knowledgeable]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Intro]
Yo, let's break it down, the biology sound,
From erection to fertilization, knowledge profound.
Listen up, kids, it's a natural flow,
[Female voice]The body's process, now let's go!

[Verse 1]
Blood flowin' down, to the cavernous zone,
Nerves get excited, yeah, the magic's on.
Nitric oxide’s the key, arteries expand,
[Female voice]Erection's here, it's nature's grand plan.

[Pre-Chorus]
Hormones in the mix, testosterone’s the lead,
Physical response, it's all guaranteed.

[Chorus]
Erection, ejaculation, fertilization,
The body's symphony in perfect coordination.
Nature’s design, no hesitation,
A journey of life in every generation.

[Verse 2]
Sperm produced in the testes, it's the start,
Travels through the ducts, ready to depart.
Seminal vesicles add the fluid blend,
[Female voice]Prostate gland's secretions, the mix they send.

[Pre-Chorus]
Muscles contract, the pressure's intense,
Ejaculation's here, it all makes sense.

[Chorus]
Erection, ejaculation, fertilization,
The body's symphony in perfect coordination.
Nature’s design, no hesitation,
[Female voice]A journey of life in every generation.

[Bridge - Fertilization]
Sperm meets egg, in the fallopian tube,
Fusion of the cells, a zygote in the groove.
DNA combines, a new life begins,
Fertilization’s magic, where it all spins.

[Pre-Chorus]
Zygote implants, in the uterine wall,
Embryo develops, life’s miracle call.

[Chorus]
Erection, ejaculation, fertilization,
The body's symphony in perfect coordination.
Nature’s design, no hesitation,
A journey of life in every generation.

[Outro]
So there you have it, the biology's clear,
From erection to life, it's a process dear.
Respect the science, and understand,
The wonders of life, it's all so grand.

[End]
Knowledge in your hands, now you know,
The biology of life, let it grow.

songs.title: Abiogenesis
songs.tags: Progressive electronic with atmospheric ambient elements. Building layers of synthesizers to represent increasing molecular complexity, with gradual evolution of musical themes.
songs.prompt:
Main Lyrics:
Primordial soup under lightning skies
Simple molecules start to rise
RNA strands begin to fold
In oceans deep and waters old

Chorus:
From chemistry to something more
Life emerging from the core
Building blocks in thermal vents
Where nothing lived, now life begins

Verse 2:
Lipid bubbles form and split
First membranes start to knit
Self-replicating in the deep
Ancient secrets molecules keep
Catalytic cycles spin
As complexity creeps in

Bridge:
Nucleotides align just right
Proteins fold in darkest night
Metabolism finds its way
Each mistake leads on its way
To something new, something strange
Chemical bonds rearrange

Verse 3:
Four billion years ago or more
On this rocky, restless shore
Life took hold, began to dance
Not by design but simply chance
From simple parts to DNA
The first cell finds its way

Final Chorus:
From chemistry to something more
Life emerging from the core
Each step taken, nothing planned
Just physics playing in the sand
Where nothing lived, now life begins
The cosmic dance spins

songs.title: Against the Grain
songs.tags: country melodic acoustic
songs.prompt:
[Verse]
I don't sip no whiskey from a rusty old jar,
Don't drive a pickup, ain't got no steel guitar,
My boots ain't worn, they're shiny and new,
But I got something real, I got something true.

[Verse 2]
Ain't singin' 'bout tractors or good ol' denim jeans,
My life's not a picture from a southern magazine,
I don't bark at the moon, ain't no back-road king,
But I write from my heart, and that's my thing.

[Chorus]
Against the grain, where the wild flowers grow,
In a world of clichés, I let my own roots show,
No guns, no beer cans, just honest words and pain,
Living my own truth, runnin' against the grain.

[Verse 3]
I don't hunt deer at dawn, don't wear a cowboy hat,
Ain't got no barnyard tales, or a dog like that,
But I sit on my porch, with a soulful tune,
Strummin' my own lines, underneath the moon.

[Bridge]
They say you ain't country if you don't play along,
But I found my own rhythm, my own kind of song,
I'm carving my path, where the crowd don't go,
In fields of honesty, I'll let my story grow.

[Chorus]
Against the grain, where the wild flowers grow,
In a world of clichés, I let my own roots show,
No guns, no beer cans, just honest words and pain,
Living my own truth, runnin' against the grain.

songs.title: The Life of Scandinavian Eels
songs.tags:
Lofi, Chilled, Ambient, Downtempo, Female Vocals
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE INFORMATION
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Chilled Lofi, Ambient, Downtempo]
[STYLE: Relaxing, Atmospheric, Lush, Clean]
[MOOD: Calm, Serene, Reflective, Dreamy]
[VOCALS: Female, Ethereal, Background]
[ARRANGEMENT: Slow tempo, Laid-back groove, Ethereal textures, Clean guitar melodies]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Clean electric guitar, Synthesizers, Ambient pads, Subtle percussion]
[TEMPO: Slow, 70-90 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Lo-fi aesthetic, Warm tones, Soft compression, Analog warmth, Masterpiece, Perfectly Recorded, Produced by Emancipator]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Chorus, Verse, Chorus, Bridge, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: Gentle throughout, Gradual builds and releases, Smooth transitions]
[EMOTIONS: Peacefulness, Contemplation, Tranquillity, Nostalgia]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Intro]
(Soft ambient sounds with gentle guitar melodies)

[Verse 1]
In the Sargasso Sea, they are born, so small and so shy,
Tiny glass eels, beneath the northern sky,
From the ocean deep, they start their life,
A journey so long, full of mystery and strife.

[Chorus]
They swim through the night, guided by the moon,
In the brackish waters, they find their tune,
From the ocean deep, to the streams they go,
Scandinavian eels, in the twilight glow.

[Verse 2]
They grow and they change, through the years they roam,
In lakes and rivers, they make their home,
Elvers to yellow eels, their colors bloom,
In the calm and the still, they find their room.

[Chorus]
They swim through the night, guided by the moon,
In the brackish waters, they find their tune,
From the ocean deep, to the streams they go,
Scandinavian eels, in the twilight glow.

[Bridge]
When the time is right, they feel the call,
To the Sargasso Sea, they return them all,
Through the tides and waves, they make their way,
To the place of birth, where they’ll lay.

[Outro]
(Gentle fade with ambient pads and soft guitar)

Life’s a circle, they’ve shown us well,
In the waters deep, their stories tell,
From birth to return, a cycle complete,
The Scandinavian eels, in the deep sea meet.

songs.title: Queen of the Underground
songs.tags: Quirky alt-rock with elements of funk and experimental jazz. Heavy basslines representing underground tunneling, with odd time signatures and playful, bouncy rythms
songs.prompt:
Main Lyrics:
Wrinkled skin and barely see
Cold-blooded mammal anomaly
Living deep in endless night
Eusocial like bees, that's right

Chorus:
We're naked mole rats, we don't care
About the oxygen down there
Cancer can't touch our DNA
Queen's the only one making babies, hey!
Living twenty years or more
In our tunnels beneath the floor

Verse 2:
No pain receptors in our skin
Acid doesn't burn, let it in
Metabolism slows way down
When food's scarce underground
Share our warmth in naked piles
Running backwards through our miles

Bridge:
T-R-P-V-1 changed our way
Feel no heat, that's how we play
Hyaluronic acid flows
Keeps our cells from growing old
High C-O-2? We're doing fine
Evolution's strange design

Chorus
We're naked mole rats, we don't care
About the oxygen down there
Cancer can't touch our DNA
Queen's the only one making babies, hey!
Living twenty years or more
In our tunnels beneath the floor

Final Verse:
Teeth outside our lips so weird
Digging tunnels, engineering
Colony minds, we work as one
In the darkness far from sun
Nature's twisted up the rules
Made us nature's precious fools

Teeth outside our lips so weird
Digging tunnels, engineering
Colony minds, we work as one
In the darkness far from sun
Nature's twisted up the rules
Made us nature's precious fools

songs.title: Pheromone Path
songs.tags: A sparse piano motif introduces the piece, joined subtly by strings employing extended techniques and gentle pizzicato. The texture shifts through gradual layering and dynamic swells, with isolated moments of silence, allowing resonance and tension to shape the contemporary classical mood.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
[Soft Intro] (Synth arpeggios twinkle, building in layers)
(Subtle, intricate percussive clicks and tiny, rustling sounds)

[Verse 1]
Beneath the green, a world unseen, a hidden empire thrives
No spoken words, no whispered sounds, just countless busy lives
They march in lines, a silent flow, a purpose in their stride
But how they know where they should go, where treasures they can hide?

[Pre-Chorus]
[Building Intensity]
It's not by sight, or guiding light, no map within their mind
A secret language, unseen trails, for all their kin to find!
A chemical whisper, a scent in the breeze
Carried on antennae, through the leaves of the trees!

[Chorus]
[Harmonies] [Catchy Hook]
Oh, the pheromone path, a fragrant design!
A sticky message, exquisitely fine!
From tiny glands, a silent command is laid
A scented blueprint, for the colony made!
Follow the trail, where the signals combine!
A molecular secret, beautifully signed!

[Verse 2]
A scout returns, with vital news, a food source now revealed
A drop of scent, a tiny sign, upon the forest field
"This way, this way!" the message spreads, a signal strong and clear
Each sister adds her own perfume, banishing all fear

[Bridge]
[Emotional Swell]
From alarm cries, to foraging routes, to finding a new home
A complex dialogue unfolds, where countless pathways roam
No single leader, just shared intent, a collective, guiding hand
Bound by odors, diligently sent, throughout their tiny land.

[Chorus]
[Harmonies]
Oh, the pheromone path, a fragrant design!
A sticky message, exquisitely fine!
From tiny glands, a silent command is laid
A scented blueprint, for the colony made!
Follow the trail, where the signals combine!
A molecular secret, beautifully signed!

[Break]
[Instrumental Break] [Layered Arrangement]
(Driving electronic beat with a swirling synth melody, representing the movement and complexity of the pheromones. High-pitched, quick synth "blips" mimic individual signals.)

[Chorus, repeated twice]
[Harmonies] [Climactic]
Oh, the pheromone path, a fragrant design!
A sticky message, exquisitely fine!
From tiny glands, a silent command is laid
A scented blueprint, for the colony made!
Follow the trail, where the signals combine!
A molecular secret, beautifully signed!

[Outro]
[Fade Out] [Powerful Outro] [Eerie Whispers] (Fading synth pads, with lingering, almost ghost-like "whispers" of a high-pitched, chemical-sounding synth, representing the fading scent trails)
Silent signals, on the ground they creep...
The unseen language, where the ant secrets sleep...
(Beat slowly dissolves, leaving only a few final, echoing synth notes)

songs.caption: This song reveals the amazing mechanism behind insect social life: pheromones. These are chemical signals released from specialized glands that act as a shared, invisible language for the entire colony. They create the crucial scented blueprints used for navigation (trail pheromones), marking food sources, coordinating complex tasks (like finding a new nest), and sending alarm signals. The song emphasizes how this decentralized molecular secret drives collective behavior. #Pheromones #Ants

songs.title: Drømmer
songs.tags: Edm
songs.prompt:
"**Vers 1:**
Ser på stjerner, ser på himmelen,
Veien er lang, men jeg vet hvor jeg vil hen.
Med drømmer i hjertet og mål i sikte,
Tar jeg steget, la ingenting forhindre.

**Pre-Chorus:**
Gjennom natten, lyser håpet sterkt,
Veien er vår, vi gir aldri opp, vi er klart.
Hør mitt rop, det er tid for å fly,
Vi skal nå toppen, vi skal skyhøyt opp i sky.

**Chorus:**
Vi drømmer stort, vi sikter høyt,
Med rytmen i hjertet, er vi aldri fornøyd.
Vi danser til drømmer, vi når våre mål,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Drop:**
Vi flyr, vi flyr, vi når våre drømmer,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Vers 2:**
Gjennom motgang, gjennom storm og vind,
Vi står sammen, vi finner alltid frem.
Med lys i øynene og tro i våre hjerter,
Vi gir alt, vi gir alt, vi gir aldri slipp på drømmer.

**Pre-Chorus:**
Gjennom natten, lyser håpet sterkt,
Veien er vår, vi gir aldri opp, vi er klart.
Hør mitt rop, det er tid for å fly,
Vi skal nå toppen, vi skal skyhøyt opp i sky.

**Chorus:**
Vi drømmer stort, vi sikter høyt,
Med rytmen i hjertet, er vi aldri fornøyd.
Vi danser til drømmer, vi når våre mål,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Drop:**
Vi flyr, vi flyr, vi når våre drømmer,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Bridge:**
Ingen grenser, ingen frykt, vi er fri,
I denne verden, er det bare deg og meg.
Vi ser fremover, vi ser mot stjerner,
Med tro på oss selv, vi er klare for alt.

**Chorus:**
Vi drømmer stort, vi sikter høyt,
Med rytmen i hjertet, er vi aldri fornøyd.
Vi danser til drømmer, vi når våre mål,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Drop:**
Vi flyr, vi flyr, vi når våre drømmer,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Outro:**
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige,
Med drømmer i hjertet, vi er uslåelige."

Veien er vår, vi gir aldri opp, vi er klart.
Hør mitt rop, det er tid for å fly,
Vi skal nå toppen, vi skal skyhøyt opp i sky.

**Chorus:**
Vi drømmer stort, vi sikter høyt,
Med rytmen i hjertet, er vi aldri fornøyd.
Vi danser til drømmer, vi når våre mål,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Drop:**
Vi flyr, vi flyr, vi når våre drømmer,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Vers 2:**
Gjennom motgang, gjennom storm og vind,
Vi står sammen, vi finner alltid frem.
Med lys i øynene og tro i våre hjerter,
Vi gir alt, vi gir alt, vi gir aldri slipp på drømmer.

**Pre-Chorus:**
Gjennom natten, lyser håpet sterkt,
Veien er vår, vi gir aldri opp, vi er klart.
Hør mitt rop, det er tid for å fly,
Vi skal nå toppen, vi skal skyhøyt opp i sky.

**Chorus:**
Vi drømmer stort, vi sikter høyt,
Med rytmen i hjertet, er vi aldri fornøyd.
Vi danser til drømmer, vi når våre mål,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Drop:**
Vi flyr, vi flyr, vi når våre drømmer,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Bridge:**
Ingen grenser, ingen frykt, vi er fri,
I denne verden, er det bare deg og meg.
Vi ser fremover, vi ser mot stjerner,
Med tro på oss selv, vi er klare for alt.

**Chorus:**
Vi drømmer stort, vi sikter høyt,
Med rytmen i hjertet, er vi aldri fornøyd.
Vi danser til drømmer, vi når våre mål,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Drop:**
Vi flyr, vi flyr, vi når våre drømmer,
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige.

**Outro:**
I nattens lys, vi er uovervinnelige,
Med drømmer i hjertet, vi er uslåelige."

songs.title: Lightbulb Moments
songs.tags: Uplifting indie pop with bright piano and orchestral build, inspired by Coldplay. Male vocals, warm tone, growing from gentle verses to anthemic choruses. D major, 72 BPM.
songs.prompt:
[Short Piano Intro]
*gentle piano notes*
(soft atmospheric sounds)

[Verse 1]
Blank stares across the room today
Another concept seems to slip away
Erasers moving, pencils down
Frustration painting every frown
But I won't give up, I'll find a way
To make these numbers, words, and ideas stay

[Pre-chorus]
Then suddenly I see it start
A spark within their eyes
That moment when it all falls into place

[Chorus - High Energy]
It's that lightbulb moment
When everything clicks
Like sunshine breaking through the clouds
Those lightbulb moments
When knowledge sticks
And silence turns to - "Now I get it!" -  out loud
(Oh, now they get it, now they get it)

[Verse 2]
Different methods, different pace
Every student, every face
Some need pictures, others need
To move around to plant the seed
Patient waiting, trying more
Till understanding opens doors

[Pre-chorus]
Then like magic, watch them soar
Beyond what came before
That instant when the puzzle pieces fit

[Chorus - High Energy]
It's that lightbulb moment
When everything clicks
Like sunshine breaking through the clouds
Those lightbulb moments
When knowledge sticks
And silence turns to - "Now I get it!" - out loud
(Oh, now they get it, now they get it)

[Bridge - Building Energy]
Every struggle leads to this
Every error, every miss
Builds the path to understanding
Now they're flying, now they're landing
Right where they were meant to be
(Building, building, watch them rise)
NOW THEY SEE!

[Final Chorus - Most Energy]
It's that lightbulb moment
When everything clicks
Like sunshine breaking through the clouds
Those lightbulb moments
When knowledge sticks
And silence turns to  - "Now I get it!" -  out loud
(They get it now, they really get it now!)

[Soft Outro]
And in these moments, I remember why
I chose to teach, to help them fly
*gentle piano fading*
(whispered) Those lightbulb moments...

[Fade Out]

songs.title: Telomerase Tango
songs.tags: Upbeat, synth-heavy pop with a dinosaur roar sample and a catchy chorus
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1 – Aging Cells]
[Female voice, weary]
My telomeres are shortening, each cell division’s theft,
Chromosome ends fraying—guess I’m aging like the rest.
Hayflick limit whispers, “You’ve got 50 splits left!”
Senescence sirens blaring—time to retire, decompress…

[Pre-Chorus – Enter Telomerase]
[Male voice, slick salesman tone]
“Wait! I’m telomerase—the time-bending rebel,
Reverse transcriptase swagger, I’ll rebuild your sequel!
Germ cells use me freely, stem cells keep me near,
Let’s crank up your lifespan—immortality, my dear!”*

[Chorus]
Telomerase tango! (Oh-oh!) Dance with DNA!
Splice your ends, cheat the clock, live another day!
Cancer’s dirty secret? I’m the enzyme they abuse,
But without me, evolution’s got no juice!

[Verse 2 – Immortality Hustle]
[Duet – Male & Female]
[Male] I’ll add T-T-A-G-G-G, extend your chromosome tails,
No more “end replication problem”—let’s break biology’s rails!
[Female] But why’s my body hiding you? Suppressed in every cell…
[Male] “Cause unchecked, I’m trouble—a carcinogen spell!”

[Rap Bridge – Cancer’s Side Hustle]
[Robotic voice, sinister beat drop]
“90% of tumors call me when they’re in strife,
Telomerase reactivation—eternal cancerous life!
No Hayflick, no limits, just divide and invade,
But hey—at least your telomeres won’t degrade…”

[Verse 3 – Evolutionary Twist]
[Female voice, sly]
Some critters cheat without you—ALT’s their backup plan,
Mice? They’ve got telomerase aplenty—short lifespan!
Humans? We’re stuck in the middle—you’re a blessing and a curse,
Aging’s price for not becoming a malignant universe!

[Outro – Jurassic Park Nod]
[Epic choir, T. rex roar]
“Life finds a way… to splice another day!”
Telomerase tango—chaos wrapped in DNA,
From embryo to tumor, we’re all just genes at play,
So raise your chromosome caps—let’s toast this twisted fray!

songs.title: Detachable Love (The Bladder Snail Song
songs.tags: Quirky indie pop with surf rock influences. , beachy vibes with a science twist. Add some whimsical synthesizer sounds for the marine atmosphere.
songs.prompt:
Main Lyrics:
Swimming through the coral reef so bright
Found a mate in the fading light
But when our loving time is done
Gotta leave my manhood, yes it's gone
'Cause I'm special, that's my style
Growing back in just a while

Chorus:
Throw away, grow again
Twenty-four hours and then
Ready for another round
Most amazing trick in town
Disposable love machine
Nature's craziest routine

Verse 2:
Hypselodoris, that's my name
Playing evolution's strangest game
Blue and purple, nudibranch so fine
Losing parts but feeling prime
Scientists just shake their head
At the life choices I've led

Bridge:
Autotomy ain't just for tails
When you're one of nature's special snails
Most animals keep their bits intact
But I've got a different contract
With biology's wild design
Breaking rules all the time

Final Chorus:
Throw away, grow again
Twenty-four hours and then
Ready for my next romance
Taking biology's weird chance
Who needs permanence anyway?
When you can regrow day by day

(Outro)
Just a nudibranch in the sea
Making history with my biology

songs.title: Not Your Typical Country Song
songs.tags: country acoustic melodic
songs.prompt:
[Verse]
Well, I ain't got a truck with massive tires, roaring down the lane,
No backyard BBQs, no hunting prize to hang.
I'm not spinning John Deere tales or chasin' whiskey shots,
Don't need a ten-gallon hat or a girl in cowboy boots—thanks a lot.

[Verse 2]
No dirt road anthems playin' on my radio,
No tales of heartbreak from that ol' rodeo.
Ain't singin' 'bout them wranglers, or the sunset on the ridge,
My life ain't a country playlist; it's just a different bridge.

[Chorus]
So here's to the folks who don't fit in 'round here,
Raisin' our own kind of bottle, with a different kind of cheer.
We ain't about them clichés, or tired ol' country tunes,
Dancin' to the beat of our hearts, under different moons.

[Verse 3]
No porch swing serenades, or moonshine in a jar,
Not every country boy's gotta strum an old guitar.
I ain't got no story 'bout a dog who ran away,
Just livin' life my own way, and that's more than okay.

[Verse 4]
Ain't worryin' 'bout them cowgirls or their sweet drawl,
I'm fine without a rodeo, or a football in the fall.
I get my kicks from city lights or a novel in my hand,
Who says a country life’s got to fit some kinda brand?

[Chorus]
So here's to the folks who don't fit in 'round here,
Raisin' our own kind of bottle, with a different kind of cheer.
We ain't about them clichés, or tired ol' country tunes,
Dancin' to the beat of our hearts, under different moons.

songs.title: Chemical Warfare
songs.tags: Energetic industrial rock with electronic elements and dramatic percussion breaks; incorporates abrupt dynamic shifts between quiet verses and explosive choruses, mirroring the beetle's defense mechan
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Hidden in the forest floor
Nature's tiny engineer
Unassuming, walking slow
Until danger comes too near

[Pre-Chorus]
Two chambers waiting
Chemistry in perfect time
A reaction building
Nature's perfect design

[Chorus]
BOOM! Chemical warfare
From a beetle so small
BOOM! Temperature rising
Watch the predators fall
Engineering perfection
A hundred degrees
The bombardier's protection
Is bringing them to their knees

[Verse 2]
Hydroquinones on standby
Hydrogen peroxide too
Catalysts and enzymes wait
For the signal to break through

[Pre-Chorus]
Two chambers combining
A volatile storm inside
The pressure is climbing
No place for threats to hide

[Repeat Chorus]

[Bridge]
Millions of years of evolution
Perfecting this chemical art
Tiny defender with explosive solutions
Tearing would-be predators apart
Nature's smallest soldier
With the hottest defense
A microscopic miracle
Making perfect chemical sense

[Final Chorus]

[Outro]
The bombardier beetle
Nature's tiny grenadier
Chemical perfection
Evolution's souvenired engineer

songs.title: Function Junction
songs.tags: Upbeat Pop Rock with educational lyrics, clear vocals, and a memorable chorus. Some calculator sounds in background. In the style of science-focused educational songs but with modern production.
songs.prompt:
[Short Instrumental Intro]
*beep* *beep* *beep*

[Verse 1]
When X goes in and Y comes out
That's what functions are about
On the coordinate plane we see
How graphs show reality

[Pre-chorus 1]
Linear functions are where we start
Y equals M times X plus B is smart
M shows how steep it's gonna be
B's where it meets the Y-axis, see?

[Chorus]
Functions, functions, mapping day and night!
Input X and Y shines bright
Find the zeros where Y hits zero
And X becomes our function hero
(Mathematics in motion!)

[Verse 2]
Quadratic functions curve with grace
X squared leads us through this space
A times X squared plus B times X plus C
Makes parabolas we can see

[Pre-chorus 2]
Maximum, minimum points we find
With derivatives we have in mind
Zeros through quadratic ways
The formula guides through the maze

[Chorus]
Functions, functions, mapping day and night!
Input X and Y shines bright
Find the zeros where Y hits zero
And X becomes our function hero
(Mathematics in motion!)

[Bridge]
Rational functions next in line
With fractions making curves so fine
Watch out when denominators fall
To zero, making asymptotes tall
Making graphs that tell it all!

[Verse 3]
Intersection points we seek
Where different graphs decide to meet
Solving equations with our minds
Solutions that we're bound to find

[Outro]
Every function tells a tale
Of patterns that will never fail
Through mathematics we can see
How numbers set our thinking free!

[Fade to end]
*calculator success beep*

songs.title: Vault Sweet Vault
songs.tags:
1940s traditional pop ballad
Medium-slow, gentle swing rhythm (♩=92)
Smooth male tenor lead with gentle vibrato, mid-Atlantic accent, theatrical projection
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1] [Soft, gentle delivery with clear enunciation, piano prominent]
Down in the Vault, where steel walls gleam
Safe from the world, or so it seems
The overseer says we're doing fine
Just don't ask what's happening topside
[Chorus] [Stronger projection, more vibrato, full accompaniment]
Vault sweet Vault, my underground home
Vault sweet Vault, where I'll never roam
They say the surface is nothing but trouble
So I'll stay right here in my nuclear bubble
[Verse 2] [Similar to first verse but slightly more intense]
Our jumpsuits blue, our smiles bright
Working our shifts both day and night
The reactor hums its lullaby
While centuries just pass us by
[Bridge] [Spoken with deep resonance, piano reduces to soft chords]
Sure, sometimes the pipes start leaking
And the robots get a mind of their own
But that's just life down here in Vault sweet Vault
Where every day's exactly the same as before
[Final Chorus] [Full voice, more emotionally expressive]
Vault sweet Vault, my underground home
Vault sweet Vault, where I'll never roam
Though sometimes I dream of the world up above
I'll stay right here in the Vault that I love
[Ending] [Gradually softer, more wistful]
(Gradual slowdown with final piano flourish and gentle fade)

songs.title: The Sperm Saga: A Journey to the Egg
songs.tags: Upbeat, empowering melody, high fidelity
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
In the testis, where the magic begins,
Spermatogenesis is about to spin.
Mitosis, mei-osis, it’s quite a ride,
Making little swimmers with a lot of pride.

Haploid cells, they’re the name of the game,
With 23 chromosomes, they’re not the same.
Spermatids mature, they grow a tail,
Now they’re ready to set sail!

[Chorus]
Oh, it’s a sperm’s life, a wiggly quest,
Swimming through the body, doing their best.
From the epididymis to the great beyond,
Millions of brothers, but only one’s the chosen one!

[Verse 2]
Through the vas deferens, they take a tour,
Mixing with fluids, they’re ready for more.
Prostate and seminal vesicles join the mix,
Creating a cocktail that does the trick!

Out they go, on a mission so grand,
Through the cervix, they’ll make their stand.
Acrosomes armed, they’re ready to fight,
To penetrate the egg and ignite new life!

[Chorus]
Oh, it’s a sperm’s life, a wiggly quest,
Swimming through the body, doing their best.
From the epididymis to the great beyond,
Millions of brothers, but only one’s the chosen one!

[Bridge]
The egg’s in the fallopian tube, just waiting there,
With a zona pellucida, it’s quite the affair.
The fastest sperm breaks through the wall,
Fertilization happens, and that’s not all!

Zygote forms, the journey’s begun,
Cell division starts, and it’s so much fun.
Implantation in the uterus, a new life’s made,
And that’s how the sperm’s journey gets played!

[Chorus]
Oh, it’s a sperm’s life, a wiggly quest,
Swimming through the body, doing their best.
From the epididymis to the great beyond,
Millions of brothers, but only one’s the chosen one!

[Outro]
So here’s to the sperm, so small yet so brave,
Their journey’s the reason we all behave.
From meiosis to baby’s first cry,
It’s the circle of life, and that’s no lie!

Creating a cocktail that does the trick!

Out they go, on a mission so grand,
Through the cervix, they’ll make their stand.
Acrosomes armed, they’re ready to fight,
To penetrate the egg and ignite new life!

[Chorus]
Oh, it’s a sperm’s life, a wiggly quest,
Swimming through the body, doing their best.
From the epididymis to the great beyond,
Millions of brothers, but only one’s the chosen one!

[Bridge]
The egg’s in the fallopian tube, just waiting there,
With a zona pellucida, it’s quite the affair.
The fastest sperm breaks through the wall,
Fertilization happens, and that’s not all!

Zygote forms, the journey’s begun,
Cell division starts, and it’s so much fun.
Implantation in the uterus, a new life’s made,
And that’s how the sperm’s journey gets played!

[Chorus]
Oh, it’s a sperm’s life, a wiggly quest,
Swimming through the body, doing their best.
From the epididymis to the great beyond,
Millions of brothers, but only one’s the chosen one!

[Outro]
So here’s to the sperm, so small yet so brave,
Their journey’s the reason we all behave.
From meiosis to baby’s first cry,
It’s the circle of life, and that’s no lie!

songs.title: Kronologisk Orden
songs.tags: experimental, funk
songs.prompt:
[Verse]
Jeg sitter her på pulten min
Ser ut over rotet mitt
Man skulle ikke tro det
Men det finnes en orden

[Verse 2]
Livet her er kronologisk
Selv om det er kaos her
Papirer overalt nå
Men de ligger systematisk

[Chorus]

Alle ting på sin plass
Kronologisk orden her

Men jeg finner alltid vei
Gjennom kaos skaper jeg
Rytmen i min egen greie

[Chorus]
Kronologisk orden her
Selv i rotet finner jeg det klart
Alle ting på sin plass
Kronologisk orden her

[Bridge]
Ser du ikke mønsteret?
Gjennom alt finnes det vei
Selv i rotet finner jeg
Kronologisk orden hver dag

songs.title: Alene på skolen
songs.tags: Chilled Lofi, Ambient, Downtempo
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
(Guitar solo)

[Verse]
Jeg sitter her på skolen, og er mutters alene  
Jeg trodde jeg var tidlig ute  
Min analoge klokke viste ti på sju  
Og jeg skyndte meg til skolen i en fart  

[Chorus]
Nå sitter jeg her og er mutters alene  
Jeg hadde tatt feil av tiden  
Klokken er åtte på kvelden  

[Verse]
Mørket senker seg, lyset fra skjermen  
Tenker på hva jeg kunne gjort, hvor jeg skulle vært  
Alt er stille rundt meg, bare klokkens tikking  
Det er ensomt å være så feil i tiden  

[Chorus]
Nå sitter jeg her og er mutters alene  
Jeg hadde tatt feil av tiden  
Klokken er åtte på kvelden  

[Bridge]
Lyset utenfor er vagt, skygger danser langs veggen  
Jeg ønsker jeg kunne skru tilbake, finne veien hjem igjen  
Men tiden er en flod, som skyller alt forbi  
Jeg må finne roen, i dette øyeblikkets tid  

[Outro]
(Guitar solo, fading out)

songs.title: Vennskap
songs.tags: rap
songs.prompt:
"
Når dagen er grå og trist,
er du alltid der, min beste venn.
Vi ler og vi har det fint,
sammen kan vi klare alt igjen

Vennskap er som solskinn,
lyser opp hver eneste dag.
Sammen er vi aldri alene,
vi deler glede, sorg og lag.
Gjennom storm og stille hav,
står vi sammen, hånd i hånd.

Du er min klippe, mitt anker,
sammen skaper vi et bånd.
Vennskap er som solskinn,
lyser opp hver eneste dag.
Sammen er vi aldri alene,
vi deler glede, sorg og lag.

Når verden føles tung og stor,
er du der og gir meg mot.
Vi møter alt med et smil,
for vennskapet vårt er så godt.

Vennskap er som solskinn,
lyser opp hver eneste dag.
Sammen er vi aldri alene,
vi deler glede, sorg og lag."

songs.title: Han er sterk
songs.tags: Country
songs.prompt:
"**Vers 1:**
Han sitter stille, i sitt eget sted,
Ser på verden med et annet fred.
Med et hjerte fullt av drøm og tro,
Ingen grenser for hva han kan gjøre og nå.

**Refreng:**
Han er sterk, han er fri,
Med en sjel så rik som poesi.
Han stråler klart, han skinner stor,
En inspirasjon, en sannhet vi tror.

**Vers 2:**
Han finner ro i de små detaljer,
Ser skjønnhet der andre går forbi som skyer.
I hver farge, i hver linje sett,
Ser han kunst som vi andre har glemt.

**Refreng:**
Han er sterk, han er fri,
Med en sjel så rik som poesi.
Han stråler klart, han skinner stor,
En inspirasjon, en sannhet vi tror.

**Bro:**
La oss feire forskjell som gjør oss hel,
I hans øyne ser vi verden med sjel.
Han viser vei med sitt store hjerte,
En påminnelse om kjærlighetens verd.

**Refreng:**
Han er sterk, han er fri,
Med en sjel så rik som poesi.
Han stråler klart, han skinner stor,
En inspirasjon, en sannhet vi tror.

**Utgang:**
Han er en stjerne i nattens ro,
Med sitt lys viser han oss hvor.
Vi er alle forskjellige, og det er bra,
For han viser styrke i å være den han er hver dag."

songs.title: The Switches of Life
songs.tags: Electronic-classical fusion with minimalist rhythms evolving throughout.  Use of layered synthesizers to represent gene cascades, with precise rhythmic patter
songs.prompt:
Main Lyrics:
In every cell the story waits
In segments stacked like ancient gates
Hox genes sleeping, then awake
Building bodies, no mistake

Chorus:
Turn it on, turn it off
Like a dance that time forgot
Same old genes in different ways
Making wings or making waves
Evolution's favorite tool
Writing nature's golden rules

Verse 2:
From fruit flies up to human minds
The same controllers you will find
Moving parts like puppet strings
Making legs or making wings
Changed expression, changed in time
Different species down the line

Bridge:
Enhancers binding, right on cue
Telling genes just what to do
One small change in regulation
Sparks a brand new innovation
Building forms we've never seen
From the same old gene machine

Final Verse:
So when you see a butterfly
Or watch a whale swim through the sea
Remember how the switches play
To shape our bodies day by day
Ancient tools in modern use
Setting patterns, breaking loose

songs.title: Autumn Blues
songs.tags: Blues, female vocals
songs.prompt:
[Start]
[Piano Instrumental]
[Intro]
Whispering winds, the leaves fall down,
In shades of gold and crimson gown.

[Piano Instrumental]


[Verse 1]
Walking through the autumn breeze,
Colors dance among the trees,
Nature paints with golden ease,
Colors dance among the trees
(Colors dance among the trees)

[Build up]
Chill in the air, whispers grow,
Autumn's song begins to flow.

[Verse 2]
Frosty mornings greet the dawn,
Pumpkin spice and cozy yarn,
Sweaters worn and fires warm,
Pumpkin spice and cozy yarn
(Pumpkin spice and cozy yarn)

[Pre-Chorus]
Leaves in flight, a swirling show,
Autumn’s magic starts to glow.

[Chorus]
Feel the season's gentle sway,
In the heart of autumn’s play,
Nature’s art on full display.

[Piano Break]


[Bridge]
Golden hues, the sunset's light,
Days grow short, and fades the night.

[Verse 3]
Memories in every leaf
Moments bright, though time is brief,
Harvest brings a sweet relief,
Moments bright, though time is brief
(Moments bright, though time is brief)

[Chorus]
Feel the season's gentle sway,
In the heart of autumn’s play,
Nature’s art on full display.

[Outro]
Whispering winds, the leaves fall down,
In shades of gold and crimson gown.

[End]

songs.title: Silent phone, Silent Night
songs.tags: Gentle acoustic guitar, soft piano, phone notification sounds, calm female vocals
songs.prompt:
[Short Instrumental Intro]
[Verse]
Silent phone, holy night
Family time, what a sight
Round the table, phones face down
Dad's new rule, makes us frown
Try to fight the urge to check
Try to fight the urge to check
[Pre-chorus]
Notifications pile up high
FOMO hitting, don't know why
What if something's trending now?
Instagram won't miss me, wow
Learning how to be present here
Learning to be present here
[Chorus]
Silent phone, peaceful meal
Conversations start to feel
Like the Christmas days of old
Stories shared and jokes are told
Finally connecting right
Finally connecting right
[Bridge]
Grandma shares her memories
Of the days before our screens
When they sang and played and talked
Now we scroll instead of walk
Time to make a change tonight
Time to make this dinner right
[Verse]
Silent phone, starlit sky
Hours passed, time did fly
Didn't miss a single post
What I gained mattered most
Family time wins tonight
Family time wins tonight
[Outro]
Silent phone, holy night
Everything turned out alright
[Fade to End]

songs.title: Drømmer og sånt da
songs.tags: Norwegian folk
songs.prompt:
"Vi står på terskelen til noe nytt, med håp i våre hender
Vår verden er full av muligheter, med venner som forstår
Vi deler hemmeligheter, ler og gråter sammen
I denne tiden av våre liv, er det ingenting som holder oss tilbake

Refreng:

Vi er ungdom, vi drømmer stort
Med vennskap som vår flamme, når vi våre mål
Vi finner styrke i hverandre, tar hver dag som den kommer
Med hjerter fylt av håp, skal vi skinne sammen

Vers 2:

Hver dag er en reise, vi lærer noe nytt
Med venner ved vår side, føles alt så riktig
Vi planlegger fremtiden, ser mot det ukjente
Med tro på oss selv, vet vi at vi kan få det til

Refreng:

Vi er ungdom, vi drømmer stort
Med vennskap som vår flamme, når vi våre mål
Vi finner styrke i hverandre, tar hver dag som den kommer
Med hjerter fylt av håp, skal vi skinne sammen

Bro:

Selv når skyene samler seg, og veien blir tung
Står vi sammen, holder ut, gir aldri opp
For i vennskap finner vi lys, i drømmer finner vi vei
Vi ser fremover, ingen hindring er for høy

Refreng:

Vi er ungdom, vi drømmer stort
Med vennskap som vår flamme, når vi våre mål
Vi finner styrke i hverandre, tar hver dag som den kommer
Med hjerter fylt av håp, skal vi skinne sammen

Outtro:

(Instrumental outro med akustisk gitar og lett piano)

Venner og drømmer, vi er klare for alt
Vi tar skrittet mot fremtiden, med tro på vår kraft
Vi er ungdom, vi drømmer stort
Sammen skal vi skinne, sammen når vi våre mål"

songs.title: Five Centuries (The Greenland Shark's Tale)
songs.tags: Atmospheric post-rock with doom metal influences. Extremely slow tempo (like the shark's movement), deep bass drones, glacial chord progressions.
songs.prompt:
Main Lyrics:
Greenland shark in endless night
Half a millennium passing by
Still swimming, through countless tides
Ancient eyes, parasite's bride

Chorus:
Five hundred years beneath the waves
Moving slower than my age
Poisoned flesh and tiny brain
Time means nothing in my veins

Verse 2:
A century and a half to breed
Patience is all I need
Watching empires rise and fall
While I grow just inches tall
In waters dark and cold as time
History's witness in my prime

Bridge:
They catch me by mistake sometimes
Turn me into fermented lines
But I've seen the ice caps grow
A thousand storms come and go
I'll still be here when you're all gone
Just swimming, swimming on

Chorus (repeat):
Five hundred years beneath the waves
Moving slower than my age
Poisoned flesh and tiny brain
Time means nothing in my veins

Final Verse:
In the depths where glaciers weep
Wisdom comes to those who sleep
Through changes fast and changes slow
There's more than humans need to know
Just drifting through the endless blue
(fade out)Time itself is nothing new

songs.title: Country Song
songs.tags: Country
songs.prompt:
Verse 1:
Got my pickup truck broken down
Dog just left me, feeling like a clown
Guitar's missing three strings somehow
But that's what makes it country now
Chorus:
I'm singing 'bout dirt roads and beer
Throwing in a tractor here and there
Mentioning my boots and my John Deere
Adding twang cause that's what folks wanna hear
Verse 2:
Every other line's about my ex
Got some fishing and my pickup truck next
Rhyming "heart" with "apart" again
Cause that's what makes a country song, amen
Chorus:
I'm singing 'bout dirt roads and beer
Throwing in a tractor here and there
Mentioning my boots and my John Deere
Adding twang cause that's what folks wanna hear
Bridge:
Need a reference to mama's cooking
And the good Lord up above
Gotta mention Friday night lights
And that small-town high school love
Final Chorus:
I'm singing 'bout dirt roads and beer
Throwing in a tractor here and there
Mentioning my boots and my John Deere
Adding twang cause that's what makes it country round here

songs.title: Oogenesis (Remastered)
songs.tags: Soul, female voice
songs.prompt:
[Piano]
[Verse 1]
In the womb it begins, a journey so grand,
O'ogonia forming, life’s delicate strand.
Mitosis creating, the starting of life,
In the fetal ovary, potential is rife.

[Pre-Chorus]
Chromosomes pair up, crossing over so fine,
Creating an ovum, with a future design.

[Chorus] [Soulful Hook]
O'ogenesis, where the life ignites,
In the quiet cells, under soft moonlight.
From o'ogonia to ova, shining bright,
In the dance of life, it all feels right.

[Verse 2]
At birth she carries millions inside,
Primary o'ocytes, in a dormant tide.
Resting in follicles, waiting their turn,
Through childhood years, patiently they yearn.

[Pre-Chorus]
Hormones signal, pathways align,
Bringing new cycles, in rhythm and time.

[Chorus] [Soulful Hook]
O'ogenesis, where the life ignites,
In the quiet cells, under soft moonlight.
From o'ogonia to ova, shining bright,
In the dance of life, it all feels right.

[Bridge]
Puberty awakens, the cycles commence,
Follicles maturing, breaking their silence.
Monthly an ovum, ready to release,
In the fallopian tube, the journey’s piece.

[Chorus] [Big Finish]
O'ogenesis, where the life ignites,
In the quiet cells, under soft moonlight.
From o'ogonia to ova, shining bright,
In the dance of life, it all feels right.

[Outro] [Fade to End]
From fetus to woman, in nature's sweet kiss,
The miracle of life, through o'ogenesis.

songs.title: The Ova Opera: A Symphony of Sisters
songs.tags: Upbeat, empowering melody
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Deep in the pelvis, where the magic’s designed,
Two ovaries rock like a powerhouse shrine!
Each month, a follicle takes center stage,
Prepping an egg for its grand escapade.

Estrogen’s rising, the hormones are lit,
Thickening linings? The uterus commits!
The fallopian tubes wave like a beckoning hand,
“Come this way, egg—we’ve got plans so grand!”

[Chorus]
Oh-oh-oh, it’s the ova opera!
A monthly rhythm, no need for a proper-a!
From vulva to cervix, it’s a sacred groove,
The vagina’s the highway where life starts to move!

[Verse 2]
Now ovulation’s the star of the show,
The egg takes off like a slow-motion glow!
If sperm hits the scene with a flagellum groove,
They’ll meet in the tube for a fertilization move.

But if there’s no date, the corpus luteum sighs,
Progesterone drops, and the tissue says, “Goodbye!”
Menstruation arrives—it’s a crimson tide,
A fresh start brewing with biology as your guide!

[Chorus]
Oh-oh-oh, it’s the ova opera!
A monthly rhythm, no need for a proper-a!
From vulva to cervix, it’s a sacred groove,
The vagina’s the highway where life starts to move!

[Bridge]
Shout-out to the clitoris—8,000 nerves strong!
It’s not just for babies; it’s where pleasure belongs.
The labia? They’re guardians, unique in their flair,
No two are alike, and that’s beyond compare!

The cervical mucus—it’s slick, it’s a clue,
When it’s egg-white stretchy, there’s fertility brew!
And the endometrium? Thick, lush, and kind,
A cozy nest waiting for life to entwine!

[Glandular Glory Section]
Now let’s talk glands—they’re the backstage crew,
Keeping the system running like a well-oiled debut!
The pituitary gland’s the boss of the show,
Sending out FSH and LH to make things grow!

The adrenal glands pitch in with a hormonal flair,
Adding androgens to the mix—just a touch, they swear!
The mammary glands? They’re ready to feed,
Prepping for lactation when there’s a baby in need!

And don’t forget the Bartholin’s glands, keeping things slick,
Lubricating the vulva—quick trick, quick trick!
The Skene’s glands? They’re the orgasmic crew,
Adding to pleasure with their fluid debut!

[Chorus]
Oh-oh-oh, it’s the ova opera!
A monthly rhythm, no need for a proper-a!
From vulva to cervix, it’s a sacred groove,
The vagina’s the highway where life starts to move!

[Outro]
So here’s to the vulva, the tubes, and the flow,
A cyclical dance only bodies can know.
From puberty’s kick to menopause’s spark,
The female form’s a masterpiece—own your remark!

songs.title: Battery Blues
songs.tags: aggressive high-energy rap
songs.prompt:
[Verse]
Phone cryin', batt'ry dyin', searchin' in the dark,
Ports don't fit, every charger miss, feelin’ like a lark.
Micro USB or Type-C? Madness in my hand,
Plugged in wrong, every socket gone, hate this twisted land.

[Verse 2]
Boy, frantic, panic hit, twenty percent left,
Desk mess, cords a tangle, feeling like a theft.
Charge me up, electric high, power's on a quest,
Wires sprawling, kitchen calling, can’t take this chest.

[Chorus]
Charger hunt, fool’s errand, phone about to drop,
Life on the brink, no juice link, gotta make it stop.
Plug in the right one, hands are shakin' fast,
Search the sea, find the key, make this nightmare pass.

[Verse 3]
Drawer's graveyard, relic cords, no solution there,
Old gen, wrong end, givin' me despair.
Lightning strikes, needs precise, no common ground,
Scrambling, gambling, clock's spinnin' round.

[Verse 4]
Friends got cables, ask 'em quick, save me from this drought,
Texts sent, replies read, no lifelines about.
Out the door, runnin' fast, to the bodega late,
Shelf scannin', last-minute plan, battery's fate await.

[Chorus]
Charger hunt, fool’s errand, phone about to drop,
Life on the brink, no juice link, gotta make it stop.
Plug in the right one, hands are shakin' fast,
Search the sea, find the key, make this nightmare pass.

songs.title: Epigenetic Blues
songs.tags: Slow, smoky 12-bar blues with a gravelly vocal tone and melancholic harmonica riffs
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Well, I got the epigenetic blues, down in my DNA,
Methylation mafia shut my genes off today.
Grandpa smoked, now I’m methylated—thanks for the trauma, fam,
Can’t scream ‘cause histones got me wrapped like a spam!

Oh, these chromatin loops, they’re tighter than my genes,
Acetylation’s on vacation—where’s my transcription machine?
My promoter’s blocked, enhancers outta sight,
It’s a gene expression mess in the pale moonlight.

[Chorus]
Ep-i-ge-net-ic bluuues… (oh yeah)
Tags on my DNA, I’m singin’ the bad news.
Environment’s a bully, messin’ with my code,
Can’t rewrite my past, but maybe… future’s a different road?

[Verse 2]
Mama ate stress for breakfast, passed it down in her milk,
Glucocorticoid receptors got me wired to freak at silk.
Imprinted genes are fightin’—Daddy’s vs. Mom’s,
It’s a custody battle in my nucleosome pods!

Oh, that histone de-acetylase—cold as ice,
Tightens up my chromatin, no room for splice.
I’m a CpG island, stranded in a methyl sea,
Silenced like a library book nobody wants to read.

[Chorus]
Ep-i-ge-net-ic bluuues… (oh yeah)
Tags on my DNA, I’m singin’ the bad news.
Environment’s a bully, messin’ with my code,
Can’t rewrite my past, but maybe… future’s a different road?

[Bridge]
[Harmonica wails]
I tried resveratrol, ate kale by the ton,
But D-n-m-t-1 just laughed—“You think you’ve won?”
Dutch Hunger Winter echoes in my veins,
Grandma’s famine scars written in my insulin chains…

[Verse 3]
Now my telomeres are frayin’, aging fast and mean,
Thanks to all those stressors I never chose to seen.
Transposable elements—they’re lurkin’ in the dark,
But heterochromatin’s got ‘em quiet… for now, at least.

[Outro]
So here’s to methyl donors, folate, and hope,
Maybe one day science’ll help me cope.
‘Til then, I’ll wail these epigenetic blues,
‘Cause my genes ain’t fate—they’re just… slightly used.

I’m a CpG island, stranded in a methyl sea,
Silenced like a library book nobody wants to read.

[Chorus]
Ep-i-ge-net-ic bluuues… (oh yeah)
Tags on my DNA, I’m singin’ the bad news.
Environment’s a bully, messin’ with my code,
Can’t rewrite my past, but maybe… future’s a different road?

[Bridge]
[Harmonica wails]
I tried resveratrol, ate kale by the ton,
But D-n-m-t-1 just laughed—“You think you’ve won?”
Dutch Hunger Winter echoes in my veins,
Grandma’s famine scars written in my insulin chains…

[Verse 3]
Now my telomeres are frayin’, aging fast and mean,
Thanks to all those stressors I never chose to seen.
Transposable elements—they’re lurkin’ in the dark,
But heterochromatin’s got ‘em quiet… for now, at least.

[Outro]
So here’s to methyl donors, folate, and hope,
Maybe one day science’ll help me cope.
‘Til then, I’ll wail these epigenetic blues,
‘Cause my genes ain’t fate—they’re just… slightly used.

Making sugars for all

[Chorus]

Oh my
Oh my
Nature's way of living
How the plants thrive
Photosynthesis

Feeding all the living things

songs.title: Lost Charger Blues
songs.tags: hard-hitting beats aggressive rap
songs.prompt:
[Verse]
Phone's on red, down to single digits,
Searchin' for a charger, feelin' so livid,
Under couch cushions, it's a mystery,
Feels like a quest from ancient history.

[Verse 2]
USB-C, Lightning or micro,
Ports everywhere, but it's a psycho,
Diggin' through drawers, tangled-up wires,
Frustration’s fuelin', burnin' like wildfire.

[Chorus]
Charger hunt, it's a modern-day odyssey,
Battery low, feelin' the hypocrisy,
Tablets, phones, tech diversities,
One plug to rule 'em, failin' on purpose, G.

[Verse 3]
Frantic now, need that juice fix,
Phone's beepin' warnings, pull off some tricks,
Find an adapter, it's a twisted tale,
Gadget jungle, tech retail sale.

[Verse 4]
Neighbors’ house storm, beg and plead,
Need that power, but can’t succeed,
Phone’s about to die, screen gets dim,
World disconnects, future lookin' grim.

[Bridge]
Dreamin' of a time, one universal cord,
Tech advancing, shouldn’t be this hard,
Battery’s fate, hanging by a thread,
Feel the stress, wake up in cold sweat.
