songs.title: Lysosome’s Breakup Anthem (Decomposition Edition)
songs.tags: Punk rock with acidic energy, guttural vocals, and a post-mortem breakdown
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Hey, trashbag ex-organelle! I’m done with your crap,
Your undigested junk’s cloggin’ up my map!
I’ve got hydrolytic enzymes—p-H 4.6,
Time to decompose your lies, turn your carbs to glucose sticks!

You’re a misfolded protein, a dysfunctional mess,
I’ll tag you with ubiquitin—cellular stress arrest!
Autophagosome squad’s rollin’ up in a vesicle,
Gonna digest your drama, make it recyclable!

[Pre-Chorus]
I’m the decomposition queen, breaking down your remains,
From worn-out mitochondria to viral chains!
Sugars, lipids, proteins—I’ll grind ‘em to dust,
Nutrient recycling? Baby, that’s a must!

[Chorus]
Lysosome’s breaking up! (Hey!) Decomposition rage!
I’m the cell’s trash compactor—clearing death’s debris stage!
You’re a lipofuscin stain, I’m a prote-ase star,
Rotting your regrets—I’m p-H SUPERSTAR!

[Verse 2]
Remember when you flooded me with R-O-S and strife?
I’m done being your suicide sac for life!
Phagocytosis called—they’ll eat your decay,
While I’m chillin’ in the cytoplasm, sweeping death away!

You left residual bodies cluttering my space,
But I’m the breakdown squad—this dumpster’s erased!
Go haunt the Golgi, or the E-R’s nightshift,
Decomposing your drama—acid don’t forgive!

[Bridge – Life vs. Death]
(Eerie bassline, spoken-word interlude)
“In life, I’m precise—p-H 4.6, acidic reign,
Digesting your junk, keeping the cell sane.
But when you DIE? Lactic acid floods the scene,
No ATP to pump protons—p-H turns obscene!”

[Breakdown – Post-Mortem Chaos]
(Frantic guitars, snarling vocals)
Now the cell’s a corpse, lactic acid’s on the rise,
Mitochondria flatlined—no energy surprise.
My membrane ruptures, prote-ases break free,
Autolysis kicks in—digesting what’s left of me!

“No more compartmentalization—enzymes eat their own home,
Tissues turn to soup, bones stand alone.
I’m the lysosome ghost, post-mortem decay,
Breaking down the body—nature’s recycle play!”

(Sound effect: Distorted pH meter beep escalating into a rupture noise)

[Outro]
So here’s my proton pump middle finger salute,
I’m the rot-revolution—you’re just cellular soot!
In life, I cleaned your mess; in death, I’m unrestrained,
Auto-lytic fury—till nothing but atoms remain!

songs.title: Ways to Die in the Verse
songs.tags: Upbeat country-folk comedy
songs.prompt:
Verse 1:
Stepped out my ship just a little too quick
Fell through the planet, that did the trick
Climbing down a ladder, feeling pretty brave
Till physics glitched and sent me to my grave
Chorus:
Oh, the many ways to die
In this buggy verse we fly
No need for pirates in the black
When elevators strike back!
Verse 2:
Train to New Babbage was going just fine
Till I clipped through the wall, left my body behind
Tried to quantum jump across Stanton's face
Now I'm spinning forever in deep space
(Repeat Chorus)
Verse 3:
Walked through my ship with my coffee in hand
Hit an invisible wall, not part of the plan
Ragdolled through the hull, what a sight to see
Now I'm doing backflips in E-V-A for free
Bridge:
Sometimes you spawn without a head
Sometimes you're inexplicably dead
Bedroom spawns can be quite mean
When you load in through your bed's machine
Final Verse:
Got killed by the ramp on my own MSR
Tried to park my ROC in a ship too far
But we love it anyway, that's just how it be
In this alpha state of our space odyssey

songs.title: Palmer
songs.tags: Rock
songs.prompt:
"
På en øy der solen alltid skinner,
Palmer vaier, brisen synger minner.
Kokosnøtter faller fra toppen av trær,
Vi lever livet her, uten noen bekymringer.


Rock n' roll blant palmer og kokos,
Rytmen av havet og bølgenes kor.
Gitaren skriker, trommene dundrer,
Vi danser under stjernene, hører vinden sus.


Strandfest, sand mellom tærne,
Kokosdrinker, vi har ingen barrierer.
Musikken sprer seg som ild i natten,
Palmer svever, vi er fanget i rytmen.


Rock n' roll blant palmer og kokos,
Rytmen av havet og bølgenes kor.
Gitaren skriker, trommene dundrer,
Vi danser under stjernene, hører vinden sus.


Solnedgangen maler himmelen i gull,
Vi er fri, lar musikken ta kontroll.
Palmer og kokos, vårt paradis,
Vi synger høyt, mister oss i hver eneste viss.


Rock n' roll blant palmer og kokos,
Rytmen av havet og bølgenes kor.
Gitaren skriker, trommene dundrer,
Vi danser under stjernene, hører vinden sus.


Palmer vaier, natten blir til dag,
Vi lever evig, i en rockende magi.
Kokos og rock n' roll, vårt evige slag,
Under himmelen, i vårt paradis, her er vi fri."

songs.title: Eternal Dance
songs.tags:
Pop Duet, Upbeat, Energetic
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE INFORMATION
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Pop]
[STYLE: Catchy, Uplifting, Dynamic]
[MOOD: Playful, Exciting, Intriguing]
[VOCALS: Male and Female]
[ARRANGEMENT: Fast tempo, Rhythmic verses, Melodic choruses, Harmonious bridges]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Electric guitar, Synthesizers, Drums, Bass]
[TEMPO: Fast, 120-130 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Polished, Crisp vocals, High-energy, Radio-friendly]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Verse, Pre-Chorus, Chorus, Bridge, Chorus, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: Energetic throughout, Strong build-ups, Powerful releases, Smooth transitions]
[EMOTIONS: Excitement, Curiosity, Playfulness, Determination]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Intro]
(Male & Female)
Ooh, ooh, yeah, yeah

[Verse 1]
[Male]
In the shadows, we both grow,
An endless dance, we ebb and flow,
You take, I give, the balance tight,
In the game of life, we fight.

[Pre-Chorus]
[Female]
But every time you think you’ve won,
I find a way to overcome,
We adapt, we change, we see,
The way we move, this destiny.

[Chorus]
[Male & Female]
We’re locked in this embrace,
A never-ending chase,
You and me, eternally,
Parasite and host, we’re free.

[Verse 2]
[Female]
From the dawn, we’ve shared this fate,
Evolution’s grand debate,
You evolve, I counteract,
In this spiral, no turning back.

[Pre-Chorus]
[Male]
But every time I feel your grasp,
I find the strength, I make it last,
We adapt, we change, we thrive,
In this cycle, we survive.

[Chorus]
[Male & Female]
We’re locked in this embrace,
A never-ending chase,
You and me, eternally,
Parasite and host, we’re free.

[Bridge]
[Male & Female]
Together we rise, together we fall,
In this dance, we heed the call,
Step by step, we twist and turn,
In this fire, we both burn.

[Chorus]
[Male & Female]
We’re locked in this embrace,
A never-ending chase,
You and me, eternally,
Parasite and host, we’re free.

[Outro]
(Male & Female)
Ooh, ooh, yeah, yeah
In this cycle, we will be,
Forever bound, eternally.

songs.title: Invisible Giants of the Sea
songs.tags: Atmospheric Indie Rock with cinematic elements, featuring ethereal female vocals, melodic bass, and subtle electronic undertones.
songs.prompt:
[Short Instrumental Intro]
*waves crashing bubbling water*
[Verse 1 - Gentle]
Millions in a teaspoon, countless in the waves
The smallest of all sailors in their watery graves
More numerous than stars that light the darkest night
Invisible armies keeping oceans running right
[Pre-chorus - Building]
From the surface down to darkness
Where no light has ever shown
Lives a force that shapes our planet
In ways we've barely known
[Chorus - Powerful]
They're the invisible giants of the sea
Though microscopic, they set the waters free
From smallest to largest, nature's grand design
Marine viruses keeping oceans fine
(keeping oceans alive)
[Melodic Interlude]
*deep ocean sounds*
[Verse 2 - Dynamic]
When they hunt bacteria, nutrients cascade
Like underwater fireworks in nature's parade
Feeding tiny plankton in the endless blue
Starting life's great cycle, making Earth renew
[Bridge - Atmospheric]
Deep beneath the surface
Where mysteries unfold
These viral architects
Shape life's story untold
(shape life's story untold)
[Final Chorus - Epic]
They're the invisible giants of the sea
(giants of the deep blue sea)
Though microscopic, they set the waters free
(setting all life free)
From smallest to largest, nature's grand design
Marine viruses keeping oceans fine
[Outro - Contemplative]
*gentle waves*
(whispered) From tiniest to mighty
All connected in the blue
These invisible giants
Make our ocean dreams come true
[Fade to End]

songs.title: The Dance of Creation
songs.tags: hi-fi, male singer, rap, powerful, bass
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
Yo, let's break it down, the biology sound,
From erection to fertilization, knowledge profound.
Listen up, kids, it's a natural flow,
[Female voice]The body's process, now let's go!

[Verse 1]
Blood flowin' down, to the cavernous zone,
Nerves get excited, yeah, the magic's on.
Nitric oxide’s the key, arteries expand,
[Female voice]Erection's here, it's nature's grand plan.

[Pre-Chorus] 
Hormones in the mix, testosterone’s the lead,
Physical response, it's all guaranteed.

[Chorus] 
Erection, ejaculation, fertilization,
The body's symphony in perfect coordination.
Nature’s design, no hesitation,

songs.title: Invisible Giants of the Sea
songs.tags: Atmospheric Indie Rock with cinematic elements, featuring ethereal female vocals, melodic bass, and subtle electronic undertones.
songs.prompt:
[Short Instrumental Intro]
*waves crashing bubbling water*
[Verse 1 - Gentle]
Millions in a teaspoon, countless in the waves
The smallest of all sailors in their watery graves
More numerous than stars that light the darkest night
Invisible armies keeping oceans running right
[Pre-chorus - Building]
From the surface down to darkness
Where no light has ever shown
Lives a force that shapes our planet
In ways we've barely known
[Chorus - Powerful]
They're the invisible giants of the sea
Though microscopic, they set the waters free
From smallest to largest, nature's grand design
Marine viruses keeping oceans fine
(keeping oceans alive)
[Melodic Interlude]
*deep ocean sounds*
[Verse 2 - Dynamic]
When they hunt bacteria, nutrients cascade
Like underwater fireworks in nature's parade
Feeding tiny plankton in the endless blue
Starting life's great cycle, making Earth renew
[Bridge - Atmospheric]
Deep beneath the surface
Where mysteries unfold
These viral architects
Shape life's story untold
(shape life's story untold)
[Final Chorus - Epic]
They're the invisible giants of the sea
(giants of the deep blue sea)
Though microscopic, they set the waters free
(setting all life free)
From smallest to largest, nature's grand design
Marine viruses keeping oceans fine
[Outro - Contemplative]
*gentle waves*
(whispered) From tiniest to mighty
All connected in the blue
These invisible giants
Make our ocean dreams come true
[Fade to End]

songs.title: Anglerfish Marriage:  A Deep Sea Romance
songs.prompt:
In the darkness deep below
Where no sunlight dares to go
I shine my light, a deadly show
For tiny males who drift so slow

They say that love is blind
But in the deep you'll find
A romance of a different kind

Bite me, my love, and hold on tight
Dissolve into me through the night
Your eyes may fade, your mind may go
But our hearts will beat as one below
(We'll never be alone)

No more hunting on your own
Merge your flesh, dissolve your bones
Feed from my blood, forget your form
In perfect union we transform

No more hunting on your own
Merge your flesh, dissolve your bones
Feed from my blood, forget your form
In perfect union we transform

As you fade into my side
No more swimming far and wide
Your brain dissolves, but that's the price
Of eternal paradise

Bite me, my love, and hold on tight
Dissolve into me through the night
Your eyes may fade, your mind may go
But our hearts will beat as one below
(We'll never be alone)

Many males may join our dance
In this deep sea romance
Each one adding to our form
In the darkness, in the storm

Now we're joined forevermore
In the depths of ocean's floor
What some call parasitic life
I call being the perfect wife

(Just my glowing light)
(And all my loving husbands)
songs.full_lyrics:
In the darkness deep below
Where no sunlight dares to go
I shine my light, a deadly show
For tiny males who drift so slow

They say that love is blind
But in the deep you'll find
A romance of a different kind

Bite me, my love, and hold on tight
Dissolve into me through the night
Your eyes may fade, your mind may go
But our hearts will beat as one below
(We'll never be alone)

No more hunting on your own
Merge your flesh, dissolve your bones
Feed from my blood, forget your form
In perfect union we transform

No more hunting on your own
Merge your flesh, dissolve your bones
Feed from my blood, forget your form
In perfect union we transform

As you fade into my side
No more swimming far and wide
Your brain dissolves, but that's the price
Of eternal paradise

Bite me, my love, and hold on tight
Dissolve into me through the night
Your eyes may fade, your mind may go
But our hearts will beat as one below
(We'll never be alone)

Many males may join our dance
In this deep sea romance
Each one adding to our form
In the darkness, in the storm

Now we're joined forevermore
In the depths of ocean's floor
What some call parasitic life
I call being the perfect wife

(Just my glowing light)
(And all my loving husbands)
songs.caption: This song depicts the bizarre mating ritual of certain deep-sea anglerfish. The tiny male, drawn by the female's bioluminescent lure, bites onto her body and then physically dissolves and merges his tissues with hers (a process called sexual parasitism). He becomes a permanently attached source of sperm, allowing the female to reproduce without ever hunting for another mate. #Anglerfish #SexualParasitism #DeepSeaBiology #Mating

songs.title: Unzip the Code (The Helicase Song)
songs.tags: Industrial electronic with mechanical rhythms, DNA-inspired percussion patterns. . Include robotic voice effects for enzyme actions.
songs.prompt:
[Synth Intro]
[mechanical whirring]
[DNA strand sounds]

[Spoken Verse]
Moving down the ladder
Breaking bonds with ease
ATP-powered engine
Watch the strands release

[Pre-chorus - Mechanical]
5-prime to 3-prime
Watch how it flows
Breaking hydrogen bonds
As down the strand it goes

[Chorus]
HeliCase! (Unzip the code)
HeliCase! (Watch it unfold)
Breaking every bond it meets
Making single strands complete
(ATP driving the molecular machine)
(Replication can't proceed without this protein)

[Bridge - Technical]
M-C-M-2 through 7
Six subunits in a ring
Rolling down the genome
Doing their thing
(Mechanical precision)
(Molecular division)

[Pre-chorus 2]
Topo-isomerase ahead
Relieving all the strain
While heliCase keeps moving
Down the D-N-A chain

HeliCase! (Nature's machine)
HeliCase! (Working unseen)
Every cell depends on you
Making old strands into new

[mechanical slowdown sounds]
Unwinding
Separating
Preparing
Replicating
(The dance of life goes on)
[Fade Out]
[DNA strand separation sounds]
[ATP hydrolysis rhythms]

songs.title: Kidneys: Filtration symphony
songs.tags: Jazz/Blues
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Deep within the body, on each side they lie (lie),
Filtering the blood as it passes by (by).
Nephrons work together, in a tireless flow (flow),
Cleansing and balancing, from head to toe (toe).

[Pre-Chorus]
Tiny filters, working all day (all day),
Keeping our system, healthy and in play (in play).

[Chorus]
Oh, the kidneys, they purify (purify),
Turning waste to urine, my oh my (oh my).
Balancing our fluids, keeping us whole (us whole),
A vital role they play, in body and soul (and soul).

[Verse 2]
Blood enters the nephron, where magic starts (starts),
Glomerulus filters, breaking down parts (parts).
Essential nutrients, back in the blood (blood),
Waste and water, become urine flood (flood).

[Pre-Chorus]

[Chorus]

[Bridge]
Through the ureters, urine flows (flows),
To the bladder, where it goes (goes).
Nervous system signals, when to release (release),
Regulating function, maintaining peace (peace).

[Verse 3]
Osmoregulation, balance is key (key),
Maintaining homeostasis, so naturally (naturally).
ADH and aldosterone, hormones in play (play),
Ensuring hydration, every single day (day).

[Pre-Chorus]

[Chorus]

[Outro]
Respect and understanding, let's aim to show (show),
The work of the kidneys, let our knowledge grow (grow).

songs.caption: This song celebrates the kidneys, the body's master regulators and balancers. Their primary job is filtration and maintaining homeostasis via the nephrons. Blood is meticulously cleansed in the glomerulus, where essential nutrients are reabsorbed, and waste is converted to urine. They control fluid and electrolyte balance (osmoregulation) using hormones like ADH and aldosterone, and crucially manage the blood's acid-base balance. #Kidneys #Nephron #Homeostasis #Physiology

songs.title: Stages of Unbecoming
songs.tags: Educational, Progressive dark ambient, melodic
songs.prompt:
[VERSE I] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the FRESH stage

[Female singer]
Stillness descends like a veil
As the final breath exhales
The clock has stopped, yet time moves on
Chemical whispers spread the news
First responders take their cues
The blowflies circle, sensing change
Drawn to secrets rearranged

[VERSE II] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the BLOATED stage

[Female singer]
Beneath the skin, a storm arises
Microscopic enterprises
Gases build and tissues stretch
The temple swells with hidden force
Life and death change intercourse
A grotesque cradle now appears
Nursery of the pioneers

[VERSE III] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the ACTIVE DECAY stage

[Female singer]
The fortress falls, the gates give way
Legions feeding night and day
A writhing blanket, white and blind
Consuming what was left behind
The heat of countless hungry mouths
Transforms the host to feeding grounds
While predators hunt those who feast
The hunters becoming the hunted beasts

[VERSE IV] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the ADVANCED DECAY stage

[Female singer]
The crowd disperses, work half-done
The loudest voices now are gone
What remains draws different eyes
Specialized and patient tribes
The slower craftsmen take their turn
On remnants others would not learn
To value or to recognize
As worthy of their enterprise

[VERSE V] 
[SPOKEN] 
The body is in the DRY REMAINS stage

[Female singer]
Now only whispers of what was
Bone and hair and final dust
The specialists complete the task
With microscopic tools that last
Through time's persistent, patient hand
Returning all unto the land
The cycle ends where it begins
The fallen rise through life again

songs.title: The Insect Timeline
songs.tags: Haunting baroque pop with harpsichord and pipe organ, dramatic operatic female vocals, Victorian-era classical influences with dark undertones
songs.prompt:
[Instrumental Intro]
[Spoken Verse]
Through compound eyes we see the truth
In death's garden, life takes root
First wave settlers tell their tale
Time of death? They cannot fail
[Pre-chorus]
Blowflies sense decay
Within the hour, they make their way
Laying eggs in precise locations
Nature's clock in operation
[Dark Chorus]
The insect timeline never lies
Successive waves before our eyes
Each species has its time to show
What happened twenty days ago
What the body needs to show
[Verse]
Colonizers stage by stage
Writing death upon this page
Calliphoridae leads the way
Then dermestids have their say
Temperature, humidity, seasonal arrays
[Bridge]
[Spoken Bridge]
Pupae cases tell the story
Larval instars mark the phases
Succession patterns paint the picture
Of time's passage, leaving traces
[Powerful Chorus]
The insect timeline never lies
Successive waves before our eyes
Each species has its time to show
What happened twenty days ago
What the body needs to show
[Outro]
[Whispered]
Through metamorphosis we learn
When life's final page did turn
Evidence wings its way to light
Nature's clock keeps time just right

songs.title: Whispers in the Sky
songs.tags: Floating, Haunting, Dreamlike, Textured
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Ethereal, Ambient, Dream Pop]
[STYLE: Floating, Haunting, Dreamlike, Textured]
[MOOD: Serene, Reflective, Mystical, Otherworldly]
[VOCALS: Female, Whispered, Lightly Layered, Reverberated]
[ARRANGEMENT: Soft synthesizers, Gentle guitar echoes, Delicate vocal harmonies]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Synth pads, Reverb-heavy guitar, Subtle electronic textures]
[TEMPO: Slow, 60-80 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Atmospheric reverb, Wide stereo space, Lush mixing]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Chorus, Verse, Chorus, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: Gentle throughout, Soothing crescendos]
[EMOTIONS: Tranquility, Mystery, Contemplation, Escapism]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Intro]
(Soft synth pads, distant echoes)

[Verse 1]
Whispers in the sky, so far, so high,
The moonlight dances, softly passing by.
In the quiet, stars align,
Waves of time, they intertwine.

[Chorus]
[Female]
Let me float, let me drift away,
In the shadows of a silver ray.
Where the wind calls my name,
In this endless dream, I remain.

[Verse 2]
Breathe the night, feel the glow,
Through the mist, where secrets flow.
Lost in dreams, we fade to light,
Echoes echo, fading sight.

[Chorus]
[Female]
Let me float, let me drift away,
In the shadows of a silver ray.
Where the wind calls my name,
In this endless dream, I remain.

[Outro]
(Whispered)
Falling, falling... into the sky,
Softly, softly... as we fly.

(Synth fades, lingering echoes)

songs.title: Cordyceps: The Puppet Master
songs.tags: Dark industrial folk with haunting electronics. Atmospheric percussion meets unsettling strings. Mechanical rhythms blend with organic elements for a sinister, naturalistic soundscape.
songs.prompt:
VERSE 1:
Tiny spores drift down like rain
Invisible invaders in the forest domain
An unsuspecting worker foraging alone
Breathes in the parasite that soon will call it home
The fungus weaves through tissue, a silent slow attack
The ant keeps on working, unaware it won't come back

CHORUS:
Puppet master in the canopy
Rewiring minds for all to see
Nature's horror, evolution's scheme
The cordyceps commands, the forest screams

VERSE 2:
Strange behavior starts to show
The worker leaves its patterns that it's always known
Climbing, climbing toward the light
Mandibles lock tight on leaf veins in plain sight
Sisters pass below, blind to what's above
The colony continues while the victim's mind dissolves

(CHORUS)

BRIDGE:
Stalks burst through exoskeleton
Cordyceps crowned in deadly bloom
Perfect location, perfect height
To rain down spores for miles around
The cycle never ends
One life exchanged for thousands more
The fungus kingdom spreads

VERSE 3:
Colonies develop ways to recognize the sick
Carrying infected bodies far from where they live
An arms race spanning millions of years
Predator and prey locked in evolutionary fears
But the fungus keeps adapting, finding ways inside
Intelligence without a brain, patient as the tide

(CHORUS)

OUTRO:
Who's really in control?
When nature writes the script so old
We think we're masters of our fates
While microbes might manipulate
The puppet master pulls the strings
In ways we've yet to understand
The puppet master pulls the strings...

songs.title: Seventeen-Year Symphony
songs.tags: Bright acoustic guitars and light synth layers introduce a calming, organic pop groove at a moderate tempo. Subtle percussion blends with handclaps and airy pads. The chorus blooms with rich harmonies and gentle strings, while birdsong samples accent transitional moments.
songs.prompt:
[Genre: Nature-themed Pop]
[Tempo: Moderate]
[Song Title: Seventeen-Year Symphony]

[Intro]
[Soft Intro] [Birdsong] [Crickets Chirping]
(Synth pad swells gently, a warm, inviting tone)

[Verse 1]
Beneath the earth, a silent count began
Seventeen springs, part of nature's plan
Little nymphs, in their earthen bed
Dreaming of the sky above their head

[Pre-Chorus]
[Building Intensity]
The soil warms, a whisper in the air
A stirring deep, a moment to prepare
The season calls, a transformation near
Their grand emergence, shedding all their fear

[Chorus]
[Harmonies] [Catchy Hook]
Oh, the seventeen-year cicada's call!
From slumber deep, they rise for one and all
A vibrant hum, a unique, fleeting cheer
Their symphony, heard once in seventeen years!

[Verse 2]
Through tiny cracks, they push and start to climb
Leaving exoskeletons behind
Wings unfold, a vibrant, buzzing sound
As life erupts all over hallowed ground

[Bridge]
[Emotional Swell]
For just a few weeks, their purpose takes flight
To sing their song, in morning, noon, and night
A fleeting dance, beneath the sun and moon
Before they fade, returning far too soon

[Chorus]
[Harmonies]
Oh, the seventeen-year cicada's call!
From slumber deep, they rise for one and all
A vibrant hum, a unique, fleeting cheer
Their symphony, heard once in seventeen years!

[Break]
[Instrumental Break] [Layered Arrangement]
(Uplifting instrumental section with melodic synth lines and a steady beat, mimicking the buzzing)

[Chorus, repeated twice]
[Harmonies] [Climactic]
Oh, the seventeen-year cicada's call!
From slumber deep, they rise for one and all
A vibrant hum, a unique, fleeting cheer
Their symphony, heard once in seventeen years!

[Outro]
[Fade Out] [Powerful Outro] [Natural Ambience]
(Synth pad fades slowly, joined by fading cicada sounds)
Until next time, beneath the earth they'll keep
Their silent vigil, in their long, deep sleep.

songs.title: Songbird
songs.tags: The piece opens with a sustained, low vocal drone—voices or bowed strings create a pulsing undercurrent. Above, agile woodwinds and bright echoes of glass harmonica trace dancing, birdlike melodies. As the piece evolves, the low drone thickens with subtle overtone singing, while the higher melodic lines flutter and spiral, mimicking dawn birds with layered intervals and rhythmic interplay, culminating in a rich polyphonic tapestry blending the earthy and the ethereal.
songs.prompt:
(Deep in the quiet forest, as dawn begins to bloom...)
A single, high, sweet whistle
Cuts through the morning gloom.

(A low, ancient hum, the forest floor awaking...)
A trill, a delicate vibrato,
The silence gently breaking.

(The earth breathes softly, a rumble from the ground...)
Another bird replies, a clear, pure sound.
A symphony of life, where echoes softly meet...
High notes like tiny bells, so wonderfully sweet.

(The trees stand still, a presence strong and vast...)
A soaring, looping melody,
Too beautiful to last.

(A drone of growing light, the world begins to stir...)
The sparrows chirp their greetings,
A joyful, happy blur.

(The bass of nature’s rhythm, steady and profound...)
The high-pitched larks ascend, without a single sound
Of effort, just pure song,
Where they truly belong.

(Until the sun is high, and morning's magic fades...)
Then settles to a hum,
Amongst the forest glades.

(Until the sun is high, and morning's magic fades...)
Then settles to a hum,
Amongst the forest glades.

songs.title: Evidence
songs.tags: Power ballad
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Fossil records in the stone
Telling stories of ages gone
Transitional forms laid to rest
Nature's evolutionary test
Tiktaalik and Archaeopteryx
Ancient puzzles science fixed

[Chorus]
It's all in the evidence, written in time
Millions of years, not seven days divine
DNA, fossils, and geology agree
Evolution shaped you and me

[Verse 2]
Vestigial parts with no design
Whale legs and blind eyes define
The recurrent laryngeal nerve
Takes the long path without reserve
Our DNA tells ancient tales
98 percent shared with chimpanzees

[Chorus]
It's all in the evidence, written in time
Millions of years, not seven days divine
DNA, fossils, and geology agree
Evolution shaped you and me

[Bridge]
Radiometric dating doesn't lie
Light from stars that's billions of years in the sky
Pangaea's drift across the deep
Continental plates that never sleep
Biogeography's distribution plans
Life adapting as best it can

[Verse 3]
In labs we watch it unfold
Bacteria evolve, as we're told
Antibiotic resistance grows
Natural selection as it goes
Ring species around mountains high
Evolution we can't deny

[Final Chorus]
It's all in the evidence, written in time
Billions of years, not seven days divine
Geology, biology, astronomy agree
Evolution shaped you and me
Evolution shaped reality

[Outro]
Follow where the evidence leads
Not ancient texts or supernatural creeds
Science adjusts as new facts appear
The natural world becomes more clear

songs.title: "Nature's Game"
songs.tags: mashup
songs.prompt:
Life finds a way, through endless time
Through countless days, through endless nights

No master plan, just what survives
Good enough to stay alive
Random chance becomes design
When death decides what's left behind
Evolution's blind but wise

Every feature has its price
Every strength comes with its vice

There's no perfect in this game
Only change remains the same
Adapt or fade into the night
Nature's rules make no mistakes
Every choice a trade-off makes
In the dance of death and life

Use it or you'll lose it all
Rise up or prepare to fall
Time keeps flowing endlessly
Past lives echo in our genes


Cooperation's selfish heart
Playing nature's clever part
Competition breeds the new
While stability makes us few
Success means living long enough
To pass along your special stuff

No free lunch in nature's show
But symbiosis helps us grow

There's no perfect in this game
Only change remains the same
Adapt or fade into the night
Nature's rules make no mistakes
Every choice a trade-off makes
In the dance of death and life

If it works, it isn't wrong
If it breeds, it carries on
Nature's judgment never ends
Success is measured in what transcends

There's no perfect in this game
Only change remains the same
Adapt or fade into the night
Nature's rules make no mistakes
Every choice a trade-off makes
In the dance of death and life

Through endless time
Through countless days
Life finds a way

songs.title: Slime Time
songs.tags: Punk-rock with slimy guitar effects, sudden dynamic shifts representing the explosive slime release, and a driving rhythm section that maintains relentless energy throughout.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Ancient swimmer in the deep
Prehistoric, nearly blind
Looks defenseless, soft and weak
But evolution had a plan in mind
When danger comes too close to home
The hagfish has a trick to show

[Chorus]
Slime time! Expanding 10,000 fold
Slime time! A defense that never gets old
From microscopic threads to mucus cloud
Nature's most disgusting shroud
Slime time!

[Verse 2]
No scales, no jaw, no vertebrae (notochord) 
Three hundred million years unchanged
One hundred glands along each side
Ready to deploy their slimy tide
A single thread could stretch six feet
Suffocating any threat they meet

[Chorus]
Slime time! Expanding 10,000 fold
Slime time! A defense that never gets old
From microscopic threads to mucus cloud
Nature's most disgusting shroud
Slime time!

[Bridge]
Protein filaments unwind
Like silk that's stronger than you'd find
In spider webs or bulletproof vests
Bioengineers put them to the test
Choking gills of any shark
That thought this meal would be a lark

[Final Chorus]
Slime time! Expanding 10,000 fold
Slime time! A defense that never gets old
From microscopic threads to mucus cloud
Nature's most disgusting shroud
Slime time!
(It's always) Slime time!

Slime time!

Slime time!

songs.caption: This song celebrates the Hagfish, a primitive fish known for its extreme defense mechanism. When threatened, it instantly deploys a massive, suffocating slime cloud. The slime uses microscopic protein threads that are stronger than steel and expand 10,000-fold upon contact with water, choking the gills of predators like sharks. This ancient, amazing biological material is now being studied by bioengineers. #Hagfish #Slime #Bioengineering #MarineBiology

songs.title: Jellyfish Life Cycle Scat Song
songs.tags: A dreamy, experimental soundscape opens with lush synth pads and bubbly, liquid-like textures. Scat-sung female vocals glide over minimal backing in verse 1, evolving into choruses with layered male scatting and rich harmonies. The bridge spotlights rhythmic male scat lines over shifting synths. The outro dissolves into airy scat interplay and ethereal whispers, synths gently fading to silence.
songs.prompt:
[Intro: Dreamy synth pads with a watery, bubbling sound]
[Scat Singing: Female Vocal]
Oooh-ba-doo-ba-doo-wahh! Pffft-shoo-ba-shoo-waah!

[Verse 1: Scat Singing, Female Vocal]
A-dop-a-doo-wah, a-jelly's on the go.
A-shoo-be-doo-be-doo, a-drifting in the flow.
A-woop-de-doo-bop, the medusa's the stage.
A-dee-bop-a-doo-wah, turning a brand new page.

[Chorus: Scat Singing, Male Vocal, Harmonies]
A-zool-be-doo-doo, the planula descends!
A-shoo-ba-doo-boo, the cycle now extends.
A-dop-a-doo-wop, a polyp takes its hold.
A-shoop-a-doo-wah, a story to be told!

[Verse 2: Scat Singing, Female Vocal]
A-doo-be-doo-wop, the polyp starts to grow.
A-shoo-ba-doo-ba-doo, a-budding, you know.
A-woop-de-doo-bah, a strobila forms!
A-dee-bop-a-doo-wah, enduring the sea storms!

[Bridge: Scat Singing, Male Vocal]
A-zoop-a-doo-wah, the ephyra breaks free!
A-shoo-be-doo-be-doo, a brand new jellyfish, you see!
A-woop-de-doo-bop, a-swimming on its own.
A-dee-bop-a-doo-wah, a kingdom it has known!

[Chorus: Scat Singing, Male Vocal, Harmonies]
A-zool-be-doo-doo, the planula descends!
A-shoo-ba-doo-boo, the cycle now extends.
A-dop-a-doo-wop, a polyp takes its hold.
A-shoop-a-doo-wah, a story to be told!

[Outro: Scat Singing, Ethereal Whispers, dreamy synth fades out]
A-doo-ba-doo-bop... a-doo-waaah... the endless, endless cycle...

A-shoo-be-doo-be-doo, a-drifting in the flow.
A-woop-de-doo-bop, the medusa's the stage.
A-dee-bop-a-doo-wah, turning a brand new page.

[Chorus: Scat Singing, Male Vocal, Harmonies]
A-zool-be-doo-doo, the planula descends!
A-shoo-ba-doo-boo, the cycle now extends.
A-dop-a-doo-wop, a polyp takes its hold.
A-shoop-a-doo-wah, a story to be told!

[Verse 2: Scat Singing, Female Vocal]
A-doo-be-doo-wop, the polyp starts to grow.
A-shoo-ba-doo-ba-doo, a-budding, you know.
A-woop-de-doo-bah, a strobila forms!
A-dee-bop-a-doo-wah, enduring the sea storms!

[Bridge: Scat Singing, Male Vocal]
A-zoop-a-doo-wah, the ephyra breaks free!
A-shoo-be-doo-be-doo, a brand new jellyfish, you see!
A-woop-de-doo-bop, a-swimming on its own.
A-dee-bop-a-doo-wah, a kingdom it has known!

[Chorus: Scat Singing, Male Vocal, Harmonies]
A-zool-be-doo-doo, the planula descends!
A-shoo-ba-doo-boo, the cycle now extends.
A-dop-a-doo-wop, a polyp takes its hold.
A-shoop-a-doo-wah, a story to be told!

[Outro: Scat Singing, Ethereal Whispers, dreamy synth fades out]
A-doo-ba-doo-bop... a-doo-waaah... the endless, endless cycle...
songs.caption: This song uses scat to trace the fascinating life cycle of a jellyfish (a Cnidarian). It begins with the free-swimming, bell-shaped medusa phase. When it reproduces, the larva (planula) settles and develops into the bottom-dwelling polyp. This polyp then buds and segments (strobilation) to release tiny, star-shaped jellyfish (ephyra), which grow into the adult medusa, completing the cycle. #Jellyfish #Cnidaria #LifeCycle #MarineBiology #Zoology

songs.title: Alpha, Beta, Gamma
songs.tags: Kicking off with a crisp, brush-driven swing beat, upright bass, and plush piano, the song layers vibrant brass, velvety saxophones, and warm guitar for a lush big band jazz texture. The finale spotlights an echoing trombone flourish, resolving with a gentle guitar strum.
songs.prompt:
(Verse 1)
Well, the world was lookin' shiny, and the future was so bright,
With our atoms split and crackin', we could light up all the night.
We'd found a brand new power, a scientific treat,
The way an unstable nucleus makes our world complete.
It's a wonder and a mystery, and a brand new kind of gleam,
We're livin' in the fallout of a scientific dream.

(Chorus)
Oh, radioactivity, that glowing, cosmic ray,
Breakin' down the matter, every single day.
With Alpha, Beta, Gamma, goin' on a wild ride,
That lonely, little nucleus just can't get no peace inside.
So don't you worry 'bout the half-life, just enjoy the cosmic show,
That's radioactivity, my sweet radio-active-oh.

(Verse 2)
Some atoms, they're not steady, they're a little bit unglued,
They're lookin' for a new arrangement, if you'll excuse their mood.
They'll shoot a little particle, like a teardrop from their core,
And change their whole identity, to somethin' new, and then some more.
From Uranium to Lead, there's a journey, you can bet,
It's a half-life of a lifetime, for the atom, don't you fret.

(Chorus)
Oh, radioactivity, that glowing, cosmic ray,
Breakin' down the matter, every single day.
With Alpha, Beta, Gamma, goin' on a wild ride,
That lonely, little nucleus just can't get no peace inside.
So don't you worry 'bout the half-life, just enjoy the cosmic show,
That's radioactivity, my sweet radio-active-oh.

(Bridge)
Now a half-life's not a mystery, it's a scientific rule,
For a certain bunch of atoms, it's a radioactive school.
Say, after a million years, just half of them will fade,
And after one more million, half of that is what's left made.
It’s the steady rate of decay, for a scientific soul,
A-tellin' us exactly when they're gonna lose control.

(Outro)
So come on, baby, don't you fear the glowing of the night,
It's just the steady, cosmic hum, and a brand new kind of light.

songs.title: Every egg is sacred - Gemini version
songs.tags: An energetic piano launches a bouncy vaudeville groove, joined by brass stabs accenting comedic lyrics. Dramatic strings add flair, as theatrical  vocals soar, offset by a lively child choir in witty call-and-response. Upright bass and brisk percussion propel the pace, while layered harmonies, playful interludes, and exaggerated orchestral swells lead to a grand, tongue-in-cheek finale.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
[Orchestral Build]
[Male Vocal]: Baritone male vocals, singing with great reverence

[Chorus]
Every egg is sacred, every egg is great.
If a cell is wasted, God gets quite irate.
Every egg is special, every egg is free,
The Lord made each one for thee.
[Choir: Gospel-style choir joins in harmony]

[Verse 1]
There are countless sperm from the male man's sack,
Billions of swimmers that are not coming back.
They're swimming to and fro, and there is no lack,
But with a lady, that's not the fact.

[Chorus]
Every egg is sacred, every egg is great.
If a cell is wasted, God gets quite irate.
Every egg is special, every egg is free,
The Lord made each one for thee.

[Verse 2]
Every month a miracle, a wondrous sight,
A single golden orb, a shining light.
Just one little egg, so small and bright,
Hoping for a sperm to come through the night.

[Break]
[Vocal]: Spoken word
[Bell Ringing]
"A woman's body holds a treasure, one by one.
Not a horde of tiny soldiers, but a solitary sun."

[Chorus]
[Crescendo]
Every egg is sacred, every egg is great.
If a cell is wasted, God gets quite irate.
Every egg is special, every egg is free,
The Lord made each one for thee.

[Outro]
[Piano Solo]
[Fade Out]
[Choir]: Gentle, fading harmonies

songs.title: Every egg is sacred - Grok version
songs.tags: An energetic piano launches a bouncy vaudeville groove, joined by brass stabs accenting comedic lyrics. Dramatic strings add flair, as theatrical female vocals soar, offset by a lively child choir in witty call-and-response. Upright bass and brisk percussion propel the pace, while layered harmonies, playful interludes, and exaggerated orchestral swells lead to a grand, tongue-in-cheek finale.
songs.prompt:
Verse 1
There are eggs in every ovary,
A precious little load.
Each one’s got potential,
To make a life explode!
From the follicles they’re born,
In a cycle every month,
If you waste a single egg,
You’re a biological dunce!

Chorus
Every egg is sacred,
Every egg is great.
If an egg is wasted,
Nature gets irate!
Every egg is sacred,
Every egg’s a gem.
Lose one in a cycle,
And the womb says, “Ahem!”

Verse 2
In the fallopian tube they glide,
So tiny, yet so grand.
Waiting for a sperm to meet,
And take it by the hand.
But if no sperm comes knocking,
It’s a menstrual tragedy,
The egg’s flushed out, oh what a pout,
It’s biology’s decree!

Chorus
Every egg is sacred,
Every egg is fine.
If you skip ovulating,
You’re messing with design!
Every egg is sacred,
Every egg’s a star.
Waste one, and the hormones cry,
“How cruel you humans are!”

Bridge
Let the sperm go swimming,
Let the eggs prepare.
When they meet, it’s magic,
A baby might be there!
But if you block their union,
Or let an egg go free,
The ovary’s disappointed,
And it glares at you and me!

Chorus
Every egg is sacred,
Every egg’s divine.
If an egg’s not fertilized,
It’s a reproductive crime!
Every egg is sacred,
Every egg’s a thrill.
Lose one, and the uterus,
Says, “You’ve missed the bill!”

Outro
So respect the humble egg cell,
It’s a miracle, you see.
From a follicle to a blastocyst,
It’s life’s biology!
Every egg is sacred,
Every egg’s a chance.
So let’s all cheer for ovum,
In this reproductive dance!

songs.title: Drake Caterpillar: "The Cat's Meow"
songs.tags: Twangy electric guitars and steady acoustic strumming set a raw, outlaw country vibe. Verses use loose, driving drums and a pulsing upright bass, with sharp slide guitar accents. Choruses build with harmonica and backing vocals, creating a tense, rebellious texture throughout.
songs.prompt:
(Verse 1)
She ain't the prettiest ship in the starlit sea,
Got more dents than a Vanduul's glee.
They call her the Caterpillar, I call her The Cat,
With a belly full of cargo, and that's a fact.
Got detachable modules, a hauler's delight,
Can switch from ore to illegal contraband overnight.
From GrimHEX to the Ghost Hollow, we make our run,
Dodgin' the Advocacy, just for fun.
(Chorus)
Oh, the Caterpillar, she's a rugged old soul,
Haulin' the goods that make a man whole.
She groans and she creaks, but she always gets through,
Me and my Cat, we're a two-man crew.
So pour me a whiskey, and I'll tell you a tale,
Of a hauler's life on the starlit trail.
(Verse 2)
Took a job out on Lyria, a package sealed tight,
A Cutlass on my six showed up in the fading light.
They hailed me for my cargo, thought I'd be easy prey,
But I opened up the side doors and blew their shields away.
My gunner in the turret laid down some heavy fire,
Sent 'em spinning to the dust, burning on a funeral pyre.
Drake builds 'em tough, with a little bit of bite,
Enough to win a scrap and vanish in the night.
(Bridge)
Some pilots chase glory in a Gladius so grand,
I chase the UEC with these calloused hands.
This ship ain't just metal, it's my freedom and my friend,
She's been with me through hell and back, right until the bitter end.
She’s my castle and my hideout, my ticket to the stars,
The only thing I trust between these asteroid scars.
(Chorus)
Oh, the Caterpillar, she's a rugged old soul,
Haulin' the goods that make a man whole.
She groans and she creaks, but she always gets through,
Me and my Cat, we're a two-man crew.
So pour me a whiskey, and I'll tell you a tale,
Of a hauler's life on the starlit trail.
(Verse 3)
Landed safe at Jumptown with the goods all intact,
The client paid in crypto, no turning back.
Now the engines are cooling, the cargo bay is bare,
Got a pocket full of credits without a single care.
Gonna buy a new power plant, maybe some better shields,
And see what kind of trouble the next trade route yields.
'Cause out here on the fringe, there's a living to be made,
For a pilot with some courage and a ship that ain't afraid.
(Outro)
Yeah, a ship that ain't afraid...
Spoken: "Alright, power her down. Let's see what tomorrow brings."

songs.title: Master system engineer (A Star Citizen song)
songs.tags: This patter song launches with brisk piano and tight staccato strings. Drums maintain a sharp, syncopated rhythm, supporting rapid-fire vocals with flawless enunciation. Verses sport sporadic clarinet riffs, while short brass bursts punctuate transitions. The energy stays taut throughout.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
I am the very model of a master systems engineer.
I’ve worked on Drake and Anvil ships, and MISC, and Origin.
I'm quite good with a power plant as a facet of my trade because
I know each circuit diagram that the manufacturer's made.

[Verse 2]
From coupled mode to decoupled, I know flight assist machinery.
I'll problem-solve with multi-tools or S9 weaponry.
I perfectly recall your cooler's thermal-pipe configuration.
(Hmm, rhyme for configuration... ahh yes!)
And cross-check it with weapon stats for optimal ablation!

[Choir]
And cross-checks it with weapon stats for optimal ablation!
And cross-checks it with weapon stats for optimal ablation!


[Verse 3]
My knowledge is impressive, though my workshop's hot and factory.
I'm fluent in the Banu script and Xi'an engine flummery.
My expertise extends from grav-lev bikes to ships exploratory.
I am the very model of a master systems engineer.

[Choir]
His expertise extends from grav-lev bikes to ships exploratory, he is the very model of a master systems engineer!

[Verse 4]
By breakfast time I’m patching up a hull breached by the Vanduul.
My ideal date would finish with a diagnostic data-pull.
I've mastered targeting computers, both lag-pip and the leading kind.
I don't begrudge the Drake pilots the loose parts they leave behind.
I've memorized eidetic every single ship component's spec,
I've written troubleshooters 'til they're coming out my bloody neck!
I'll overload a power plant without the least apology.

(Hmm, ology... simple enough!)
Then spend my weekends reading up on RSI componentology.

[Choir]
He spends his weekends reading up on RSI componentology.
He spends his weekends reading up on RSI componentology.


[Verse 5]
When I complete a patch job, I sign my name in welding seams.
I've salvaged more old Javelins than you have seen in fever dreams.
If knowledge is a toolkit, then I’m its prime servicer here,
I am the very model of a master systems engineer.

[Choir]
If knowledge is a toolkit, then he’s its prime servicer here, he is the very model of a master systems engineer!

[Verse 6]
When I can quote the heat profile of every single laser type,
When I can tune a Quantum Drive to bypass an interdictor's gripe,
When I expound on theories that my colleagues find fantastical,
When I know all the finer points of shields and armor, tactical,
When I could reboot a capital ship with just my brain half-functioning,
When I can trace a power fault to one fried micro-junctioning.
In short, when I've a smattering of jump point navigation strategy...

(Hmm, strategy... tough one. Tragedy? No. Battery? Too simple. Wait... what's the worst thing that can happen? A calamity!)
A failure's a calamity, which won't occur because of me!

[Choir]
A failure's a calamity, which won't occur because of he!
A failure's a calamity, which won't occur because of he!


[Verse 7]
My rivals call me reckless and the UEE may censure me.
And Advocacy tables say I'll die within the century.
I’ve tuned up ships in every port from Pyro out to Sol, you see,
I am the very model of a master systems engineer.

[Choir]
He’s tuned up ships in every port from Pyro out to Sol, you see, he is the very model of a master systems engineer!

[Verse 8]
At fixing up a PTV I've singular ability.
I don't install grey market parts that compromise stability.
I've shifted power conduits from ArcCorp to New Babbage.
I've studied salvage techniques both destructive and less savage.
Of Cousin Crows' components my memory's a catalog.
I know the pirate clans from Grim HEX down to Ninetails' grog.
My mastery of tractor beams is simply beyond parity.
My work on terraformers could fix Hurston's air clarity!

[Choir]
His work on terraformers could fix Hurston's air clarity!
His work on terraformers could fix Hurston's air clarity!


[Verse 9]
I know the use of components from industrial to military.
My business is more profitable than Shubin Interstell-iary.
I'll give a lecture just for fun, my knowledge has no carrier,
I am the very model of a master systems engineer!

[Choir]
He'll give a lecture just for fun, his knowledge has no carrier, he is the very model of a master systems engineer!

songs.caption: This song describes the life cycle of the Guinea Worm (Dracunculus medinensis). Humans are infected by drinking water containing the parasite's larvae in copepods. After a year, the worm causes a painful blister, which compels the host to seek water, allowing the worm to release its young and continue the cycle. This ancient disease is now close to global eradication. #GuineaWorm #Dracunculus #Parasitology

songs.title: Beneath the corpse (Remastered)
songs.tags: raspy, quick, Female singer, Dark ambient/industrial fusion with gothic folk elements; blends atmospheric drones, mechanical percussion and haunting strings to evoke the beetle's nocturnal world
songs.prompt:
[Verse]
Drawn to the scent that others avoid
I sense what's happened in the void
Where life has ceased, my work begins
Neh-kro-deez lit-tor-ah-lis - decay's kin
Black carapace gleaming in moonlight
Orange-tipped antennae sense what's right
Upon fallen giants I will crawl
Answering nature's solemn call

[CHORUS]
Beneath the corpse, I make my home
Through flesh and bone, I roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, I bask

[Verse]
My antennae quiver with delight
Sensing chemicals in the night
The perfect corpse, too large to bury
Unlike my cousins, I'm not wary
I carry mites upon my shell
Travelers with stories to tell
They fight the flies that would compete
Our alliance makes our work complete

[CHORUS]

[Verse]
Eggs I lay in this fertile ground
Where nutrients and shelter abound
My larvae feast on what remains
Nature's custodians, we take the reins
We're not monsters though you might recoil
Just recyclers of this mortal coil
Breaking down what once had breath
Transforming ending into life's next step

[BRIDGE]
The shore sexton's story seldom told
Our purpose ancient, necessary, bold
What seems macabre serves a greater good
Nature's balance perfectly understood
In twilight hours when shadows fall
I emerge ready for nature's call
Too large to bury, the carcass waits
My work begins as day abates

[CHORUS]

[Verse]
The giants fall and we arrive
With tiny allies that help us thrive
A partnership of give and take
A perfect system, no mistake
In the dance between life and demise
We are the link that harmonize
The carrion's end is just the start
Of nature's most beautiful art

[FINAL CHORUS]
Beneath the corpse, we make our home
Through flesh and bone, we roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, we'll always bask

songs.title: Wires in my mind, they hum and they weave
songs.tags: bass male voice, Acapella, spoken word, human beat box, beautiful female voice, duet, jazz, post-punk revival
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Wires in my mind, they hum and they weave,
(Patterns of thought, what do I believe?)
Voices in the air, they pull and they sway,
(Why do I feel what I feel today?)
Like circuits in code, my heart can be swayed,
(A whisper, a spark, and my truth might fade.)  
[Pre-Chorus]
They try to rewrite me, to break through my core,
Like machines in the dark, they keep wanting more.
But I’ll trace the roots, where my shadows begin,
To know who I am, gotta look deep within.  
[Chorus]
Why do we think, why do we feel?
What’s the truth, and what’s the real?
Guard my mind, build walls of light,
Question the stars in the endless night.
From code to soul, we’re wired the same,
Learning to rise, to master the game.
Why we believe, I’ll find the key,
To set my heart and my spirit free.  
[Verse 2]
Screens flash their lies, they twist and they turn,
(Feeding my fears, till my bridges burn.)
Like a machine, I could fall to their call,
(Hacked by the noise, till I lose it all.)
But I’ll ask the why, let the questions ignite,
(Cut through the fog, find my own light. ) 
[Pre-Chorus]
They try to control me, with words that deceive,
But I’ll guard my soul, choose what I believe.
From neurons to networks, we’re fragile, it’s true,
Yet strength comes from knowing what’s me, what’s you.  
[Chorus]
Why do we think, why do we feel?
What’s the truth, and what’s the real?
Guard my mind, build walls of light,
Question the stars in the endless night.
From code to soul, we’re wired the same,
Learning to rise, to master the game.
Why we believe, I’ll find the key,
To set my heart and my spirit free.  
[Bridge]
Epistemology, my compass, my guide,
Unravel the threads where the shadows hide.
Every thought, every tear, every choice that I make,
I’ll question, I’ll learn, for my freedom’s sake.
Like a system that’s locked, I’ll rewrite my own code,
With wisdom and heart, I’ll lighten the load.  
[Chorus]
Why do we think, why do we feel?
What’s the truth, and what’s the real?
Guard my mind, build walls of light,
Question the stars in the endless night.
From code to soul, we’re wired the same,
Learning to rise, to master the game.
Why we believe, I’ll find the key,
To set my heart and my spirit free.  
[Outro]
So I’ll ask, and I’ll seek, till the answers align,
My thoughts, my feelings, my truth divine.
From circuits to dreams, we’re searching, it’s clear,
Why we believe—it starts right here

songs.title: MISC Hull C: "The Corporate Hauler"
songs.tags: Kicks off with driving electric guitar riffs and thunderous drums, laying a bold rock foundation. Bass grooves lock in tightly, supporting soaring lead vocals. Explosive choruses bring layered harmonies and dynamic cymbal crashes, closing with an electrifying extended guitar solo.
songs.prompt:
(Verse 1)
Got a Hull C beneath me, a beast of the black,
Miles of cargo containers, ain't no turnin' back.
I'm a corporate hauler, a professional you see,
Signed a contract with Shubin, got no time to be free.
From the refineries of Hurston to the shipyards of Lorville,
I move the goods that build the verse, a cog in the wheel.
The pirates give me a wide berth, the fighters clear the way,
When you're movin' this much tonnage, you've got somethin' to say.

(Chorus)
Yeah, the Hull C's roarin', a thunder in the void,
A river of commerce, that cannot be destroyed.
I'm a king of the spacelanes, a master of my trade,
In the cockpit of my Hull C, my fortune is made.

(Verse 2)
The landing sequence started, two hours out from port,
Extending out the spindle, a ballet of a sort.
Thousands of containers, a fragile metal spine,
All hangin' on my piloting, every single time.
Automated cargo arms unload me in the rain,
While I file the paperwork and calculate the gain.
No time for the cantina, no time to stretch my feet,
Just a quick refuel and off to face the fleet.

(Bridge)
The cockpit is an island, surrounded by the stars,
I see the bars on Terra through these viewport scars.
They call me a king, but a king can feel alone,
Just a voice on the comms link, a man without a home.
I'm responsible for millions, a payload of pure gold,
A story of logistics that will never be told.

(Chorus)
Yeah, the Hull C's roarin', a thunder in the void,
A river of commerce, that cannot be destroyed.
I'm a king of the spacelanes, a master of my trade,
In the cockpit of my Hull C, my fortune is made.

(Verse 3)
Dropping out of quantum, see the lights of Area18,
A sight to make a hauler feel like part of the machine.
This city needs the products that I carry in my hold,
To keep the system running, a story to be sold.
I watch the little ships all scatter, like fish before a whale,
As my shadow falls upon them, I know I cannot fail.
I'm the lifeblood of the system, the pulse of industry,
And this ship's my mighty chariot, for all the 'verse to see.

(Outro)
Yeah, for all the 'verse to see!
Movin' the 'verse!
One haul at a time!
[Heavy guitar riff builds and then cuts off abruptly with the sound of a massive airlock hissing shut]

songs.title: Love You to Death (Literally) (Remastered)
songs.tags: Dark indie rock with elements of psychedelic guitar and dramatic tempo changes, haunting male vocals and sudden aggressive breaks.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
Green dancer in the garden light
Your movements catch my compound eyes
Six legs moving with such grace
I know the risks, but can't escape

[Chorus]
She's got that deadly kind of love
One kiss could be my last
But something deep inside me knows
This is how I'll help our bloodline grow

[Verse 2]
The dance begins, my heart beats fast
This might just be my final dance
Her embrace is worth the price
My genes will live though I might die

[Bridge]
Feel her mandibles on my neck
No regrets as my world goes black
My body keeps on giving
Even when I stop living
That's evolution's gift to me
My sacrifice sets my children free

[Chorus]
She's got that deadly kind of love
One kiss could be my last
But something deep inside me knows
This is how I'll help our bloodline grow

[Final Verse]
Other males might run and hide
But I'll give everything inside
Nature's twisted kind of grace
Love and death in her embrace

[Outro]
Mantis religiosa,
Ancient dance of life and death
Mantis religiosa,
Till my very final breath

songs.caption: This track highlights the extreme mating ritual of the praying mantis (Mantis religiosa), specifically the male's perspective. After or during copulation, the larger female often engages in sexual cannibalism, consuming the male. Despite the fatal risk, the male's sacrifice is thought to provide the female with vital nutrients she needs to produce more, healthier eggs, maximizing the chance for their shared genes to survive. #PrayingMantis #MantisReligiosa #SexualCannibalism #EvolutionaryBiology

songs.title: The Mycelial Network
songs.tags: Sparkling fingerpicked acoustic guitar leads, joined by warm upright bass and light hand percussion. Subtle harmonies and brushed snare fill the verses, while the chorus opens with layered vocals and gentle banjo. Delicate violin weaves throughout, enriching the indie folk texture.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
[Soft Intro]
Beneath the moss and fallen leaves, a secret world unseen
Whispers through the silent trees, a vibrant, living sheen
Mycelial threads, a silver lace, stretch out in earthen dark
A quiet, slow, and ancient pace, leaving their subtle mark

[Chorus]
[Harmonies]
Oh, the wood wide web, a silent, humble hum
Where wisdom flows and secrets spread, for all the forest come
A network vast, a fungal art, connecting root to bough
Each pulse a beating, generous heart, helping the forest grow

[Verse 2]
The old oak whispers to the pine, through pathways underground
A sharing of the sweet design, where sustenance is found
From parent tree to sapling small, a gentle, giving hand
Responding to the forest's call, across the fertile land

[Bridge]
[Melodic Interlude]
No boundaries here, no greed, no plight, just unity's embrace
A testament to nature's might, in this hidden, sacred space
For fungi teach what humans crave, a selfless, giving plea
To nurture all that nature gave, for all eternity

[Chorus]
[Harmonies]
Oh, the wood wide web, a silent, humble hum
Where wisdom flows and secrets spread, for all the forest come
A network vast, a fungal art, connecting root to bough
Each pulse a beating, generous heart, helping the forest grow

[Outro]
[Fade Out]
So listen close, when breezes sigh, through branches green and high
The forest breathes, and so do I, beneath the watchful sky.
songs.full_lyrics:
[Verse 1]
[Soft Intro]
Beneath the moss and fallen leaves, a secret world unseen
Whispers through the silent trees, a vibrant, living sheen
Mycelial threads, a silver lace, stretch out in earthen dark
A quiet, slow, and ancient pace, leaving their subtle mark

[Chorus]
[Harmonies]
Oh, the wood wide web, a silent, humble hum
Where wisdom flows and secrets spread, for all the forest come
A network vast, a fungal art, connecting root to bough
Each pulse a beating, generous heart, helping the forest grow

[Verse 2]
The old oak whispers to the pine, through pathways underground
A sharing of the sweet design, where sustenance is found
From parent tree to sapling small, a gentle, giving hand
Responding to the forest's call, across the fertile land

[Bridge]
[Melodic Interlude]
No boundaries here, no greed, no plight, just unity's embrace
A testament to nature's might, in this hidden, sacred space
For fungi teach what humans crave, a selfless, giving plea
To nurture all that nature gave, for all eternity

[Chorus]
[Harmonies]
Oh, the wood wide web, a silent, humble hum
Where wisdom flows and secrets spread, for all the forest come
A network vast, a fungal art, connecting root to bough
Each pulse a beating, generous heart, helping the forest grow

[Outro]
[Fade Out]
So listen close, when breezes sigh, through branches green and high
The forest breathes, and so do I, beneath the watchful sky.

songs.title: The Genome Remix (Remix)
songs.tags: A sparse acoustic ballad led by fingerpicked guitar, featuring intimate female vocals upfront. Subtle string pads enter during the chorus for warmth, while occasional soft harmonies support the melody. The arrangement remains minimal, emphasizing emotion and rawness.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
They called it "junk," just clutter in our code,
A dusty old attic, a heavy genetic load.
A sliver of genes, they said, wrote the score,
The rest was just noise, not worth exploring more.
But now those shadows sing, their secrets ignite,
A hidden spark glowing in our DNA's light! (Aha!)

[Chorus]
It’s the Genome Remix, a vibrant new beat,
From ancient invaders, our story’s complete!
Those ERVs, once ignored, left behind,
Now flip the switch, rewiring our mind! (Whoa!)
In the strands of our DNA, a viral design,
Makes us human, bold and divine!

[Verse 2]
A retrovirus crashed, long ages ago,
Slipped into our cells, where life’s rivers flow.
In germline it lingered, from kin to kin,
Its wild roots tamed, new roles to begin.
Evolution’s a maestro, rewriting the art,
Old foes turned allies, now beating our heart! (Yeah!)

[Chorus]
It’s the Genome Remix, a vibrant new beat,
From ancient invaders, our story’s complete!
Those ERVs, once ignored, left behind,
Now flip the switch, rewiring our mind! (Whoa!)
In the strands of our DNA, a viral design,
Makes us human, bold and divine!

[Bridge]
(Woo!) LTRs are dancing, directing the show,
(Boom!) Syncytin-1 helps new life to grow!
From viral fusion, placentas take flight,
Turning old foes to a beacon of light.
In embryos sparking, our genes they command,
Crafting our future with a viral hand!

[Chorus]
It’s the Genome Remix, a vibrant new beat,
From ancient invaders, our story’s complete!
Those ERVs, once ignored, left behind,
Now flip the switch, rewiring our mind! (Whoa!)
In the strands of our DNA, a viral design,
Makes us human, bold and divine!

[Outro]
So when you gaze at the code in your core,
See the viruses that shaped evermore.
Not junk, but a symphony, woven so grand,
Life’s epic remix, forever to stand! (Alright!)

songs.title: Tough Tiny Tardigrade
songs.tags: Upbeat Indie Folk driven by a percussive acoustic guitar groove, lively handclaps, and playful melodic lines led by mandolin and glockenspiel accents. Verses feature bright, clear vocals, while choruses layer jubilant group harmonies. Energetic drums propel a festive, instructive atmosphere.
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
[Acoustic Guitar, Banjo, Light Percussion]
(Sound of a gentle breeze and crickets chirping)

[Verse 1]
From the mossy cushion, to the ocean's deep
A tiny marvel, a secret it keeps
Eight little legs, a body so grand
A water bear strollin' across the land
It's a tardigrade, a sight to behold
A micro-wonder, brave and so bold
With a stylet mouth, it pierces a cell
A little meal, a story to tell

[Chorus]
[Harmonies, Building Intensity]
Oh, the tardigrade, the water bear
It's got a cuticle, a body so rare
It molts and it sheds, a new coat it makes
For a life of survival, for goodness sakes
From the egg to the adult, a cycle so small
It's a life of persistence, standing up tall
With a brain and a nerve cord, a simple design
The tardigrade's world, a cosmic sign

[Verse 2]
When the world gets dry, and the sun gets hot
It curls in a tun, an incredible plot
It slows its life, a metabolic rest
Putting its being to the ultimate test
It can handle the vacuum, the cold and the heat
A champion survivor, you can't be beat
Radiation and pressure, it takes it all in
A tiny warrior, where do we begin?

[Chorus]
[Harmonies, Upbeat tempo]
Oh, the tardigrade, the water bear
It's got a cuticle, a body so rare
It molts and it sheds, a new coat it makes
For a life of survival, for goodness sakes
From the egg to the adult, a cycle so small
It's a life of persistence, standing up tall
With a brain and a nerve cord, a simple design
The tardigrade's world, a cosmic sign

[Bridge]
[Acoustic guitar strumming slows, creating a sense of awe]
It's a cryptobiote, a special new word
The toughest creature, you've ever heard
When conditions get better, it comes back to life
Escaping the danger, the turmoil, the strife
A masterpiece of evolution's art
Playing a tiny but an essential part

[Outro]
[Acoustic guitar fades, light banjo riff]
So next time you see a patch of green moss
Remember the tardigrade, count the cost
Of a life so small, yet so great and so grand
The toughest little critter in all of the land.
[Fade Out]

songs.title: Secrets of the Female Body (Remastered x2)
songs.tags: Jazz/Blues
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
The vulva's on the outside, it's easy to see,
Labia majora, labia minora, in harmony (in harmony).
The clitoris, a wonder, with nerves galore (nerves galore),
Its function and pleasure, let's explore (let's explore).

[Pre-Chorus]
With intricate design, and complex role (complex role),
The female genitalia, a marvel to behold (to behold).

[Chorus]
Oh, the anatomy, let’s celebrate (let’s celebrate),
The wonders of the body, intricate and great (intricate and great).
From the vulva to the ovaries, all parts play (all parts play),
A vital role in life, every single day (every single day).

[Verse 2]
The vagina's a canal, leading the way (leading the way),
Connecting the cervix, where secrets lay (secrets lay).
The uterus, a cradle, where life begins (life begins),
With the fallopian tubes, the journey spins (journey spins).

[Pre-Chorus]
With intricate design, and complex role (complex role),
The female genitalia, a marvel to behold (to behold).

[Chorus]
Oh, the anatomy, let’s celebrate (let’s celebrate),
The wonders of the body, intricate and great (intricate and great).
From the vulva to the ovaries, all parts play (all parts play),
A vital role in life, every single day (every single day).

[Bridge]
Ovaries hold the eggs, potential to grow (potential to grow),
Hormones like estrogen, making their flow (making their flow).
Endometrium lining, shedding away (shedding away),
In the menstrual cycle, every twentyeight day (twenty eight day).

[Verse 3]
The Bartholin's glands, they play their part (play their part),
Providing lubrication, a work of art (work of art).
During arousal, they come alive (they come alive),
Ensuring comfort, helping bodies thrive (bodies thrive).

[Pre-Chorus]
With intricate design, and complex role (complex role),
The female genitalia, a marvel to behold (to behold).

[Chorus]
Oh, the anatomy, let’s celebrate (let’s celebrate),
The wonders of the body, intricate and great (intricate and great).
From the vulva to the ovaries, all parts play (all parts play),
A vital role in life, every single day (every single day).

[Outro]
Respect and understanding, let's aim to show (aim to show),
The female genitalia, let our knowledge grow (knowledge grow).

songs.caption: This track is an educational celebration of the female genitalia and reproductive system. It covers the vulva (including the labia and clitoris), the internal organs like the vagina, uterus, and ovaries, and essential functions such as hormone production (like estrogen), menstruation, and Bartholin's glands for lubrication. It's a shout-out to the intricate complexity of the human body. #Anatomy #ReproductiveHealth #Biology #Estrogen

songs.title: Muscle Beat
songs.tags: country, acoustic, acoustic guitar, bass
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
Feel the beat in every move, the groove finds its way,

[Verse 1]
When the signal hits, muscles start to play,
MY-oh-sin slides, Actin paves the way.
Rhythmic contraction, smooth and tight,
Power in motion, groove in the night.

[Pre-Chorus]
Nerves send the message, electric and clear,
Muscles respond, bringing the cheer.

[Chorus]
We got the funk, we got the drive,
Every move we make, we come alive.
Our muscles groove, to a funky beat,
Strength and rhythm, can't be beat.

[Bridge]
Tendons pulling, fibers tight,
In the groove, we feel the might.
Force and tension, dance in sync,
Muscles working, feel the link.

[Outro]
Feel the beat in every move, the groove finds its way,
Together, forever, let's dance and sway.

Feathers bright, colors bold,
Stories of attraction, centuries old.

[Chorus] [Folk] [Catchy Hook]

To win a mate in morning's gloom.

songs.title: Decomposition's Lament
songs.tags:
Ambient Rock, Progressive, Atmospheric, Mixed Vocals,
SEE <SONG_DETAILS> IN LYRICS FOR MORE INFO
songs.prompt:
<SONG_DETAILS>
[GENRES: Ambient Rock, Progressive Rock]
[SOUNDS LIKE: Pink Floyd, Porcupine Tree, Anathema]
[STYLE: Atmospheric, Melodic, Experimental, Narrative]
[MOOD: Reflective, Contemplative, Mysterious, Somber]
[VOCALS: Mixed (Male and Female), Harmonious, Layered]
[ARRANGEMENT: Moderate tempo, Ethereal textures, Dynamic builds]
[INSTRUMENTATION: Electric guitar, Synthesizers, Bass, Drums, Piano, Ambient pads]
[TEMPO: Moderate, 90-110 BPM]
[PRODUCTION: Rich soundscapes, Warm tones, Layered textures, High fidelity, Produced by Steven Wilson]
[STRUCTURE: Intro, Verse, Chorus, Verse, Chorus, Bridge, Outro]
[DYNAMICS: Gentle and dynamic, Gradual builds and releases, Smooth transitions]
[EMOTIONS: Reflection, Curiosity, Mystery, Tranquility]
</SONG_DETAILS>

[Short Instrumental Intro]

[Verse 1 - Male]
Silent beginning, cells break down within
Autolysis starts, where life once had been
Enzymes released, breaking bonds so deep
Molecules unravel in this final sleep

[Chorus - Female]
Rigor mortis, stiffness claims the form
Muscles freeze in death’s quiet storm
Algor mortis, body cools its heat
Nature's clock ticks, in its rhythmic beat

[Instrumental Interlude]

[Verse 2 - Female]
Decomposition takes its toll, bacteria thrive
Breaking down the body's soul, in a dance, alive
Odor’s veil, gases rise, a spectral display
Insects come, drawn to the decay

[Chorus - Male]
Rigor mortis, stiffness claims the form
Muscles freeze in death’s quiet storm
Algor mortis, body cools its heat
Nature's clock ticks, in its rhythmic beat

[Bridge - Mixed Vocals]
Through decay, life reclaims, recycling the dead
Nutrients return, where once life had bled
Bones remain, a testament to time
Nature’s cycle, in rhythm and rhyme

[Outro]
(Ambient guitar and synth pads, fading out gently)

Odor’s veil, gases rise, a spectral display
Insects come, drawn to the decay

[Chorus - Male]
Rigor mortis, stiffness claims the form
Muscles freeze in death’s quiet storm
Algor mortis, body cools its heat
Nature's clock ticks, in its rhythmic beat

[Bridge - Mixed Vocals]
Through decay, life reclaims, recycling the dead
Nutrients return, where once life had bled
Bones remain, a testament to time
Nature’s cycle, in rhythm and rhyme

[Outro]
(Ambient guitar and synth pads, fading out gently)

songs.title: Genitalia Jamboree: A Co-Evolution Rhapsody
songs.tags:
Upbeat, funky tune with playful call-and-response vocals, rap battle
Male and female voices, duets
Funk-pop with a bassline mimicking sperm wiggles.
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1 – Ducks]
[Male voice]
I’m a mallard drake with a corkscrew spiral,
But duck ladies evolved a vaginal maze—it’s a trial!
Left-twist, dead ends, a labyrinthine plot,
Sexual conflict? Oh, you’d better duck and dodge a lot!

[Female voice]
“Your spiral’s a hassle, but my oviduct’s sly,
Blocking forced sperm—nice try, oh nice try!
Co-evolution’s tango, we’re stuck in this dance,
Your phallus, my maze—let’s call it romance!”

[Chorus]
Genitalia jamboree! (Oh-oh!)
It’s an evolutionary spree!
From ducks to bugs, it’s a wild ride,
Lock-and-key? Nah—it’s sexual side-eye worldwide!

[Verse 2 – Bed Bugs]
[Male voice]
I’m a bed bug dude with a traumatic spike,
Stab your exoskeleton—sperm delivery, take a hike!
Traumatic insemination? Yeah, it’s rough and rude,
But lady bed bugs evolved spermalege—a trauma-reducing mood!

[Female voice]
“You stab my back, but I’ve got a groove,
Redirect your chaos to where I approve!
Your weapon’s no match for my paragenital shield,
Co-evolve or perish—that’s the battlefield!”

[Chorus]
Genitalia jamboree! (Oh-oh!)
It’s an evolutionary spree!
From ducks to bugs, it’s a wild ride,
Lock-and-key? Nah—it’s sexual side-eye worldwide!

[Verse 3 – Squid & Octopuses]
[Male voice]
I’m a squid with a hectocotylus arm,
Snap it off in your mantle—sperm’s my charm!
Detachable appendage? Evolution’s flex,
But females evolved sperm storage—what’s next?

[Female voice]
“Keep your spare arm, buddy, I’ll pick and choose,
Cryptic female choice—I’ve got nothing to lose!
Store your packets, use ‘em when I please,
Co-evolution’s chess—checkmate, Mr. Squeeze!”

[Bridge – Humans]
[Both voices harmonize]
Humans play it subtle, but the race is there,
Penis shape vs. cervical crypts—a molecular dare!
Fast sperm? Slow sperm? The vagina’s pH game,
Even our parts evolved to screen, block, and claim!

“Baculum or no baculum?* That’s the bone to pick,
But co-evolution’s groove keeps our genes quick!”*

[Verse 4 – Damselflies]
[Male voice]
I’m a damselfly dude with a sperm-scooping spoon,
Scrape out rivals’ genes ‘fore I plant my own moon!

[Female voice]
“But my vaginal tracts are a spiraled trap,
Flick out your leftovers—nice try, chap!
Sperm competition’s messy, but I’ve got the last laugh,
Co-evolution’s punchline? I’m the better half!”

[Chorus]
Genitalia jamboree! (Oh-oh!)
It’s an evolutionary spree!
From ducks to bugs, it’s a wild ride,
Lock-and-key? Nah—it’s sexual side-eye worldwide!

[Outro – Evolutionary Truce]
[Both voices, upbeat]
So here’s to the arms race of lust and disdain,
Where genitalia morph in a feedback chain!
Conflict or co-operation? Why not both, my friend?
Without this twisted tango, life’s spark would end!

songs.title: The Sperm Saga: A Journey to the Egg
songs.tags: Upbeat, empowering melody, high fidelity
songs.prompt:
[Verse 1]
In the testis, where the magic begins,
Spermatogenesis is about to spin.
Mitosis, mei-osis, it’s quite a ride,
Making little swimmers with a lot of pride.

Haploid cells, they’re the name of the game,
With 23 chromosomes, they’re not the same.
Spermatids mature, they grow a tail,
Now they’re ready to set sail!

[Chorus]
Oh, it’s a sperm’s life, a wiggly quest,
Swimming through the body, doing their best.
From the epididymis to the great beyond,
Millions of brothers, but only one’s the chosen one!

[Verse 2]
Through the vas deferens, they take a tour,
Mixing with fluids, they’re ready for more.
Prostate and seminal vesicles join the mix,
Creating a cocktail that does the trick!

Out they go, on a mission so grand,
Through the cervix, they’ll make their stand.
Acrosomes armed, they’re ready to fight,
To penetrate the egg and ignite new life!

[Chorus]
Oh, it’s a sperm’s life, a wiggly quest,
Swimming through the body, doing their best.
From the epididymis to the great beyond,
Millions of brothers, but only one’s the chosen one!

[Bridge]
The egg’s in the fallopian tube, just waiting there,
With a zona pellucida, it’s quite the affair.
The fastest sperm breaks through the wall,
Fertilization happens, and that’s not all!

Zygote forms, the journey’s begun,
Cell division starts, and it’s so much fun.
Implantation in the uterus, a new life’s made,
And that’s how the sperm’s journey gets played!

[Chorus]
Oh, it’s a sperm’s life, a wiggly quest,
Swimming through the body, doing their best.
From the epididymis to the great beyond,
Millions of brothers, but only one’s the chosen one!

[Outro]
So here’s to the sperm, so small yet so brave,
Their journey’s the reason we all behave.
From meiosis to baby’s first cry,
It’s the circle of life, and that’s no lie!

songs.title: Beneath the corpse - Shore Sexton Beetle V5.5
songs.tags: dark folk, gothic americana, deep male vocals, acoustic guitar, cello, minor key, atmospheric, storytelling, 85 bpm
songs.prompt:
[Intro]
[Acoustic Guitar] [Soft]

[Verse 1]
[Deep Male Vocal]
Drawn to the scent that others avoid
I sense what's happened in the void
Where life has ceased, my work begins
Necrodes littoralis - decay's kin
[Building]
Black carapace gleaming in moonlight
Orange-tipped antennae sense what's right
Upon fallen giants I will crawl
Answering nature's solemn call

[Chorus]
[Powerful] [Cello]
Beneath the corpse, I make my home
Through flesh and bone, I roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, I bask

[Verse 2]
[Soft]
My antennae quiver with delight
Sensing chemicals in the night
The perfect corpse, too large to bury
Unlike my cousins, I'm not wary
[Building]
I carry mites upon my shell
Travelers with stories to tell
They fight the flies that would compete
Our alliance makes our work complete

[Chorus]
[Powerful] [Cello]
Beneath the corpse, I make my home
Through flesh and bone, I roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, I bask

[Verse 3]
[Soft]
Eggs I lay in this fertile ground
Where nutrients and shelter abound
My larvae feast on what remains
Nature's custodians, we take the reins
[Building]
We're not monsters though you might recoil
Just recyclers of this mortal coil
Breaking down what once had breath
Transforming ending into life's next step

[Bridge]
[Whispered]
The shore sexton's story seldom told
Our purpose ancient, necessary, bold
[Building]
What seems macabre serves a greater good
Nature's balance perfectly understood
[Powerful]
In twilight hours when shadows fall
I emerge ready for nature's call
Too large to bury, the carcass waits
My work begins as day abates

[Chorus]
[Powerful] [Cello]
Beneath the corpse, I make my home
Through flesh and bone, I roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, I bask

[Verse 4]
[Soft]
The giants fall and we arrive
With tiny allies that help us thrive
A partnership of give and take
A perfect system, no mistake
[Building]
In the dance between life and demise
We are the link that harmonize
The carrion's end is just the start
Of nature's most beautiful art

[Final Chorus]
[Powerful] [Full Instrumentation]
Beneath the corpse, we make our home
Through flesh and bone, we roam
The shore sexton's sacred task
In death's shadow, we'll always bask

[Outro]
[Fading] [Acoustic Guitar] [Soft]
